Genesis1:1 In the beginning when God created the heavens and the earth
Genesis1:2 the earth was a formless void and darkness covered the face of the deep
Genesis1:3 Then God said
Genesis1:4 And God saw that the light was good; and God separated the light from the darkness.
Genesis1:5 God called the light Day
Genesis1:6 And God said
Genesis1:7 So God made the dome and separated the waters that were under the dome from the waters that were above the dome. And it was so.
Genesis1:8 God called the dome Sky. And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:9 And God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry land Earth
Genesis1:11 Then God said
Genesis1:12 The earth brought forth vegetation: plants yielding seed of every kind
Genesis1:13 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:14 And God said
Genesis1:15 and let them be lights in the dome of the sky to give light upon the earth. And it was so.
Genesis1:16 God made the two great lights-- the greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night-- and the stars.
Genesis1:17 God set them in the dome of the sky to give light upon the earth
Genesis1:18 to rule over the day and over the night
Genesis1:19 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:20 And God said
Genesis1:21 So God created the great sea monsters and every living creature that moves
Genesis1:22 God blessed them
Genesis1:23 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:24 And God said
Genesis1:25 God made the wild animals of the earth of every kind
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 So God created humankind in his image
Genesis1:28 God blessed them
Genesis1:29 God said
Genesis1:30 And to every beast of the earth
Genesis1:31 God saw everything that he had made
Genesis2:1 Thus the heavens and the earth were finished
Genesis2:2 And on the seventh day God finished the work that he had done
Genesis2:3 So God blessed the seventh day and hallowed it
Genesis2:4 These are the generations of the heavens and the earth when they were created. In the day that the LORD God made the earth and the heavens
Genesis2:5 when no plant of the field was yet in the earth and no herb of the field had yet sprung up-- for the LORD God had not caused it to rain upon the earth
Genesis2:6 but a stream would rise from the earth
Genesis2:7 then the LORD God formed man from the dust of the ground
Genesis2:8 And the LORD God planted a garden in Eden
Genesis2:9 Out of the ground the LORD God made to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food
Genesis2:10 A river flows out of Eden to water the garden
Genesis2:11 The name of the first is Pishon; it is the one that flows around the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 and the gold of that land is good; bdellium and onyx stone are there.
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is Gihon; it is the one that flows around the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The name of the third river is Tigris
Genesis2:15 The LORD God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to till it and keep it.
Genesis2:16 And the LORD God commanded the man
Genesis2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat
Genesis2:18 Then the LORD God said
Genesis2:19 So out of the ground the LORD God formed every animal of the field and every bird of the air
Genesis2:20 The man gave names to all cattle
Genesis2:21 So the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man
Genesis2:22 And the rib that the LORD God had taken from the man he made into a woman and brought her to the man.
Genesis2:23 Then the man said
Genesis2:24 Therefore a man leaves his father and his mother and clings to his wife
Genesis2:25 And the man and his wife were both naked

Genesis3:2 The woman said to the serpent
Genesis3:3 but God said
Genesis3:4 But the serpent said to the woman
Genesis3:5 for God knows that when you eat of it your eyes will be opened

Genesis3:7 Then the eyes of both were opened
Genesis3:8 They heard the sound of the LORD God walking in the garden at the time of the evening breeze
Genesis3:9 But the LORD God called to the man
Genesis3:10 He said
Genesis3:11 He said
Genesis3:12 The man said
Genesis3:13 Then the LORD God said to the woman

Genesis4:1 Now the man knew his wife Eve
Genesis4:2 Next she bore his brother Abel. Now Abel was a keeper of sheep
Genesis4:3 In the course of time Cain brought to the LORD an offering of the fruit of the ground
Genesis4:4 and Abel for his part brought of the firstlings of his flock
Genesis4:5 but for Cain and his offering he had no regard. So Cain was very angry
Genesis4:6 The LORD said to Cain
Genesis4:7 If you do well
Genesis4:8 Cain said to his brother Abel
Genesis4:9 Then the LORD said to Cain

Genesis5:1 This is the list of the descendants of Adam. When God created humankind
Genesis5:2 Male and female he created them
Genesis5:3 When Adam had lived one hundred thirty years
Genesis5:4 The days of Adam after he became the father of Seth were eight hundred years; and he had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:5 Thus all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred thirty years; and he died.
Genesis5:6 When Seth had lived one hundred five years
Genesis5:7 Seth lived after the birth of Enosh eight hundred seven years
Genesis5:8 Thus all the days of Seth were nine hundred twelve years; and he died.
Genesis5:9 When Enosh had lived ninety years
Genesis5:10 Enosh lived after the birth of Kenan eight hundred fifteen years
Genesis5:11 Thus all the days of Enosh were nine hundred five years; and he died.
Genesis5:12 When Kenan had lived seventy years
Genesis5:13 Kenan lived after the birth of Mahalalel eight hundred and forty years
Genesis5:14 Thus all the days of Kenan were nine hundred and ten years; and he died.
Genesis5:15 When Mahalalel had lived sixty-five years
Genesis5:16 Mahalalel lived after the birth of Jared eight hundred thirty years
Genesis5:17 Thus all the days of Mahalalel were eight hundred ninety-five years; and he died.
Genesis5:18 When Jared had lived one hundred sixty-two years he became the father of Enoch.
Genesis5:19 Jared lived after the birth of Enoch eight hundred years
Genesis5:20 Thus all the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty-two years; and he died.
Genesis5:21 When Enoch had lived sixty-five years
Genesis5:22 Enoch walked with God after the birth of Methuselah three hundred years
Genesis5:23 Thus all the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty-five years.
Genesis5:24 Enoch walked with God; then he was no more
Genesis5:25 When Methuselah had lived one hundred eighty-seven years
Genesis5:26 Methuselah lived after the birth of Lamech seven hundred eighty-two years
Genesis5:27 Thus all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred sixty-nine years; and he died.
Genesis5:28 When Lamech had lived one hundred eighty-two years
Genesis5:29 he named him Noah
Genesis5:30 Lamech lived after the birth of Noah five hundred ninety-five years
Genesis5:31 Thus all the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy-seven years; and he died.
Genesis5:32 After Noah was five hundred years old

Genesis6:2 the sons of God saw that they were fair; and they took wives for themselves of all that they chose.
Genesis6:3 Then the LORD said
Genesis6:4 The Nephilim were on the earth in those days-- and also afterward-- when the sons of God went in to the daughters of humans
Genesis6:5 The LORD saw that the wickedness of humankind was great in the earth
Genesis6:6 And the LORD was sorry that he had made humankind on the earth
Genesis6:7 So the LORD said
Genesis6:8 But Noah found favor in the sight of the LORD.
Genesis6:9 These are the descendants of Noah. Noah was a righteous man
Genesis6:10 And Noah had three sons
Genesis6:11 Now the earth was corrupt in God's sight
Genesis6:12 And God saw that the earth was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted its ways upon the earth.

Genesis6:18 But I will establish my covenant with you; and you shall come into the ark
Genesis6:19 And of every living thing
Genesis6:20 Of the birds according to their kinds
Genesis6:21 Also take with you every kind of food that is eaten
Genesis6:22 Noah did this; he did all that God commanded him.

Genesis7:2 Take with you seven pairs of all clean animals
Genesis7:3 and seven pairs of the birds of the air also

Genesis7:5 And Noah did all that the LORD had commanded him.
Genesis7:6 Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters came on the earth.

Genesis7:8 Of clean animals
Genesis7:9 two and two

Genesis7:13 On the very same day Noah with his sons
Genesis7:14 they and every wild animal of every kind

Genesis7:16 And those that entered
Genesis7:17 The flood continued forty days on the earth; and the waters increased
Genesis7:18 The waters swelled and increased greatly on the earth; and the ark floated on the face of the waters.
Genesis7:19 The waters swelled so mightily on the earth that all the high mountains under the whole heaven were covered;
Genesis7:20 the waters swelled above the mountains
Genesis7:21 And all flesh died that moved on the earth
Genesis7:22 everything on dry land in whose nostrils was the breath of life died.
Genesis7:23 He blotted out every living thing that was on the face of the ground
Genesis7:24 And the waters swelled on the earth for one hundred fifty days.

Genesis8:2 the fountains of the deep and the windows of the heavens were closed
Genesis8:3 and the waters gradually receded from the earth. At the end of one hundred fifty days the waters had abated;
Genesis8:4 and in the seventh month
Genesis8:5 The waters continued to abate until the tenth month; in the tenth month
Genesis8:6 At the end of forty days Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made
Genesis8:7 and sent out the raven; and it went to and fro until the waters were dried up from the earth.

Genesis8:9 but the dove found no place to set its foot
Genesis8:10 He waited another seven days
Genesis8:11 and the dove came back to him in the evening
Genesis8:12 Then he waited another seven days
Genesis8:13 In the six hundred first year
Genesis8:14 In the second month

Exodus12:1 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt:
Exodus12:2 This month shall mark for you the beginning of months; it shall be the first month of the year for you.
Exodus12:3 Tell the whole congregation of Israel that on the tenth of this month they are to take a lamb for each family
Exodus12:4 If a household is too small for a whole lamb
Exodus12:5 Your lamb shall be without blemish
Exodus12:6 You shall keep it until the fourteenth day of this month; then the whole assembled congregation of Israel shall slaughter it at twilight.
Exodus12:7 They shall take some of the blood and put it on the two doorposts and the lintel of the houses in which they eat it.
Exodus12:8 They shall eat the lamb that same night; they shall eat it roasted over the fire with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.
Exodus12:9 Do not eat any of it raw or boiled in water
Exodus12:10 You shall let none of it remain until the morning; anything that remains until the morning you shall burn.
Exodus12:11 This is how you shall eat it: your loins girded
Exodus12:12 For I will pass through the land of Egypt that night
Exodus12:13 The blood shall be a sign for you on the houses where you live: when I see the blood
Exodus12:14 This day shall be a day of remembrance for you. You shall celebrate it as a festival to the LORD; throughout your generations you shall observe it as a perpetual ordinance.
Exodus12:15 Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread; on the first day you shall remove leaven from your houses
Exodus12:16 On the first day you shall hold a solemn assembly
Exodus12:17 You shall observe the festival of unleavened bread
Exodus12:18 In the first month
Exodus12:19 For seven days no leaven shall be found in your houses; for whoever eats what is leavened shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel
Exodus12:20 You shall eat nothing leavened; in all your settlements you shall eat unleavened bread.
Exodus12:21 Then Moses called all the elders of Israel and said to them
Exodus12:22 Take a bunch of hyssop
Exodus12:23 For the LORD will pass through to strike down the Egyptians; when he sees the blood on the lintel and on the two doorposts
Exodus12:24 You shall observe this rite as a perpetual ordinance for you and your children.
Exodus12:25 When you come to the land that the LORD will give you
Exodus12:26 And when your children ask you
Exodus12:27 you shall say
Exodus12:28 The Israelites went and did just as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron.

Exodus12:30 Pharaoh arose in the night
Exodus12:31 Then he summoned Moses and Aaron in the night
Exodus12:32 Take your flocks and your herds
Exodus12:33 The Egyptians urged the people to hasten their departure from the land
Exodus12:34 So the people took their dough before it was leavened
Exodus12:35 The Israelites had done as Moses told them; they had asked the Egyptians for jewelry of silver and gold
Exodus12:36 and the LORD had given the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians
Exodus12:37 The Israelites journeyed from Rameses to Succoth
Exodus12:38 A mixed crowd also went up with them
Exodus12:39 They baked unleavened cakes of the dough that they had brought out of Egypt; it was not leavened
Exodus12:40 The time that the Israelites had lived in Egypt was four hundred thirty years.

Exodus12:42 That was for the LORD a night of vigil

Exodus12:45 no bound or hired servant may eat of it.
Exodus12:46 It shall be eaten in one house; you shall not take any of the animal outside the house
Exodus12:47 The whole congregation of Israel shall celebrate it.
Exodus12:48 If an alien who resides with you wants to celebrate the passover to the LORD
Exodus12:49 there shall be one law for the native and for the alien who resides among you.

Exodus20:1 Then God spoke all these words:
Exodus20:2 I am the LORD your God
Exodus20:3 you shall have no other gods before me.
Exodus20:4 You shall not make for yourself an idol
Exodus20:5 You shall not bow down to them or worship them; for I the LORD your God am a jealous God
Exodus20:6 but showing steadfast love to the thousandth generation of those who love me and keep my commandments.
Exodus20:7 You shall not make wrongful use of the name of the LORD your God
Exodus20:8 Remember the sabbath day
Exodus20:9 Six days you shall labor and do all your work.
Exodus20:10 But the seventh day is a sabbath to the LORD your God; you shall not do any work-- you
Exodus20:11 For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth
Exodus20:12 Honor your father and your mother
Exodus20:13 You shall not murder.
Exodus20:14 You shall not commit adultery.
Exodus20:15 You shall not steal.
Exodus20:16 You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
Exodus20:17 You shall not covet your neighbor's house; you shall not covet your neighbor's wife

Exodus20:19 and said to Moses
Exodus20:20 Moses said to the people
Exodus20:21 Then the people stood at a distance

Exodus20:23 You shall not make gods of silver alongside me

Exodus20:25 But if you make for me an altar of stone
Exodus20:26 You shall not go up by steps to my altar
Exodus21:1 These are the ordinances that you shall set before them:
Exodus21:2 When you buy a male Hebrew slave
Exodus21:3 If he comes in single
Exodus21:4 If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters
Exodus21:5 But if the slave declares
Exodus21:6 then his master shall bring him before God. He shall be brought to the door or the doorpost; and his master shall pierce his ear with an awl; and he shall serve him for life.
Exodus21:7 When a man sells his daughter as a slave
Exodus21:8 If she does not please her master
Exodus21:9 If he designates her for his son
Exodus21:10 If he takes another wife to himself
Exodus21:11 And if he does not do these three things for her
Exodus21:12 Whoever strikes a person mortally shall be put to death.
Exodus21:13 If it was not premeditated
Exodus21:14 But if someone willfully attacks and kills another by treachery
Exodus21:15 Whoever strikes father or mother shall be put to death.
Exodus21:16 Whoever kidnaps a person
Exodus21:17 Whoever curses father or mother shall be put to death.
Exodus21:18 When individuals quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone or fist so that the injured party
Exodus21:19 but recovers and walks around outside with the help of a staff
Exodus21:20 When a slaveowner strikes a male or female slave with a rod and the slave dies immediately
Exodus21:21 But if the slave survives a day or two
Exodus21:22 When people who are fighting injure a pregnant woman so that there is a miscarriage
Exodus21:23 If any harm follows
Exodus21:24 eye for eye
Exodus21:25 burn for burn
Exodus21:26 When a slaveowner strikes the eye of a male or female slave
Exodus21:27 If the owner knocks out a tooth of a male or female slave
Exodus21:28 When an ox gores a man or a woman to death
Exodus21:29 If the ox has been accustomed to gore in the past
Exodus21:30 If a ransom is imposed on the owner
Exodus21:31 If it gores a boy or a girl
Exodus21:32 If the ox gores a male or female slave
Exodus21:33 If someone leaves a pit open
Exodus21:34 the owner of the pit shall make restitution
Exodus21:35 If someone's ox hurts the ox of another
Exodus21:36 But if it was known that the ox was accustomed to gore in the past
Exodus22:1 When someone steals an ox or a sheep
Exodus22:2 If a thief is found breaking in
Exodus22:3 but if it happens after sunrise
Exodus22:4 When the animal
Exodus22:5 When someone causes a field or vineyard to be grazed over
Exodus22:6 When fire breaks out and catches in thorns so that the stacked grain or the standing grain or the field is consumed
Exodus22:7 When someone delivers to a neighbor money or goods for safekeeping
Exodus22:8 If the thief is not caught
Exodus22:9 In any case of disputed ownership involving ox
Exodus22:10 When someone delivers to another a donkey

Exodus22:12 But if it was stolen
Exodus22:13 If it was mangled by beasts
Exodus22:14 When someone borrows an animal from another and it is injured or dies
Exodus22:15 If the owner was present
Exodus22:16 When a man seduces a virgin who is not engaged to be married
Exodus22:17 But if her father refuses to give her to him
Exodus22:18 You shall not permit a female sorcerer to live.
Exodus22:19 Whoever lies with an animal shall be put to death.
Exodus22:20 Whoever sacrifices to any god
Exodus22:21 You shall not wrong or oppress a resident alien
Exodus22:22 You shall not abuse any widow or orphan.
Exodus22:23 If you do abuse them
Exodus22:24 my wrath will burn
Exodus22:25 If you lend money to my people
Exodus22:26 If you take your neighbor's cloak in pawn
Exodus22:27 for it may be your neighbor's only clothing to use as cover; in what else shall that person sleep? And if your neighbor cries out to me
Exodus22:28 You shall not revile God
Exodus22:29 You shall not delay to make offerings from the fullness of your harvest and from the outflow of your presses. The firstborn of your sons you shall give to me.
Exodus22:30 You shall do the same with your oxen and with your sheep: seven days it shall remain with its mother; on the eighth day you shall give it to me.
Exodus22:31 You shall be people consecrated to me; therefore you shall not eat any meat that is mangled by beasts in the field; you shall throw it to the dogs.
Exodus23:1 You shall not spread a false report. You shall not join hands with the wicked to act as a malicious witness.
Exodus23:2 You shall not follow a majority in wrongdoing; when you bear witness in a lawsuit
Exodus23:3 nor shall you be partial to the poor in a lawsuit.
Exodus23:4 When you come upon your enemy's ox or donkey going astray
Exodus23:5 When you see the donkey of one who hates you lying under its burden and you would hold back from setting it free
Exodus23:6 You shall not pervert the justice due to your poor in their lawsuits.
Exodus23:7 Keep far from a false charge
Exodus23:8 You shall take no bribe
Exodus23:9 You shall not oppress a resident alien; you know the heart of an alien
Exodus23:10 For six years you shall sow your land and gather in its yield;
Exodus23:11 but the seventh year you shall let it rest and lie fallow
Exodus23:12 Six days you shall do your work
Exodus23:13 Be attentive to all that I have said to you. Do not invoke the names of other gods; do not let them be heard on your lips.
Exodus23:14 Three times in the year you shall hold a festival for me.
Exodus23:15 You shall observe the festival of unleavened bread; as I commanded you
Exodus23:16 You shall observe the festival of harvest
Exodus23:17 Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the Lord GOD.
Exodus23:18 You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with anything leavened
Exodus23:19 The choicest of the first fruits of your ground you shall bring into the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk.
Exodus23:20 I am going to send an angel in front of you
Exodus23:21 Be attentive to him and listen to his voice; do not rebel against him
Exodus23:22 But if you listen attentively to his voice and do all that I say
Exodus23:23 When my angel goes in front of you
Exodus23:24 you shall not bow down to their gods
Exodus23:25 You shall worship the LORD your God
Exodus23:26 No one shall miscarry or be barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.
Exodus23:27 I will send my terror in front of you
Exodus23:28 And I will send the pestilence in front of you
Exodus23:29 I will not drive them out from before you in one year
Exodus23:30 Little by little I will drive them out from before you
Exodus23:31 I will set your borders from the Red Sea to the sea of the Philistines
Exodus23:32 You shall make no covenant with them and their gods.
Exodus23:33 They shall not live in your land

Exodus27:12 For the width of the court on the west side there shall be fifty cubits of hangings

Exodus27:15 There shall be fifteen cubits of hangings on the other side
Exodus27:16 For the gate of the court there shall be a screen twenty cubits long
Exodus27:17 All the pillars around the court shall be banded with silver; their hooks shall be of silver
Exodus27:18 The length of the court shall be one hundred cubits
Exodus27:19 All the utensils of the tabernacle for every use
Exodus27:20 You shall further command the Israelites to bring you pure oil of beaten olives for the light
Exodus27:21 In the tent of meeting
Exodus28:1 Then bring near to you your brother Aaron
Exodus28:2 You shall make sacred vestments for the glorious adornment of your brother Aaron.
Exodus28:3 And you shall speak to all who have ability
Exodus28:4 These are the vestments that they shall make: a breastpiece
Exodus28:5 they shall use gold
Exodus28:6 They shall make the ephod of gold
Exodus28:7 It shall have two shoulder-pieces attached to its two edges
Exodus28:8 The decorated band on it shall be of the same workmanship and materials
Exodus28:9 You shall take two onyx stones
Exodus28:10 six of their names on the one stone
Exodus28:11 As a gem-cutter engraves signets
Exodus28:12 You shall set the two stones on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod
Exodus28:13 You shall make settings of gold filigree
Exodus28:14 and two chains of pure gold
Exodus28:15 You shall make a breastpiece of judgment
Exodus28:16 It shall be square and doubled
Exodus28:17 You shall set in it four rows of stones. A row of carnelian
Exodus28:18 and the second row a turquoise
Exodus28:19 and the third row a jacinth
Exodus28:20 and the fourth row a beryl
Exodus28:21 There shall be twelve stones with names corresponding to the names of the sons of Israel; they shall be like signets
Exodus28:22 You shall make for the breastpiece chains of pure gold
Exodus28:23 and you shall make for the breastpiece two rings of gold
Exodus28:24 You shall put the two cords of gold in the two rings at the edges of the breastpiece;
Exodus28:25 the two ends of the two cords you shall attach to the two settings
Exodus28:26 You shall make two rings of gold
Exodus28:27 You shall make two rings of gold
Exodus28:28 The breastpiece shall be bound by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord
Exodus28:29 So Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment on his heart when he goes into the holy place
Exodus28:30 In the breastpiece of judgment you shall put the Urim and the Thummim
Exodus28:31 You shall make the robe of the ephod all of blue.
Exodus28:32 It shall have an opening for the head in the middle of it
Exodus28:33 On its lower hem you shall make pomegranates of blue
Exodus28:34 a golden bell and a pomegranate alternating all around the lower hem of the robe.
Exodus28:35 Aaron shall wear it when he ministers
Exodus28:36 You shall make a rosette of pure gold
Exodus28:37 You shall fasten it on the turban with a blue cord; it shall be on the front of the turban.
Exodus28:38 It shall be on Aaron's forehead
Exodus28:39 You shall make the checkered tunic of fine linen
Exodus28:40 For Aaron's sons you shall make tunics and sashes and headdresses; you shall make them for their glorious adornment.
Exodus28:41 You shall put them on your brother Aaron
Exodus28:42 You shall make for them linen undergarments to cover their naked flesh; they shall reach from the hips to the thighs;
Exodus28:43 Aaron and his sons shall wear them when they go into the tent of meeting
Exodus29:1 Now this is what you shall do to them to consecrate them
Exodus29:2 and unleavened bread
Exodus29:3 You shall put them in one basket and bring them in the basket
Exodus29:4 You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting
Exodus29:5 Then you shall take the vestments
Exodus29:6 and you shall set the turban on his head
Exodus29:7 You shall take the anointing oil
Exodus29:8 Then you shall bring his sons
Exodus29:9 and you shall gird them with sashes and tie headdresses on them; and the priesthood shall be theirs by a perpetual ordinance. You shall then ordain Aaron and his sons.
Exodus29:10 You shall bring the bull in front of the tent of meeting. Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the bull
Exodus29:11 and you shall slaughter the bull before the LORD
Exodus29:12 and shall take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger
Exodus29:13 You shall take all the fat that covers the entrails
Exodus29:14 But the flesh of the bull
Exodus29:15 Then you shall take one of the rams
Exodus29:16 and you shall slaughter the ram
Exodus29:17 Then you shall cut the ram into its parts
Exodus29:18 and turn the whole ram into smoke on the altar; it is a burnt offering to the LORD; it is a pleasing odor
Exodus29:19 You shall take the other ram; and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram
Exodus29:20 and you shall slaughter the ram
Exodus29:21 Then you shall take some of the blood that is on the altar
Exodus29:22 You shall also take the fat of the ram
Exodus29:23 and one loaf of bread
Exodus29:24 and you shall place all these on the palms of Aaron and on the palms of his sons
Exodus29:25 Then you shall take them from their hands
Exodus29:26 You shall take the breast of the ram of Aaron's ordination and raise it as an elevation offering before the LORD; and it shall be your portion.
Exodus29:27 You shall consecrate the breast that was raised as an elevation offering and the thigh that was raised as an elevation offering from the ram of ordination
Exodus29:28 These things shall be a perpetual ordinance for Aaron and his sons from the Israelites
Exodus29:29 The sacred vestments of Aaron shall be passed on to his sons after him; they shall be anointed in them and ordained in them.
Exodus29:30 The son who is priest in his place shall wear them seven days
Exodus29:31 You shall take the ram of ordination
Exodus29:32 and Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket
Exodus29:33 They themselves shall eat the food by which atonement is made
Exodus29:34 If any of the flesh for the ordination
Exodus29:35 Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons
Exodus29:36 Also every day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement. Also you shall offer a sin offering for the altar
Exodus29:37 Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar
Exodus29:38 Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old regularly each day.
Exodus29:39 One lamb you shall offer in the morning
Exodus29:40 and with the first lamb one-tenth of a measure of choice flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of beaten oil
Exodus29:41 And the other lamb you shall offer in the evening
Exodus29:42 It shall be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the LORD
Exodus29:43 I will meet with the Israelites there
Exodus29:44 I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar; Aaron also and his sons I will consecrate
Exodus29:45 I will dwell among the Israelites
Exodus29:46 And they shall know that I am the LORD their God
Exodus30:1 You shall make an altar on which to offer incense; you shall make it of acacia wood.
Exodus30:2 It shall be one cubit long
Exodus30:3 You shall overlay it with pure gold
Exodus30:4 And you shall make two golden rings for it; under its molding on two opposite sides of it you shall make them
Exodus30:5 You shall make the poles of acacia wood
Exodus30:6 You shall place it in front of the curtain that is above the ark of the covenant
Exodus30:7 Aaron shall offer fragrant incense on it; every morning when he dresses the lamps he shall offer it
Exodus30:8 and when Aaron sets up the lamps in the evening
Exodus30:9 You shall not offer unholy incense on it
Exodus30:10 Once a year Aaron shall perform the rite of atonement on its horns. Throughout your generations he shall perform the atonement for it once a year with the blood of the atoning sin offering. It is most holy to the LORD.
Exodus30:11 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus30:12 When you take a census of the Israelites to register them
Exodus30:13 This is what each one who is registered shall give: half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (the shekel is twenty gerahs)
Exodus30:14 Each one who is registered
Exodus30:15 The rich shall not give more
Exodus30:16 You shall take the atonement money from the Israelites and shall designate it for the service of the tent of meeting; before the LORD it will be a reminder to the Israelites of the ransom given for your lives.
Exodus30:17 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus30:18 You shall make a bronze basin with a bronze stand for washing. You shall put it between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus30:19 with the water Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet.
Exodus30:20 When they go into the tent of meeting
Exodus30:21 They shall wash their hands and their feet
Exodus30:22 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus30:23 Take the finest spices: of liquid myrrh five hundred shekels
Exodus30:24 and five hundred of cassia-- measured by the sanctuary shekel-- and a hin of olive oil;
Exodus30:25 and you shall make of these a sacred anointing oil blended as by the perfumer; it shall be a holy anointing oil.
Exodus30:26 With it you shall anoint the tent of meeting and the ark of the covenant
Exodus30:27 and the table and all its utensils
Exodus30:28 and the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils
Exodus30:29 you shall consecrate them
Exodus30:30 You shall anoint Aaron and his sons
Exodus30:31 You shall say to the Israelites
Exodus30:32 It shall not be used in any ordinary anointing of the body
Exodus30:33 Whoever compounds any like it or whoever puts any of it on an unqualified person shall be cut off from the people.
Exodus30:34 The LORD said to Moses: Take sweet spices
Exodus30:35 and make an incense blended as by the perfumer
Exodus30:36 and you shall beat some of it into powder
Exodus30:37 When you make incense according to this composition
Exodus30:38 Whoever makes any like it to use as perfume shall be cut off from the people.

Exodus34:1 The LORD said to Moses

Exodus34:25 You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leaven
Exodus34:26 The best of the first fruits of your ground you shall bring to the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk.
Exodus34:27 The LORD said to Moses: Write these words; in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.
Exodus34:28 He was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he neither ate bread nor drank water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant

Exodus36:3 and they received from Moses all the freewill offerings that the Israelites had brought for doing the work on the sanctuary. They still kept bringing him freewill offerings every morning
Exodus36:4 so that all the artisans who were doing every sort of task on the sanctuary came
Exodus36:5 and said to Moses
Exodus36:6 So Moses gave command
Exodus36:7 for what they had already brought was more than enough to do all the work.
Exodus36:8 All those with skill among the workers made the tabernacle with ten curtains; they were made of fine twisted linen
Exodus36:9 The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits
Exodus36:10 He joined five curtains to one another
Exodus36:11 He made loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain of the first set; likewise he made them on the edge of the outermost curtain of the second set;
Exodus36:12 he made fifty loops on the one curtain
Exodus36:13 And he made fifty clasps of gold
Exodus36:14 He also made curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle; he made eleven curtains.

Exodus36:16 He joined five curtains by themselves
Exodus36:17 He made fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain of the one set
Exodus36:18 He made fifty clasps of bronze to join the tent together so that it might be one whole.
Exodus36:19 And he made for the tent a covering of tanned rams' skins and an outer covering of fine leather.

Exodus36:22 Each frame had two pegs for fitting together; he did this for all the frames of the tabernacle.

Exodus40:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus40:2 On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:3 You shall put in it the ark of the covenant
Exodus40:4 You shall bring in the table
Exodus40:5 You shall put the golden altar for incense before the ark of the covenant
Exodus40:6 You shall set the altar of burnt offering before the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:7 and place the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:8 You shall set up the court all around
Exodus40:9 Then you shall take the anointing oil
Exodus40:10 You shall also anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils
Exodus40:11 You shall also anoint the basin with its stand
Exodus40:12 Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:13 and put on Aaron the sacred vestments
Exodus40:14 You shall bring his sons also and put tunics on them
Exodus40:15 and anoint them
Exodus40:16 Moses did everything just as the LORD had commanded him.
Exodus40:17 In the first month in the second year
Exodus40:18 Moses set up the tabernacle; he laid its bases
Exodus40:19 and he spread the tent over the tabernacle
Exodus40:20 He took the covenant and put it into the ark
Exodus40:21 and he brought the ark into the tabernacle
Exodus40:22 He put the table in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:23 and set the bread in order on it before the LORD; as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Exodus40:24 He put the lampstand in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:25 and set up the lamps before the LORD; as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Exodus40:26 He put the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the curtain
Exodus40:27 and offered fragrant incense on it; as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Exodus40:28 He also put in place the screen for the entrance of the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 He set the altar of burnt offering at the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:30 He set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:31 with which Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet.
Exodus40:32 When they went into the tent of meeting
Exodus40:33 He set up the court around the tabernacle and the altar
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:35 Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud settled upon it
Exodus40:36 Whenever the cloud was taken up from the tabernacle
Exodus40:37 but if the cloud was not taken up
Exodus40:38 For the cloud of the LORD was on the tabernacle by day
Leviticus1:1 The LORD summoned Moses and spoke to him from the tent of meeting
Leviticus1:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When any of you bring an offering of livestock to the LORD
Leviticus1:3 If the offering is a burnt offering from the herd
Leviticus1:4 You shall lay your hand on the head of the burnt offering
Leviticus1:5 The bull shall be slaughtered before the LORD; and Aaron's sons the priests shall offer the blood
Leviticus1:6 The burnt offering shall be flayed and cut up into its parts.
Leviticus1:7 The sons of the priest Aaron shall put fire on the altar and arrange wood on the fire.
Leviticus1:8 Aaron's sons the priests shall arrange the parts
Leviticus1:9 but its entrails and its legs shall be washed with water. Then the priest shall turn the whole into smoke on the altar as a burnt offering
Leviticus1:10 If your gift for a burnt offering is from the flock
Leviticus1:11 It shall be slaughtered on the north side of the altar before the LORD
Leviticus1:12 It shall be cut up into its parts
Leviticus1:13 but the entrails and the legs shall be washed with water. Then the priest shall offer the whole and turn it into smoke on the altar; it is a burnt offering
Leviticus1:14 If your offering to the LORD is a burnt offering of birds
Leviticus1:15 The priest shall bring it to the altar and wring off its head
Leviticus1:16 He shall remove its crop with its contents and throw it at the east side of the altar
Leviticus1:17 He shall tear it open by its wings without severing it. Then the priest shall turn it into smoke on the altar
Leviticus2:1 When anyone presents a grain offering to the LORD
Leviticus2:2 and bring it to Aaron's sons the priests. After taking from it a handful of the choice flour and oil
Leviticus2:3 And what is left of the grain offering shall be for Aaron and his sons
Leviticus2:4 When you present a grain offering baked in the oven
Leviticus2:5 If your offering is grain prepared on a griddle
Leviticus2:6 break it in pieces
Leviticus2:7 If your offering is grain prepared in a pan
Leviticus2:8 You shall bring to the LORD the grain offering that is prepared in any of these ways; and when it is presented to the priest
Leviticus2:9 The priest shall remove from the grain offering its token portion and turn this into smoke on the altar
Leviticus2:10 And what is left of the grain offering shall be for Aaron and his sons; it is a most holy part of the offerings by fire to the LORD.
Leviticus2:11 No grain offering that you bring to the LORD shall be made with leaven
Leviticus2:12 You may bring them to the LORD as an offering of choice products
Leviticus2:13 You shall not omit from your grain offerings the salt of the covenant with your God; with all your offerings you shall offer salt.
Leviticus2:14 If you bring a grain offering of first fruits to the LORD
Leviticus2:15 You shall add oil to it and lay frankincense on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:16 And the priest shall turn a token portion of it into smoke-- some of the coarse grain and oil with all its frankincense; it is an offering by fire to the LORD.
Leviticus3:1 If the offering is a sacrifice of well-being
Leviticus3:2 You shall lay your hand on the head of the offering and slaughter it at the entrance of the tent of meeting; and Aaron's sons the priests shall dash the blood against all sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:3 You shall offer from the sacrifice of well-being
Leviticus3:4 the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus3:5 Then Aaron's sons shall turn these into smoke on the altar
Leviticus3:6 If your offering for a sacrifice of well-being to the LORD is from the flock
Leviticus3:7 If you present a sheep as your offering
Leviticus3:8 and lay your hand on the head of the offering. It shall be slaughtered before the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:9 You shall present its fat from the sacrifice of well-being
Leviticus3:10 the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus3:11 Then the priest shall turn these into smoke on the altar as a food offering by fire to the LORD.
Leviticus3:12 If your offering is a goat
Leviticus3:13 and lay your hand on its head; it shall be slaughtered before the tent of meeting; and the sons of Aaron shall dash its blood against all sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:14 You shall present as your offering from it
Leviticus3:15 the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus3:16 Then the priest shall turn these into smoke on the altar as a food offering by fire for a pleasing odor. All fat is the LORD's.
Leviticus3:17 It shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations
Leviticus4:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus4:2 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus4:3 If it is the anointed priest who sins
Leviticus4:4 He shall bring the bull to the entrance of the tent of meeting before the LORD and lay his hand on the head of the bull; the bull shall be slaughtered before the LORD.
Leviticus4:5 The anointed priest shall take some of the blood of the bull and bring it into the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:6 The priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times before the LORD in front of the curtain of the sanctuary.
Leviticus4:7 The priest shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense that is in the tent of meeting before the LORD; and the rest of the blood of the bull he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:8 He shall remove all the fat from the bull of sin offering: the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is around the entrails;
Leviticus4:9 the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins; and the appendage of the liver
Leviticus4:10 just as these are removed from the ox of the sacrifice of well-being. The priest shall turn them into smoke upon the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:11 But the skin of the bull and all its flesh
Leviticus4:12 all the rest of the bull-- he shall carry out to a clean place outside the camp
Leviticus4:13 If the whole congregation of Israel errs unintentionally and the matter escapes the notice of the assembly
Leviticus4:14 when the sin that they have committed becomes known
Leviticus4:15 The elders of the congregation shall lay their hands on the head of the bull before the LORD
Leviticus4:16 The anointed priest shall bring some of the blood of the bull into the tent of meeting
Leviticus4:17 and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the LORD
Leviticus4:18 He shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar that is before the LORD in the tent of meeting; and the rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering that is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:19 He shall remove all its fat and turn it into smoke on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 He shall do with the bull just as is done with the bull of sin offering; he shall do the same with this. The priest shall make atonement for them
Leviticus4:21 He shall carry the bull outside the camp
Leviticus4:22 When a ruler sins
Leviticus4:23 once the sin that he has committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:24 He shall lay his hand on the head of the goat; it shall be slaughtered at the spot where the burnt offering is slaughtered before the LORD; it is a sin offering.
Leviticus4:25 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:26 All its fat he shall turn into smoke on the altar
Leviticus4:27 If anyone of the ordinary people among you sins unintentionally in doing any one of the things that by the LORD's commandments ought not to be done and incurs guilt
Leviticus4:28 when the sin that you have committed is made known to you
Leviticus4:29 You shall lay your hand on the head of the sin offering; and the sin offering shall be slaughtered at the place of the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:30 The priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:31 He shall remove all its fat
Leviticus4:32 If the offering you bring as a sin offering is a sheep
Leviticus4:33 You shall lay your hand on the head of the sin offering; and it shall be slaughtered as a sin offering at the spot where the burnt offering is slaughtered.
Leviticus4:34 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:35 You shall remove all its fat
Leviticus5:1 When any of you sin in that you have heard a public adjuration to testify and-- though able to testify as one who has seen or learned of the matter-- does not speak up
Leviticus5:2 Or when any of you touch any unclean thing-- whether the carcass of an unclean beast or the carcass of unclean livestock or the carcass of an unclean swarming thing-- and are unaware of it
Leviticus5:3 Or when you touch human uncleanness-- any uncleanness by which one can become unclean-- and are unaware of it
Leviticus5:4 Or when any of you utter aloud a rash oath for a bad or a good purpose
Leviticus5:5 When you realize your guilt in any of these
Leviticus5:6 And you shall bring to the LORD
Leviticus5:7 But if you cannot afford a sheep
Leviticus5:8 You shall bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 He shall sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 And the second he shall offer for a burnt offering according to the regulation. Thus the priest shall make atonement on your behalf for the sin that you have committed
Leviticus5:11 But if you cannot afford two turtledoves or two pigeons
Leviticus5:12 You shall bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 Thus the priest shall make atonement on your behalf for whichever of these sins you have committed
Leviticus5:14 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus5:15 When any of you commit a trespass and sin unintentionally in any of the holy things of the LORD
Leviticus5:16 And you shall make restitution for the holy thing in which you were remiss
Leviticus5:17 If any of you sin without knowing it
Leviticus5:18 You shall bring to the priest a ram without blemish from the flock
Leviticus5:19 It is a guilt offering; you have incurred guilt before the LORD.
Leviticus6:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:2 When any of you sin and commit a trespass against the LORD by deceiving a neighbor in a matter of a deposit or a pledge
Leviticus6:3 or have found something lost and lied about it-- if you swear falsely regarding any of the various things that one may do and sin thereby--
Leviticus6:4 when you have sinned and realize your guilt
Leviticus6:5 or anything else about which you have sworn falsely
Leviticus6:6 And you shall bring to the priest
Leviticus6:7 The priest shall make atonement on your behalf before the LORD
Leviticus6:8 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:9 Command Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:10 The priest shall put on his linen vestments after putting on his linen undergarments next to his body; and he shall take up the ashes to which the fire has reduced the burnt offering on the altar
Leviticus6:11 Then he shall take off his vestments and put on other garments
Leviticus6:12 The fire on the altar shall be kept burning; it shall not go out. Every morning the priest shall add wood to it
Leviticus6:13 A perpetual fire shall be kept burning on the altar; it shall not go out.
Leviticus6:14 This is the ritual of the grain offering: The sons of Aaron shall offer it before the LORD
Leviticus6:15 They shall take from it a handful of the choice flour and oil of the grain offering
Leviticus6:16 Aaron and his sons shall eat what is left of it; it shall be eaten as unleavened cakes in a holy place; in the court of the tent of meeting they shall eat it.
Leviticus6:17 It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it as their portion of my offerings by fire; it is most holy
Leviticus6:18 Every male among the descendants of Aaron shall eat of it
Leviticus6:19 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:20 This is the offering that Aaron and his sons shall offer to the LORD on the day when he is anointed: one-tenth of an ephah of choice flour as a regular offering
Leviticus6:21 It shall be made with oil on a griddle; you shall bring it well soaked
Leviticus6:22 And so the priest
Leviticus6:23 Every grain offering of a priest shall be wholly burned; it shall not be eaten.
Leviticus6:24 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:25 Speak to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers it as a sin offering shall eat of it; it shall be eaten in a holy place
Leviticus6:27 Whatever touches its flesh shall become holy; and when any of its blood is spattered on a garment
Leviticus6:28 An earthen vessel in which it was boiled shall be broken; but if it is boiled in a bronze vessel
Leviticus6:29 Every male among the priests shall eat of it; it is most holy.
Leviticus6:30 But no sin offering shall be eaten from which any blood is brought into the tent of meeting for atonement in the holy place; it shall be burned with fire.
Leviticus7:1 This is the ritual of the guilt offering. It is most holy;
Leviticus7:2 at the spot where the burnt offering is slaughtered
Leviticus7:3 All its fat shall be offered: the broad tail
Leviticus7:4 the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus7:5 The priest shall turn them into smoke on the altar as an offering by fire to the LORD; it is a guilt offering.
Leviticus7:6 Every male among the priests shall eat of it; it shall be eaten in a holy place; it is most holy.
Leviticus7:7 The guilt offering is like the sin offering
Leviticus7:8 So
Leviticus7:9 And every grain offering baked in the oven
Leviticus7:10 But every other grain offering
Leviticus7:11 This is the ritual of the sacrifice of the offering of well-being that one may offer to the LORD.
Leviticus7:12 If you offer it for thanksgiving
Leviticus7:13 With your thanksgiving sacrifice of well-being you shall bring your offering with cakes of leavened bread.
Leviticus7:14 From this you shall offer one cake from each offering
Leviticus7:15 And the flesh of your thanksgiving sacrifice of well-being shall be eaten on the day it is offered; you shall not leave any of it until morning.
Leviticus7:16 But if the sacrifice you offer is a votive offering or a freewill offering
Leviticus7:17 but what is left of the flesh of the sacrifice shall be burned up on the third day.
Leviticus7:18 If any of the flesh of your sacrifice of well-being is eaten on the third day
Leviticus7:19 Flesh that touches any unclean thing shall not be eaten; it shall be burned up. As for other flesh
Leviticus7:20 But those who eat flesh from the LORD's sacrifice of well-being while in a state of uncleanness shall be cut off from their kin.
Leviticus7:21 When any one of you touches any unclean thing-- human uncleanness or an unclean animal or any unclean creature-- and then eats flesh from the LORD's sacrifice of well-being
Leviticus7:22 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:23 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus7:24 The fat of an animal that died or was torn by wild animals may be put to any use
Leviticus7:25 If any one of you eats the fat from an animal of which an offering by fire may be made to the LORD
Leviticus7:26 You must not eat any blood whatever
Leviticus7:27 Any one of you who eats any blood shall be cut off from your kin.
Leviticus7:28 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:29 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus7:30 Your own hands shall bring the LORD's offering by fire; you shall bring the fat with the breast
Leviticus7:31 The priest shall turn the fat into smoke on the altar
Leviticus7:32 And the right thigh from your sacrifices of well-being you shall give to the priest as an offering;
Leviticus7:33 the one among the sons of Aaron who offers the blood and fat of the offering of well-being shall have the right thigh for a portion.
Leviticus7:34 For I have taken the breast of the elevation offering
Leviticus7:35 This is the portion allotted to Aaron and to his sons from the offerings made by fire to the LORD
Leviticus7:36 these the LORD commanded to be given them
Leviticus7:37 This is the ritual of the burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 which the LORD commanded Moses on Mount Sinai
Leviticus8:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus8:2 Take Aaron and his sons with him
Leviticus8:3 and assemble the whole congregation at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus8:4 And Moses did as the LORD commanded him. When the congregation was assembled at the entrance of the tent of meeting
Leviticus8:5 Moses said to the congregation
Leviticus8:6 Then Moses brought Aaron and his sons forward
Leviticus8:7 He put the tunic on him
Leviticus8:8 He placed the breastpiece on him
Leviticus8:9 And he set the turban on his head
Leviticus8:10 Then Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and all that was in it
Leviticus8:11 He sprinkled some of it on the altar seven times
Leviticus8:12 He poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron's head and anointed him
Leviticus8:13 And Moses brought forward Aaron's sons
Leviticus8:14 He led forward the bull of sin offering; and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the bull of sin offering
Leviticus8:15 and it was slaughtered. Moses took the blood and with his finger put some on each of the horns of the altar
Leviticus8:16 Moses took all the fat that was around the entrails
Leviticus8:17 But the bull itself
Leviticus8:18 Then he brought forward the ram of burnt offering. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram
Leviticus8:19 and it was slaughtered. Moses dashed the blood against all sides of the altar.
Leviticus8:20 The ram was cut into its parts
Leviticus8:21 And after the entrails and the legs were washed with water
Leviticus8:22 Then he brought forward the second ram
Leviticus8:23 and it was slaughtered. Moses took some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron's right ear and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.
Leviticus8:24 After Aaron's sons were brought forward
Leviticus8:25 He took the fat-- the broad tail
Leviticus8:26 From the basket of unleavened bread that was before the LORD
Leviticus8:27 He placed all these on the palms of Aaron and on the palms of his sons
Leviticus8:28 Then Moses took them from their hands and turned them into smoke on the altar with the burnt offering. This was an ordination offering for a pleasing odor
Leviticus8:29 Moses took the breast and raised it as an elevation offering before the LORD; it was Moses' portion of the ram of ordination
Leviticus8:30 Then Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar and sprinkled them on Aaron and his vestments
Leviticus8:31 And Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 and what remains of the flesh and the bread you shall burn with fire.
Leviticus8:33 You shall not go outside the entrance of the tent of meeting for seven days
Leviticus8:34 as has been done today
Leviticus8:35 You shall remain at the entrance of the tent of meeting day and night for seven days
Leviticus8:36 Aaron and his sons did all the things that the LORD commanded through Moses.
Leviticus9:1 On the eighth day Moses summoned Aaron and his sons and the elders of Israel.
Leviticus9:2 He said to Aaron
Leviticus9:3 And say to the people of Israel
Leviticus9:4 and an ox and a ram for an offering of well-being to sacrifice before the LORD; and a grain offering mixed with oil. For today the LORD will appear to you.'
Leviticus9:5 They brought what Moses commanded to the front of the tent of meeting; and the whole congregation drew near and stood before the LORD.
Leviticus9:6 And Moses said
Leviticus9:7 Then Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus9:8 Aaron drew near to the altar
Leviticus9:9 The sons of Aaron presented the blood to him
Leviticus9:10 But the fat
Leviticus9:11 and the flesh and the skin he burned with fire outside the camp.
Leviticus9:12 Then he slaughtered the burnt offering. Aaron's sons brought him the blood
Leviticus9:13 And they brought him the burnt offering piece by piece
Leviticus9:14 He washed the entrails and the legs and
Leviticus9:15 Next he presented the people's offering. He took the goat of the sin offering that was for the people
Leviticus9:16 He presented the burnt offering
Leviticus9:17 He presented the grain offering
Leviticus9:18 He slaughtered the ox and the ram as a sacrifice of well-being for the people. Aaron's sons brought him the blood
Leviticus9:19 and the fat of the ox and of the ram-- the broad tail
Leviticus9:20 They first laid the fat on the breasts
Leviticus9:21 and the breasts and the right thigh Aaron raised as an elevation offering before the LORD
Leviticus9:22 Aaron lifted his hands toward the people and blessed them; and he came down after sacrificing the sin offering
Leviticus9:23 Moses and Aaron entered the tent of meeting
Leviticus9:24 Fire came out from the LORD and consumed the burnt offering and the fat on the altar; and when all the people saw it
Leviticus10:1 Now Aaron's sons
Leviticus10:2 And fire came out from the presence of the LORD and consumed them
Leviticus10:3 Then Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus10:4 Moses summoned Mishael and Elzaphan
Leviticus10:5 They came forward and carried them by their tunics out of the camp
Leviticus10:6 And Moses said to Aaron and to his sons Eleazar and Ithamar
Leviticus10:7 You shall not go outside the entrance of the tent of meeting
Leviticus10:8 And the LORD spoke to Aaron:
Leviticus10:9 Drink no wine or strong drink
Leviticus10:10 You are to distinguish between the holy and the common
Leviticus10:11 and you are to teach the people of Israel all the statutes that the LORD has spoken to them through Moses.
Leviticus10:12 Moses spoke to Aaron and to his remaining sons
Leviticus10:13 you shall eat it in a holy place
Leviticus10:14 But the breast that is elevated and the thigh that is raised
Leviticus10:15 The thigh that is raised and the breast that is elevated they shall bring
Leviticus10:16 Then Moses made inquiry about the goat of the sin offering
Leviticus10:17 Why did you not eat the sin offering in the sacred area? For it is most holy
Leviticus10:18 Its blood was not brought into the inner part of the sanctuary. You should certainly have eaten it in the sanctuary
Leviticus10:19 And Aaron spoke to Moses
Leviticus10:20 And when Moses heard that
Leviticus11:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus11:3 Any animal that has divided hoofs and is cleft-footed and chews the cud-- such you may eat.
Leviticus11:4 But among those that chew the cud or have divided hoofs
Leviticus11:5 The rock badger
Leviticus11:6 The hare
Leviticus11:7 The pig
Leviticus11:8 Of their flesh you shall not eat
Leviticus11:9 These you may eat
Leviticus11:10 But anything in the seas or the streams that does not have fins and scales
Leviticus11:11 and detestable they shall remain. Of their flesh you shall not eat
Leviticus11:12 Everything in the waters that does not have fins and scales is detestable to you.
Leviticus11:13 These you shall regard as detestable among the birds. They shall not be eaten; they are an abomination: the eagle
Leviticus11:14 the buzzard
Leviticus11:15 every raven of any kind;
Leviticus11:16 the ostrich
Leviticus11:17 the little owl
Leviticus11:18 the water hen
Leviticus11:19 the stork
Leviticus11:20 All winged insects that walk upon all fours are detestable to you.
Leviticus11:21 But among the winged insects that walk on all fours you may eat those that have jointed legs above their feet
Leviticus11:22 Of them you may eat: the locust according to its kind
Leviticus11:23 But all other winged insects that have four feet are detestable to you.
Leviticus11:24 By these you shall become unclean; whoever touches the carcass of any of them shall be unclean until the evening
Leviticus11:25 and whoever carries any part of the carcass of any of them shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:26 Every animal that has divided hoofs but is not cleft-footed or does not chew the cud is unclean for you; everyone who touches one of them shall be unclean.
Leviticus11:27 All that walk on their paws
Leviticus11:28 and the one who carries the carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean for you.
Leviticus11:29 These are unclean for you among the creatures that swarm upon the earth: the weasel
Leviticus11:30 the gecko
Leviticus11:31 These are unclean for you among all that swarm; whoever touches one of them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:32 And anything upon which any of them falls when they are dead shall be unclean
Leviticus11:33 And if any of them falls into any earthen vessel
Leviticus11:34 Any food that could be eaten shall be unclean if water from any such vessel comes upon it; and any liquid that could be drunk shall be unclean if it was in any such vessel.
Leviticus11:35 Everything on which any part of the carcass falls shall be unclean; whether an oven or stove
Leviticus11:36 But a spring or a cistern holding water shall be clean
Leviticus11:37 If any part of their carcass falls upon any seed set aside for sowing
Leviticus11:38 but if water is put on the seed and any part of their carcass falls on it
Leviticus11:39 If an animal of which you may eat dies
Leviticus11:40 Those who eat of its carcass shall wash their clothes and be unclean until the evening; and those who carry the carcass shall wash their clothes and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:41 All creatures that swarm upon the earth are detestable; they shall not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Whatever moves on its belly
Leviticus11:43 You shall not make yourselves detestable with any creature that swarms; you shall not defile yourselves with them
Leviticus11:44 For I am the LORD your God; sanctify yourselves therefore
Leviticus11:45 For I am the LORD who brought you up from the land of Egypt
Leviticus11:46 This is the law pertaining to land animal and bird and every living creature that moves through the waters and every creature that swarms upon the earth
Leviticus11:47 to make a distinction between the unclean and the clean
Leviticus12:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus12:2 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus12:3 On the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised.
Leviticus12:4 Her time of blood purification shall be thirty-three days; she shall not touch any holy thing
Leviticus12:5 If she bears a female child
Leviticus12:6 When the days of her purification are completed
Leviticus12:7 He shall offer it before the LORD
Leviticus12:8 If she cannot afford a sheep
Leviticus13:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 When a person has on the skin of his body a swelling or an eruption or a spot
Leviticus13:3 The priest shall examine the disease on the skin of his body
Leviticus13:4 But if the spot is white in the skin of his body
Leviticus13:5 The priest shall examine him on the seventh day
Leviticus13:6 The priest shall examine him again on the seventh day
Leviticus13:7 But if the eruption spreads in the skin after he has shown himself to the priest for his cleansing
Leviticus13:8 The priest shall make an examination
Leviticus13:9 When a person contracts a leprous disease
Leviticus13:10 The priest shall make an examination
Leviticus13:11 it is a chronic leprous disease in the skin of his body. The priest shall pronounce him unclean; he shall not confine him
Leviticus13:12 But if the disease breaks out in the skin
Leviticus13:13 then the priest shall make an examination
Leviticus13:14 But if raw flesh ever appears on him
Leviticus13:15 the priest shall examine the raw flesh and pronounce him unclean. Raw flesh is unclean
Leviticus13:16 But if the raw flesh again turns white
Leviticus13:17 the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:18 When there is on the skin of one's body a boil that has healed
Leviticus13:19 and in the place of the boil there appears a white swelling or a reddish-white spot
Leviticus13:20 The priest shall make an examination
Leviticus13:21 But if the priest examines it and the hair on it is not white
Leviticus13:22 If it spreads in the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the spot remains in one place and does not spread
Leviticus13:24 Or
Leviticus13:25 the priest shall examine it. If the hair in the spot has turned white and it appears deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest examines it and the hair in the spot is not white
Leviticus13:27 The priest shall examine him the seventh day; if it is spreading in the skin
Leviticus13:28 But if the spot remains in one place and does not spread in the skin but has abated
Leviticus13:29 When a man or woman has a disease on the head or in the beard
Leviticus13:30 the priest shall examine the disease. If it appears deeper than the skin and the hair in it is yellow and thin
Leviticus13:31 If the priest examines the itching disease
Leviticus13:32 On the seventh day the priest shall examine the itch; if the itch has not spread
Leviticus13:33 he shall shave
Leviticus13:34 On the seventh day the priest shall examine the itch; if the itch has not spread in the skin and it appears to be no deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:35 But if the itch spreads in the skin after he was pronounced clean
Leviticus13:36 the priest shall examine him. If the itch has spread in the skin
Leviticus13:37 But if in his eyes the itch is checked
Leviticus13:38 When a man or a woman has spots on the skin of the body
Leviticus13:39 the priest shall make an examination
Leviticus13:40 If anyone loses the hair from his head
Leviticus13:41 If he loses the hair from his forehead and temples
Leviticus13:42 But if there is on the bald head or the bald forehead a reddish-white diseased spot
Leviticus13:43 The priest shall examine him; if the diseased swelling is reddish-white on his bald head or on his bald forehead
Leviticus13:44 he is leprous
Leviticus13:45 The person who has the leprous disease shall wear torn clothes and let the hair of his head be disheveled; and he shall cover his upper lip and cry out
Leviticus13:46 He shall remain unclean as long as he has the disease; he is unclean. He shall live alone; his dwelling shall be outside the camp.
Leviticus13:47 Concerning clothing: when a leprous disease appears in it
Leviticus13:48 in warp or woof of linen or wool
Leviticus13:49 if the disease shows greenish or reddish in the garment
Leviticus13:50 The priest shall examine the disease
Leviticus13:51 He shall examine the disease on the seventh day. If the disease has spread in the cloth
Leviticus13:52 He shall burn the clothing
Leviticus13:53 If the priest makes an examination
Leviticus13:54 the priest shall command them to wash the article in which the disease appears
Leviticus13:55 The priest shall examine the diseased article after it has been washed. If the diseased spot has not changed color
Leviticus13:56 If the priest makes an examination
Leviticus13:57 If it appears again in the garment
Leviticus13:58 But the cloth
Leviticus13:59 This is the ritual for a leprous disease in a cloth of wool or linen
Leviticus14:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus14:2 This shall be the ritual for the leprous person at the time of his cleansing: He shall be brought to the priest;
Leviticus14:3 the priest shall go out of the camp
Leviticus14:4 the priest shall command that two living clean birds and cedarwood and crimson yarn and hyssop be brought for the one who is to be cleansed.
Leviticus14:5 The priest shall command that one of the birds be slaughtered over fresh water in an earthen vessel.
Leviticus14:6 He shall take the living bird with the cedarwood and the crimson yarn and the hyssop
Leviticus14:7 He shall sprinkle it seven times upon the one who is to be cleansed of the leprous disease; then he shall pronounce him clean
Leviticus14:8 The one who is to be cleansed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus14:9 On the seventh day he shall shave all his hair: of head
Leviticus14:10 On the eighth day he shall take two male lambs without blemish
Leviticus14:11 The priest who cleanses shall set the person to be cleansed
Leviticus14:12 The priest shall take one of the lambs
Leviticus14:13 He shall slaughter the lamb in the place where the sin offering and the burnt offering are slaughtered in the holy place; for the guilt offering
Leviticus14:14 The priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:15 The priest shall take some of the log of oil and pour it into the palm of his own left hand
Leviticus14:16 and dip his right finger in the oil that is in his left hand and sprinkle some oil with his finger seven times before the LORD.
Leviticus14:17 Some of the oil that remains in his hand the priest shall put on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:18 The rest of the oil that is in the priest's hand he shall put on the head of the one to be cleansed. Then the priest shall make atonement on his behalf before the LORD:
Leviticus14:19 the priest shall offer the sin offering
Leviticus14:20 and the priest shall offer the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar. Thus the priest shall make atonement on his behalf and he shall be clean.
Leviticus14:21 But if he is poor and cannot afford so much
Leviticus14:22 also two turtledoves or two pigeons
Leviticus14:23 On the eighth day he shall bring them for his cleansing to the priest
Leviticus14:24 and the priest shall take the lamb of the guilt offering and the log of oil
Leviticus14:25 The priest shall slaughter the lamb of the guilt offering and shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering
Leviticus14:26 The priest shall pour some of the oil into the palm of his own left hand
Leviticus14:27 and shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is in his left hand seven times before the LORD.
Leviticus14:28 The priest shall put some of the oil that is in his hand on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:29 The rest of the oil that is in the priest's hand he shall put on the head of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:30 And he shall offer
Leviticus14:31 one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering
Leviticus14:32 This is the ritual for the one who has a leprous disease
Leviticus14:33 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus14:34 When you come into the land of Canaan
Leviticus14:35 the owner of the house shall come and tell the priest
Leviticus14:36 The priest shall command that they empty the house before the priest goes to examine the disease
Leviticus14:37 He shall examine the disease; if the disease is in the walls of the house with greenish or reddish spots
Leviticus14:38 the priest shall go outside to the door of the house and shut up the house seven days.
Leviticus14:39 The priest shall come again on the seventh day and make an inspection; if the disease has spread in the walls of the house
Leviticus14:40 the priest shall command that the stones in which the disease appears be taken out and thrown into an unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:41 He shall have the inside of the house scraped thoroughly
Leviticus14:42 They shall take other stones and put them in the place of those stones
Leviticus14:43 If the disease breaks out again in the house
Leviticus14:44 the priest shall go and make inspection; if the disease has spread in the house
Leviticus14:45 He shall have the house torn down
Leviticus14:46 All who enter the house while it is shut up shall be unclean until the evening;
Leviticus14:47 and all who sleep in the house shall wash their clothes; and all who eat in the house shall wash their clothes.
Leviticus14:48 If the priest comes and makes an inspection
Leviticus14:49 For the cleansing of the house he shall take two birds
Leviticus14:50 and shall slaughter one of the birds over fresh water in an earthen vessel
Leviticus14:51 and shall take the cedarwood and the hyssop and the crimson yarn
Leviticus14:52 Thus he shall cleanse the house with the blood of the bird
Leviticus14:53 and he shall let the living bird go out of the city into the open field; so he shall make atonement for the house
Leviticus14:54 This is the ritual for any leprous disease: for an itch
Leviticus14:55 for leprous diseases in clothing and houses
Leviticus14:56 and for a swelling or an eruption or a spot
Leviticus14:57 to determine when it is unclean and when it is clean. This is the ritual for leprous diseases.
Leviticus15:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus15:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When any man has a discharge from his member
Leviticus15:3 The uncleanness of his discharge is this: whether his member flows with his discharge
Leviticus15:4 Every bed on which the one with the discharge lies shall be unclean; and everything on which he sits shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:5 Anyone who touches his bed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:6 All who sit on anything on which the one with the discharge has sat shall wash their clothes
Leviticus15:7 All who touch the body of the one with the discharge shall wash their clothes
Leviticus15:8 If the one with the discharge spits on persons who are clean
Leviticus15:9 Any saddle on which the one with the discharge rides shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:10 All who touch anything that was under him shall be unclean until the evening
Leviticus15:11 All those whom the one with the discharge touches without his having rinsed his hands in water shall wash their clothes
Leviticus15:12 Any earthen vessel that the one with the discharge touches shall be broken; and every vessel of wood shall be rinsed in water.
Leviticus15:13 When the one with a discharge is cleansed of his discharge
Leviticus15:14 On the eighth day he shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons and come before the LORD to the entrance of the tent of meeting and give them to the priest.
Leviticus15:15 The priest shall offer them
Leviticus15:16 If a man has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:17 Everything made of cloth or of skin on which the semen falls shall be washed with water
Leviticus15:18 If a man lies with a woman and has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:19 When a woman has a discharge of blood that is her regular discharge from her body
Leviticus15:20 Everything upon which she lies during her impurity shall be unclean; everything also upon which she sits shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:21 Whoever touches her bed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:22 Whoever touches anything upon which she sits shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:23 whether it is the bed or anything upon which she sits
Leviticus15:24 If any man lies with her
Leviticus15:25 If a woman has a discharge of blood for many days
Leviticus15:26 Every bed on which she lies during all the days of her discharge shall be treated as the bed of her impurity; and everything on which she sits shall be unclean
Leviticus15:27 Whoever touches these things shall be unclean
Leviticus15:28 If she is cleansed of her discharge
Leviticus15:29 On the eighth day she shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons and bring them to the priest to the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus15:30 The priest shall offer one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering; and the priest shall make atonement on her behalf before the LORD for her unclean discharge.
Leviticus15:31 Thus you shall keep the people of Israel separate from their uncleanness
Leviticus15:32 This is the ritual for those who have a discharge: for him who has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:33 for her who is in the infirmity of her period
Leviticus16:1 The LORD spoke to Moses after the death of the two sons of Aaron
Leviticus16:2 The LORD said to Moses: Tell your brother Aaron not to come just at any time into the sanctuary inside the curtain before the mercy seat that is upon the ark
Leviticus16:3 Thus shall Aaron come into the holy place: with a young bull for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:4 He shall put on the holy linen tunic
Leviticus16:5 He shall take from the congregation of the people of Israel two male goats for a sin offering
Leviticus16:6 Aaron shall offer the bull as a sin offering for himself
Leviticus16:7 He shall take the two goats and set them before the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting;
Leviticus16:8 and Aaron shall cast lots on the two goats
Leviticus16:9 Aaron shall present the goat on which the lot fell for the LORD
Leviticus16:10 but the goat on which the lot fell for Azazel shall be presented alive before the LORD to make atonement over it
Leviticus16:11 Aaron shall present the bull as a sin offering for himself
Leviticus16:12 He shall take a censer full of coals of fire from the altar before the LORD
Leviticus16:13 and put the incense on the fire before the LORD
Leviticus16:14 He shall take some of the blood of the bull
Leviticus16:15 He shall slaughter the goat of the sin offering that is for the people and bring its blood inside the curtain
Leviticus16:16 Thus he shall make atonement for the sanctuary
Leviticus16:17 No one shall be in the tent of meeting from the time he enters to make atonement in the sanctuary until he comes out and has made atonement for himself and for his house and for all the assembly of Israel.
Leviticus16:18 Then he shall go out to the altar that is before the LORD and make atonement on its behalf
Leviticus16:19 He shall sprinkle some of the blood on it with his finger seven times
Leviticus16:20 When he has finished atoning for the holy place and the tent of meeting and the altar
Leviticus16:21 Then Aaron shall lay both his hands on the head of the live goat
Leviticus16:22 The goat shall bear on itself all their iniquities to a barren region; and the goat shall be set free in the wilderness.
Leviticus16:23 Then Aaron shall enter the tent of meeting
Leviticus16:24 He shall bathe his body in water in a holy place
Leviticus16:25 The fat of the sin offering he shall turn into smoke on the altar.
Leviticus16:26 The one who sets the goat free for Azazel shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Leviticus16:27 The bull of the sin offering and the goat of the sin offering
Leviticus16:28 The one who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Leviticus16:29 This shall be a statute to you forever: In the seventh month
Leviticus16:30 For on this day atonement shall be made for you
Leviticus16:31 It is a sabbath of complete rest to you
Leviticus16:32 The priest who is anointed and consecrated as priest in his father's place shall make atonement
Leviticus16:33 He shall make atonement for the sanctuary
Leviticus16:34 This shall be an everlasting statute for you
Leviticus17:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus17:2 Speak to Aaron and his sons and to all the people of Israel and say to them: This is what the LORD has commanded.
Leviticus17:3 If anyone of the house of Israel slaughters an ox or a lamb or a goat in the camp
Leviticus17:4 and does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting
Leviticus17:5 This is in order that the people of Israel may bring their sacrifices that they offer in the open field
Leviticus17:6 The priest shall dash the blood against the altar of the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting
Leviticus17:7 so that they may no longer offer their sacrifices for goat-demons
