Leviticus17:8 And say to them further: Anyone of the house of Israel or of the aliens who reside among them who offers a burnt offering or sacrifice
Leviticus17:9 and does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting
Leviticus17:10 If anyone of the house of Israel or of the aliens who reside among them eats any blood
Leviticus17:11 For the life of the flesh is in the blood; and I have given it to you for making atonement for your lives on the altar; for
Leviticus17:12 Therefore I have said to the people of Israel: No person among you shall eat blood
Leviticus17:13 And anyone of the people of Israel
Leviticus17:14 For the life of every creature-- its blood is its life; therefore I have said to the people of Israel: You shall not eat the blood of any creature
Leviticus17:15 All persons
Leviticus17:16 But if they do not wash themselves or bathe their body
Leviticus18:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus18:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus18:3 You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt
Leviticus18:4 My ordinances you shall observe and my statutes you shall keep
Leviticus18:5 You shall keep my statutes and my ordinances; by doing so one shall live: I am the LORD.
Leviticus18:6 None of you shall approach anyone near of kin to uncover nakedness: I am the LORD.
Leviticus18:7 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father
Leviticus18:8 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's wife; it is the nakedness of your father.
Leviticus18:9 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your sister
Leviticus18:10 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your son's daughter or of your daughter's daughter
Leviticus18:11 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's wife's daughter
Leviticus18:12 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's sister; she is your father's flesh.
Leviticus18:13 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother's sister
Leviticus18:14 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's brother
Leviticus18:15 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law: she is your son's wife; you shall not uncover her nakedness.
Leviticus18:16 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother's wife; it is your brother's nakedness.
Leviticus18:17 You shall not uncover the nakedness of a woman and her daughter
Leviticus18:18 And you shall not take a woman as a rival to her sister
Leviticus18:19 You shall not approach a woman to uncover her nakedness while she is in her menstrual uncleanness.
Leviticus18:20 You shall not have sexual relations with your kinsman's wife
Leviticus18:21 You shall not give any of your offspring to sacrifice them to Molech
Leviticus18:22 You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; it is an abomination.
Leviticus18:23 You shall not have sexual relations with any animal and defile yourself with it
Leviticus18:24 Do not defile yourselves in any of these ways
Leviticus18:25 Thus the land became defiled; and I punished it for its iniquity
Leviticus18:26 But you shall keep my statutes and my ordinances and commit none of these abominations
Leviticus18:27 (for the inhabitants of the land
Leviticus18:28 otherwise the land will vomit you out for defiling it
Leviticus18:29 For whoever commits any of these abominations shall be cut off from their people.
Leviticus18:30 So keep my charge not to commit any of these abominations that were done before you
Leviticus19:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus19:2 Speak to all the congregation of the people of Israel and say to them: You shall be holy
Leviticus19:3 You shall each revere your mother and father
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn to idols or make cast images for yourselves: I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus19:5 When you offer a sacrifice of well-being to the LORD
Leviticus19:6 It shall be eaten on the same day you offer it
Leviticus19:7 If it is eaten at all on the third day
Leviticus19:8 All who eat it shall be subject to punishment
Leviticus19:9 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 You shall not strip your vineyard bare
Leviticus19:11 You shall not steal; you shall not deal falsely; and you shall not lie to one another.
Leviticus19:12 And you shall not swear falsely by my name
Leviticus19:13 You shall not defraud your neighbor; you shall not steal; and you shall not keep for yourself the wages of a laborer until morning.
Leviticus19:14 You shall not revile the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind; you shall fear your God: I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:15 You shall not render an unjust judgment; you shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great: with justice you shall judge your neighbor.
Leviticus19:16 You shall not go around as a slanderer among your people
Leviticus19:17 You shall not hate in your heart anyone of your kin; you shall reprove your neighbor
Leviticus19:18 You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against any of your people
Leviticus19:19 You shall keep my statutes. You shall not let your animals breed with a different kind; you shall not sow your field with two kinds of seed; nor shall you put on a garment made of two different materials.
Leviticus19:20 If a man has sexual relations with a woman who is a slave
Leviticus19:21 but he shall bring a guilt offering for himself to the LORD
Leviticus19:22 And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of guilt offering before the LORD for his sin that he committed; and the sin he committed shall be forgiven him.
Leviticus19:23 When you come into the land and plant all kinds of trees for food
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year all their fruit shall be set apart for rejoicing in the LORD.
Leviticus19:25 But in the fifth year you may eat of their fruit
Leviticus19:26 You shall not eat anything with its blood. You shall not practice augury or witchcraft.
Leviticus19:27 You shall not round off the hair on your temples or mar the edges of your beard.
Leviticus19:28 You shall not make any gashes in your flesh for the dead or tattoo any marks upon you: I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:29 Do not profane your daughter by making her a prostitute
Leviticus19:30 You shall keep my sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:31 Do not turn to mediums or wizards; do not seek them out
Leviticus19:32 You shall rise before the aged
Leviticus19:33 When an alien resides with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 The alien who resides with you shall be to you as the citizen among you; you shall love the alien as yourself
Leviticus19:35 You shall not cheat in measuring length
Leviticus19:36 You shall have honest balances
Leviticus19:37 You shall keep all my statutes and all my ordinances
Leviticus20:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus20:2 Say further to the people of Israel: Any of the people of Israel
Leviticus20:3 I myself will set my face against them
Leviticus20:4 And if the people of the land should ever close their eyes to them
Leviticus20:5 I myself will set my face against them and against their family
Leviticus20:6 If any turn to mediums and wizards
Leviticus20:7 Consecrate yourselves therefore
Leviticus20:8 Keep my statutes
Leviticus20:9 All who curse father or mother shall be put to death; having cursed father or mother
Leviticus20:10 If a man commits adultery with the wife of his neighbor
Leviticus20:11 The man who lies with his father's wife has uncovered his father's nakedness; both of them shall be put to death; their blood is upon them.
Leviticus20:12 If a man lies with his daughter-in-law
Leviticus20:13 If a man lies with a male as with a woman
Leviticus20:14 If a man takes a wife and her mother also
Leviticus20:15 If a man has sexual relations with an animal
Leviticus20:16 If a woman approaches any animal and has sexual relations with it
Leviticus20:17 If a man takes his sister
Leviticus20:18 If a man lies with a woman having her sickness and uncovers her nakedness
Leviticus20:19 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother's sister or of your father's sister
Leviticus20:20 If a man lies with his uncle's wife
Leviticus20:21 If a man takes his brother's wife
Leviticus20:22 You shall keep all my statutes and all my ordinances
Leviticus20:23 You shall not follow the practices of the nation that I am driving out before you. Because they did all these things
Leviticus20:24 But I have said to you: You shall inherit their land
Leviticus20:25 You shall therefore make a distinction between the clean animal and the unclean
Leviticus20:26 You shall be holy to me; for I the LORD am holy
Leviticus20:27 A man or a woman who is a medium or a wizard shall be put to death; they shall be stoned to death
Leviticus21:1 The LORD said to Moses: Speak to the priests
Leviticus21:2 except for his nearest kin: his mother
Leviticus21:3 likewise
Leviticus21:4 But he shall not defile himself as a husband among his people and so profane himself.
Leviticus21:5 They shall not make bald spots upon their heads
Leviticus21:6 They shall be holy to their God
Leviticus21:7 They shall not marry a prostitute or a woman who has been defiled; neither shall they marry a woman divorced from her husband. For they are holy to their God
Leviticus21:8 and you shall treat them as holy
Leviticus21:9 When the daughter of a priest profanes herself through prostitution
Leviticus21:10 The priest who is exalted above his fellows
Leviticus21:11 He shall not go where there is a dead body; he shall not defile himself even for his father or mother.
Leviticus21:12 He shall not go outside the sanctuary and thus profane the sanctuary of his God; for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the LORD.
Leviticus21:13 He shall marry only a woman who is a virgin.
Leviticus21:14 A widow
Leviticus21:15 that he may not profane his offspring among his kin; for I am the LORD; I sanctify him.
Leviticus21:16 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus21:17 Speak to Aaron and say: No one of your offspring throughout their generations who has a blemish may approach to offer the food of his God.
Leviticus21:18 For no one who has a blemish shall draw near
Leviticus21:19 or one who has a broken foot or a broken hand
Leviticus21:20 or a hunchback
Leviticus21:21 No descendant of Aaron the priest who has a blemish shall come near to offer the LORD's offerings by fire; since he has a blemish
Leviticus21:22 He may eat the food of his God
Leviticus21:23 But he shall not come near the curtain or approach the altar
Leviticus21:24 Thus Moses spoke to Aaron and to his sons and to all the people of Israel.
Leviticus22:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:2 Direct Aaron and his sons to deal carefully with the sacred donations of the people of Israel
Leviticus22:3 Say to them: If anyone among all your offspring throughout your generations comes near the sacred donations
Leviticus22:4 No one of Aaron's offspring who has a leprous disease or suffers a discharge may eat of the sacred donations until he is clean. Whoever touches anything made unclean by a corpse or a man who has had an emission of semen
Leviticus22:5 and whoever touches any swarming thing by which he may be made unclean or any human being by whom he may be made unclean-- whatever his uncleanness may be--
Leviticus22:6 the person who touches any such shall be unclean until evening and shall not eat of the sacred donations unless he has washed his body in water.
Leviticus22:7 When the sun sets he shall be clean; and afterward he may eat of the sacred donations
Leviticus22:8 That which died or was torn by wild animals he shall not eat
Leviticus22:9 They shall keep my charge
Leviticus22:10 No lay person shall eat of the sacred donations. No bound or hired servant of the priest shall eat of the sacred donations;
Leviticus22:11 but if a priest acquires anyone by purchase
Leviticus22:12 If a priest's daughter marries a layman
Leviticus22:13 but if a priest's daughter is widowed or divorced
Leviticus22:14 If a man eats of the sacred donation unintentionally
Leviticus22:15 No one shall profane the sacred donations of the people of Israel
Leviticus22:16 causing them to bear guilt requiring a guilt offering
Leviticus22:17 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:18 Speak to Aaron and his sons and all the people of Israel and say to them: When anyone of the house of Israel or of the aliens residing in Israel presents an offering
Leviticus22:19 to be acceptable in your behalf it shall be a male without blemish
Leviticus22:20 You shall not offer anything that has a blemish
Leviticus22:21 When anyone offers a sacrifice of well-being to the LORD
Leviticus22:22 Anything blind
Leviticus22:23 An ox or a lamb that has a limb too long or too short you may present for a freewill offering; but it will not be accepted for a vow.
Leviticus22:24 Any animal that has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn or cut
Leviticus22:25 nor shall you accept any such animals from a foreigner to offer as food to your God; since they are mutilated
Leviticus22:26 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:27 When an ox or a sheep or a goat is born
Leviticus22:28 But you shall not slaughter
Leviticus22:29 When you sacrifice a thanksgiving offering to the LORD
Leviticus22:30 It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall not leave any of it until morning: I am the LORD.
Leviticus22:31 Thus you shall keep my commandments and observe them: I am the LORD.
Leviticus22:32 You shall not profane my holy name
Leviticus22:33 I who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I am the LORD.
Leviticus23:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: These are the appointed festivals of the LORD that you shall proclaim as holy convocations
Leviticus23:3 Six days shall work be done; but the seventh day is a sabbath of complete rest
Leviticus23:4 These are the appointed festivals of the LORD
Leviticus23:5 In the first month
Leviticus23:6 and on the fifteenth day of the same month is the festival of unleavened bread to the LORD; seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
Leviticus23:8 For seven days you shall present the LORD's offerings by fire; on the seventh day there shall be a holy convocation: you shall not work at your occupations.
Leviticus23:9 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:10 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you and you reap its harvest
Leviticus23:11 He shall raise the sheaf before the LORD
Leviticus23:12 On the day when you raise the sheaf
Leviticus23:13 And the grain offering with it shall be two-tenths of an ephah of choice flour mixed with oil
Leviticus23:14 You shall eat no bread or parched grain or fresh ears until that very day
Leviticus23:15 And from the day after the sabbath
Leviticus23:16 You shall count until the day after the seventh sabbath
Leviticus23:17 You shall bring from your settlements two loaves of bread as an elevation offering
Leviticus23:18 You shall present with the bread seven lambs a year old without blemish
Leviticus23:19 You shall also offer one male goat for a sin offering
Leviticus23:20 The priest shall raise them with the bread of the first fruits as an elevation offering before the LORD
Leviticus23:21 On that same day you shall make proclamation; you shall hold a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations. This is a statute forever in all your settlements throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:24 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus23:25 You shall not work at your occupations; and you shall present the LORD's offering by fire.
Leviticus23:26 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:27 Now
Leviticus23:28 and you shall do no work during that entire day; for it is a day of atonement
Leviticus23:29 For anyone who does not practice self-denial during that entire day shall be cut off from the people.
Leviticus23:30 And anyone who does any work during that entire day
Leviticus23:31 You shall do no work: it is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your settlements.
Leviticus23:32 It shall be to you a sabbath of complete rest
Leviticus23:33 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:34 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus23:35 The first day shall be a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
Leviticus23:36 Seven days you shall present the LORD's offerings by fire; on the eighth day you shall observe a holy convocation and present the LORD's offerings by fire; it is a solemn assembly; you shall not work at your occupations.
Leviticus23:37 These are the appointed festivals of the LORD
Leviticus23:38 apart from the sabbaths of the LORD
Leviticus23:39 Now
Leviticus23:40 On the first day you shall take the fruit of majestic trees
Leviticus23:41 You shall keep it as a festival to the LORD seven days in the year; you shall keep it in the seventh month as a statute forever throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:42 You shall live in booths for seven days; all that are citizens in Israel shall live in booths
Leviticus23:43 so that your generations may know that I made the people of Israel live in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus23:44 Thus Moses declared to the people of Israel the appointed festivals of the LORD.
Leviticus24:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:2 Command the people of Israel to bring you pure oil of beaten olives for the lamp
Leviticus24:3 Aaron shall set it up in the tent of meeting
Leviticus24:4 He shall set up the lamps on the lampstand of pure gold before the LORD regularly.
Leviticus24:5 You shall take choice flour
Leviticus24:6 You shall place them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 You shall put pure frankincense with each row
Leviticus24:8 Every sabbath day Aaron shall set them in order before the LORD regularly as a commitment of the people of Israel
Leviticus24:9 They shall be for Aaron and his descendants
Leviticus24:10 A man whose mother was an Israelite and whose father was an Egyptian came out among the people of Israel; and the Israelite woman's son and a certain Israelite began fighting in the camp.
Leviticus24:11 The Israelite woman's son blasphemed the Name in a curse. And they brought him to Moses-- now his mother's name was Shelomith
Leviticus24:12 and they put him in custody
Leviticus24:13 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus24:14 Take the blasphemer outside the camp; and let all who were within hearing lay their hands on his head
Leviticus24:15 And speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus24:16 One who blasphemes the name of the LORD shall be put to death; the whole congregation shall stone the blasphemer. Aliens as well as citizens
Leviticus24:17 Anyone who kills a human being shall be put to death.
Leviticus24:18 Anyone who kills an animal shall make restitution for it
Leviticus24:19 Anyone who maims another shall suffer the same injury in return:
Leviticus24:20 fracture for fracture
Leviticus24:21 One who kills an animal shall make restitution for it; but one who kills a human being shall be put to death.
Leviticus24:22 You shall have one law for the alien and for the citizen: for I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus24:23 Moses spoke thus to the people of Israel; and they took the blasphemer outside the camp
Leviticus25:1 The LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you
Leviticus25:3 Six years you shall sow your field
Leviticus25:4 but in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of complete rest for the land
Leviticus25:5 You shall not reap the aftergrowth of your harvest or gather the grapes of your unpruned vine: it shall be a year of complete rest for the land.
Leviticus25:6 You may eat what the land yields during its sabbath-- you
Leviticus25:7 for your livestock also
Leviticus25:8 You shall count off seven weeks of years
Leviticus25:9 Then you shall have the trumpet sounded loud; on the tenth day of the seventh month-- on the day of atonement-- you shall have the trumpet sounded throughout all your land.
Leviticus25:10 And you shall hallow the fiftieth year and you shall proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you: you shall return
Leviticus25:11 That fiftieth year shall be a jubilee for you: you shall not sow
Leviticus25:12 For it is a jubilee; it shall be holy to you: you shall eat only what the field itself produces.
Leviticus25:13 In this year of jubilee you shall return
Leviticus25:14 When you make a sale to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor
Leviticus25:15 When you buy from your neighbor
Leviticus25:16 If the years are more
Leviticus25:17 You shall not cheat one another
Leviticus25:18 You shall observe my statutes and faithfully keep my ordinances
Leviticus25:19 The land will yield its fruit
Leviticus25:20 Should you ask
Leviticus25:21 I will order my blessing for you in the sixth year
Leviticus25:22 When you sow in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 The land shall not be sold in perpetuity
Leviticus25:24 Throughout the land that you hold
Leviticus25:25 If anyone of your kin falls into difficulty and sells a piece of property
Leviticus25:26 If the person has no one to redeem it
Leviticus25:27 the years since its sale shall be computed and the difference shall be refunded to the person to whom it was sold
Leviticus25:28 But if there is not sufficient means to recover it
Leviticus25:29 If anyone sells a dwelling house in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 If it is not redeemed before a full year has elapsed
Leviticus25:31 But houses in villages that have no walls around them shall be classed as open country; they may be redeemed
Leviticus25:32 As for the cities of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 Such property as may be redeemed from the Levites-- houses sold in a city belonging to them-- shall be released in the jubilee; because the houses in the cities of the Levites are their possession among the people of Israel.
Leviticus25:34 But the open land around their cities may not be sold; for that is their possession for all time.
Leviticus25:35 If any of your kin fall into difficulty and become dependent on you
Leviticus25:36 Do not take interest in advance or otherwise make a profit from them
Leviticus25:37 You shall not lend them your money at interest taken in advance
Leviticus25:38 I am the LORD your God
Leviticus25:39 If any who are dependent on you become so impoverished that they sell themselves to you
Leviticus25:40 They shall remain with you as hired or bound laborers. They shall serve with you until the year of the jubilee.
Leviticus25:41 Then they and their children with them shall be free from your authority; they shall go back to their own family and return to their ancestral property.
Leviticus25:42 For they are my servants
Leviticus25:43 You shall not rule over them with harshness
Leviticus25:44 As for the male and female slaves whom you may have
Leviticus25:45 You may also acquire them from among the aliens residing with you
Leviticus25:46 You may keep them as a possession for your children after you
Leviticus25:47 If resident aliens among you prosper
Leviticus25:48 after they have sold themselves they shall have the right of redemption; one of their brothers may redeem them
Leviticus25:49 or their uncle or their uncle's son may redeem them
Leviticus25:50 They shall compute with the purchaser the total from the year when they sold themselves to the alien until the jubilee year; the price of the sale shall be applied to the number of years: the time they were with the owner shall be rated as the time of a hired laborer.
Leviticus25:51 If many years remain
Leviticus25:52 and if few years remain until the jubilee year
Leviticus25:53 As a laborer hired by the year they shall be under the alien's authority
Leviticus25:54 And if they have not been redeemed in any of these ways
Leviticus25:55 For to me the people of Israel are servants; they are my servants whom I brought out from the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus26:1 You shall make for yourselves no idols and erect no carved images or pillars
Leviticus26:2 You shall keep my sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.
Leviticus26:3 If you follow my statutes and keep my commandments and observe them faithfully
Leviticus26:4 I will give you your rains in their season
Leviticus26:5 Your threshing shall overtake the vintage
Leviticus26:6 And I will grant peace in the land
Leviticus26:7 You shall give chase to your enemies
Leviticus26:8 Five of you shall give chase to a hundred
Leviticus26:9 I will look with favor upon you and make you fruitful and multiply you; and I will maintain my covenant with you.
Leviticus26:10 You shall eat old grain long stored
Leviticus26:11 I will place my dwelling in your midst
Leviticus26:12 And I will walk among you
Leviticus26:13 I am the LORD your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt
Leviticus26:14 But if you will not obey me
Leviticus26:15 if you spurn my statutes
Leviticus26:16 I in turn will do this to you: I will bring terror on you; consumption and fever that waste the eyes and cause life to pine away. You shall sow your seed in vain
Leviticus26:17 I will set my face against you
Leviticus26:18 And if in spite of this you will not obey me
Leviticus26:19 I will break your proud glory
Leviticus26:20 Your strength shall be spent to no purpose: your land shall not yield its produce
Leviticus26:21 If you continue hostile to me
Leviticus26:22 I will let loose wild animals against you
Leviticus26:23 If in spite of these punishments you have not turned back to me
Leviticus26:24 then I too will continue hostile to you: I myself will strike you sevenfold for your sins.
Leviticus26:25 I will bring the sword against you
Leviticus26:26 When I break your staff of bread
Leviticus26:27 But if
Leviticus26:28 I will continue hostile to you in fury; I in turn will punish you myself sevenfold for your sins.
Leviticus26:29 You shall eat the flesh of your sons
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your high places and cut down your incense altars; I will heap your carcasses on the carcasses of your idols. I will abhor you.
Leviticus26:31 I will lay your cities waste
Leviticus26:32 I will devastate the land
Leviticus26:33 And you I will scatter among the nations
Leviticus26:34 Then the land shall enjoy its sabbath years as long as it lies desolate
Leviticus26:35 As long as it lies desolate
Leviticus26:36 And as for those of you who survive
Leviticus26:37 They shall stumble over one another
Leviticus26:38 You shall perish among the nations
Leviticus26:39 And those of you who survive shall languish in the land of your enemies because of their iniquities; also they shall languish because of the iniquities of their ancestors.
Leviticus26:40 But if they confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their ancestors
Leviticus26:41 so that I
Leviticus26:42 then will I remember my covenant with Jacob; I will remember also my covenant with Isaac and also my covenant with Abraham
Leviticus26:43 For the land shall be deserted by them
Leviticus26:44 Yet for all that
Leviticus26:45 but I will remember in their favor the covenant with their ancestors whom I brought out of the land of Egypt in the sight of the nations
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes and ordinances and laws that the LORD established between himself and the people of Israel on Mount Sinai through Moses.
Leviticus27:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus27:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When a person makes an explicit vow to the LORD concerning the equivalent for a human being
Leviticus27:3 the equivalent for a male shall be: from twenty to sixty years of age the equivalent shall be fifty shekels of silver by the sanctuary shekel.
Leviticus27:4 If the person is a female
Leviticus27:5 If the age is from five to twenty years of age
Leviticus27:6 If the age is from one month to five years
Leviticus27:7 And if the person is sixty years old or over
Leviticus27:8 If any cannot afford the equivalent
Leviticus27:9 If it concerns an animal that may be brought as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus27:10 Another shall not be exchanged or substituted for it
Leviticus27:11 If it concerns any unclean animal that may not be brought as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus27:12 The priest shall assess it: whether good or bad
Leviticus27:13 But if it is to be redeemed
Leviticus27:14 If a person consecrates a house to the LORD
Leviticus27:15 And if the one who consecrates the house wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:16 If a person consecrates to the LORD any inherited landholding
Leviticus27:17 If the person consecrates the field as of the year of jubilee
Leviticus27:18 but if the field is consecrated after the jubilee
Leviticus27:19 And if the one who consecrates the field wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:20 but if the field is not redeemed
Leviticus27:21 But when the field is released in the jubilee
Leviticus27:22 If someone consecrates to the LORD a field that has been purchased
Leviticus27:23 the priest shall compute for it the proportionate assessment up to the year of jubilee
Leviticus27:24 In the year of jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom it was bought
Leviticus27:25 All assessments shall be by the sanctuary shekel: twenty gerahs shall make a shekel.
Leviticus27:26 A firstling of animals
Leviticus27:27 If it is an unclean animal
Leviticus27:28 Nothing that a person owns that has been devoted to destruction for the LORD
Leviticus27:29 No human beings who have been devoted to destruction can be ransomed; they shall be put to death.
Leviticus27:30 All tithes from the land
Leviticus27:31 If persons wish to redeem any of their tithes
Leviticus27:32 All tithes of herd and flock
Leviticus27:33 Let no one inquire whether it is good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments that the LORD gave to Moses for the people of Israel on Mount Sinai.
Numbers1:1 The LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers1:2 Take a census of the whole congregation of Israelites
Numbers1:3 from twenty years old and upward
Numbers1:4 A man from each tribe shall be with you
Numbers1:5 These are the names of the men who shall assist you: From Reuben
Numbers1:6 From Simeon
Numbers1:7 From Judah
Numbers1:8 From Issachar
Numbers1:9 From Zebulun
Numbers1:10 From the sons of Joseph: from Ephraim
Numbers1:11 From Benjamin
Numbers1:12 From Dan
Numbers1:13 From Asher
Numbers1:14 From Gad
Numbers1:15 From Naphtali
Numbers1:16 These were the ones chosen from the congregation
Numbers1:17 Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name
Numbers1:18 and on the first day of the second month they assembled the whole congregation together. They registered themselves in their clans
Numbers1:19 as the LORD commanded Moses. So he enrolled them in the wilderness of Sinai.
Numbers1:20 The descendants of Reuben
Numbers1:21 those enrolled of the tribe of Reuben were forty-six thousand five hundred.
Numbers1:22 The descendants of Simeon
Numbers1:23 those enrolled of the tribe of Simeon were fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
Numbers1:24 The descendants of Gad
Numbers1:25 those enrolled of the tribe of Gad were forty-five thousand six hundred fifty.
Numbers1:26 The descendants of Judah
Numbers1:27 those enrolled of the tribe of Judah were seventy-four thousand six hundred.
Numbers1:28 The descendants of Issachar
Numbers1:29 those enrolled of the tribe of Issachar were fifty-four thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:30 The descendants of Zebulun
Numbers1:31 those enrolled of the tribe of Zebulun were fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:32 The descendants of Joseph
Numbers1:33 those enrolled of the tribe of Ephraim were forty thousand five hundred.
Numbers1:34 The descendants of Manasseh
Numbers1:35 those enrolled of the tribe of Manasseh were thirty-two thousand two hundred.
Numbers1:36 The descendants of Benjamin
Numbers1:37 those enrolled of the tribe of Benjamin were thirty-five thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:38 The descendants of Dan
Numbers1:39 those enrolled of the tribe of Dan were sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
Numbers1:40 The descendants of Asher
Numbers1:41 those enrolled of the tribe of Asher were forty-one thousand five hundred.
Numbers1:42 The descendants of Naphtali
Numbers1:43 those enrolled of the tribe of Naphtali were fifty-three thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:44 These are those who were enrolled
Numbers1:45 So the whole number of the Israelites
Numbers1:46 their whole number was six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty.
Numbers1:47 The Levites
Numbers1:48 The LORD had said to Moses:
Numbers1:49 Only the tribe of Levi you shall not enroll
Numbers1:50 Rather you shall appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the covenant
Numbers1:51 When the tabernacle is to set out
Numbers1:52 The other Israelites shall camp in their respective regimental camps
Numbers1:53 but the Levites shall camp around the tabernacle of the covenant
Numbers1:54 The Israelites did so; they did just as the LORD commanded Moses.
Numbers2:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers2:2 The Israelites shall camp each in their respective regiments
Numbers2:3 Those to camp on the east side toward the sunrise shall be of the regimental encampment of Judah by companies. The leader of the people of Judah shall be Nahshon son of Amminadab
Numbers2:4 with a company as enrolled of seventy-four thousand six hundred.
Numbers2:5 Those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Issachar. The leader of the Issacharites shall be Nethanel son of Zuar
Numbers2:6 with a company as enrolled of fifty-four thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:7 Then the tribe of Zebulun: The leader of the Zebulunites shall be Eliab son of Helon
Numbers2:8 with a company as enrolled of fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:9 The total enrollment of the camp of Judah
Numbers2:10 On the south side shall be the regimental encampment of Reuben by companies. The leader of the Reubenites shall be Elizur son of Shedeur
Numbers2:11 with a company as enrolled of forty-six thousand five hundred.
Numbers2:12 And those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Simeon. The leader of the Simeonites shall be Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai
Numbers2:13 with a company as enrolled of fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
Numbers2:14 Then the tribe of Gad: The leader of the Gadites shall be Eliasaph son of Reuel
Numbers2:15 with a company as enrolled of forty-five thousand six hundred fifty.
Numbers2:16 The total enrollment of the camp of Reuben
Numbers2:17 The tent of meeting
Numbers2:18 On the west side shall be the regimental encampment of Ephraim by companies. The leader of the people of Ephraim shall be Elishama son of Ammihud
Numbers2:19 with a company as enrolled of forty thousand five hundred.
Numbers2:20 Next to him shall be the tribe of Manasseh. The leader of the people of Manasseh shall be Gamaliel son of Pedahzur
Numbers2:21 with a company as enrolled of thirty-two thousand two hundred.
Numbers2:22 Then the tribe of Benjamin: The leader of the Benjaminites shall be Abidan son of Gideoni
Numbers2:23 with a company as enrolled of thirty-five thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:24 The total enrollment of the camp of Ephraim
Numbers2:25 On the north side shall be the regimental encampment of Dan by companies. The leader of the Danites shall be Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai
Numbers2:26 with a company as enrolled of sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
Numbers2:27 Those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Asher. The leader of the Asherites shall be Pagiel son of Ochran
Numbers2:28 with a company as enrolled of forty-one thousand five hundred.
Numbers2:29 Then the tribe of Naphtali: The leader of the Naphtalites shall be Ahira son of Enan
Numbers2:30 with a company as enrolled of fifty-three thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:31 The total enrollment of the camp of Dan is one hundred fifty-seven thousand six hundred. They shall set out last
Numbers2:32 This was the enrollment of the Israelites by their ancestral houses; the total enrollment in the camps by their companies was six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty.
Numbers2:33 Just as the LORD had commanded Moses
Numbers2:34 The Israelites did just as the LORD had commanded Moses: They camped by regiments
Numbers3:1 This is the lineage of Aaron and Moses at the time when the LORD spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn
Numbers3:3 these are the names of the sons of Aaron
Numbers3:4 Nadab and Abihu died before the LORD when they offered illicit fire before the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:5 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers3:6 Bring the tribe of Levi near
Numbers3:7 They shall perform duties for him and for the whole congregation in front of the tent of meeting
Numbers3:8 they shall be in charge of all the furnishings of the tent of meeting
Numbers3:9 You shall give the Levites to Aaron and his descendants; they are unreservedly given to him from among the Israelites.
Numbers3:10 But you shall make a register of Aaron and his descendants; it is they who shall attend to the priesthood
Numbers3:11 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers3:12 I hereby accept the Levites from among the Israelites as substitutes for all the firstborn that open the womb among the Israelites. The Levites shall be mine
Numbers3:13 for all the firstborn are mine; when I killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 Then the LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:15 Enroll the Levites by ancestral houses and by clans. You shall enroll every male from a month old and upward.
Numbers3:16 So Moses enrolled them according to the word of the LORD
Numbers3:17 The following were the sons of Levi
Numbers3:18 These are the names of the sons of Gershon by their clans: Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:19 The sons of Kohath by their clans: Amram
Numbers3:20 The sons of Merari by their clans: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites
Numbers3:21 To Gershon belonged the clan of the Libnites and the clan of the Shimeites; these were the clans of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:22 Their enrollment
Numbers3:23 The clans of the Gershonites were to camp behind the tabernacle on the west
Numbers3:24 with Eliasaph son of Lael as head of the ancestral house of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:25 The responsibility of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting was to be the tabernacle
Numbers3:26 the hangings of the court
Numbers3:27 To Kohath belonged the clan of the Amramites
Numbers3:28 Counting all the males
Numbers3:29 The clans of the Kohathites were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle
Numbers3:30 with Elizaphan son of Uzziel as head of the ancestral house of the clans of the Kohathites.
Numbers3:31 Their responsibility was to be the ark
Numbers3:32 Eleazar son of Aaron the priest was to be chief over the leaders of the Levites
Numbers3:33 To Merari belonged the clan of the Mahlites and the clan of the Mushites: these are the clans of Merari.
Numbers3:34 Their enrollment
Numbers3:35 The head of the ancestral house of the clans of Merari was Zuriel son of Abihail; they were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 The responsibility assigned to the sons of Merari was to be the frames of the tabernacle
Numbers3:37 also the pillars of the court all around
Numbers3:38 Those who were to camp in front of the tabernacle on the east-- in front of the tent of meeting toward the east-- were Moses and Aaron and Aaron's sons
Numbers3:39 The total enrollment of the Levites whom Moses and Aaron enrolled at the commandment of the LORD
Numbers3:40 Then the LORD said to Moses: Enroll all the firstborn males of the Israelites
Numbers3:41 But you shall accept the Levites for me-- I am the LORD-- as substitutes for all the firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:42 So Moses enrolled all the firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:43 The total enrollment
Numbers3:44 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers3:45 Accept the Levites as substitutes for all the firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:46 As the price of redemption of the two hundred seventy-three of the firstborn of the Israelites
Numbers3:47 you shall accept five shekels apiece
Numbers3:48 Give to Aaron and his sons the money by which the excess number of them is redeemed.
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the redemption money from those who were over and above those redeemed by the Levites;
Numbers3:50 from the firstborn of the Israelites he took the money
Numbers3:51 and Moses gave the redemption money to Aaron and his sons
Numbers4:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:2 Take a census of the Kohathites separate from the other Levites
Numbers4:3 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:4 The service of the Kohathites relating to the tent of meeting concerns the most holy things.
Numbers4:5 When the camp is to set out
Numbers4:6 then they shall put on it a covering of fine leather
Numbers4:7 Over the table of the bread of the Presence they shall spread a blue cloth
Numbers4:8 then they shall spread over them a crimson cloth
Numbers4:9 They shall take a blue cloth
Numbers4:10 and they shall put it with all its utensils in a covering of fine leather
Numbers4:11 Over the golden altar they shall spread a blue cloth
Numbers4:12 and they shall take all the utensils of the service that are used in the sanctuary
Numbers4:13 They shall take away the ashes from the altar
Numbers4:14 and they shall put on it all the utensils of the altar
Numbers4:15 When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the furnishings of the sanctuary
Numbers4:16 Eleazar son of Aaron the priest shall have charge of the oil for the light
Numbers4:17 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:18 You must not let the tribe of the clans of the Kohathites be destroyed from among the Levites.
Numbers4:19 This is how you must deal with them in order that they may live and not die when they come near to the most holy things: Aaron and his sons shall go in and assign each to a particular task or burden.
Numbers4:20 But the Kohathites must not go in to look on the holy things even for a moment; otherwise they will die.
Numbers4:21 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers4:22 Take a census of the Gershonites also
Numbers4:23 from thirty years old up to fifty years old you shall enroll them
Numbers4:24 This is the service of the clans of the Gershonites
Numbers4:25 They shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle
Numbers4:26 and the hangings of the court
Numbers4:27 All the service of the Gershonites shall be at the command of Aaron and his sons
Numbers4:28 This is the service of the clans of the Gershonites relating to the tent of meeting
Numbers4:29 As for the Merarites
Numbers4:30 from thirty years old up to fifty years old you shall enroll them
Numbers4:31 This is what they are charged to carry
Numbers4:32 and the pillars of the court all around with their bases
Numbers4:33 This is the service of the clans of the Merarites
Numbers4:34 So Moses and Aaron and the leaders of the congregation enrolled the Kohathites
Numbers4:35 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:36 and their enrollment by clans was two thousand seven hundred fifty.
Numbers4:37 This was the enrollment of the clans of the Kohathites
Numbers4:38 The enrollment of the Gershonites
Numbers4:39 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:40 their enrollment by their clans and their ancestral houses was two thousand six hundred thirty.
Numbers4:41 This was the enrollment of the clans of the Gershonites
Numbers4:42 The enrollment of the clans of the Merarites
Numbers4:43 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:44 their enrollment by their clans was three thousand two hundred.
Numbers4:45 This is the enrollment of the clans of the Merarites
Numbers4:46 All those who were enrolled of the Levites
Numbers4:47 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:48 their enrollment was eight thousand five hundred eighty.
Numbers4:49 According to the commandment of the LORD through Moses they were appointed to their several tasks of serving or carrying; thus they were enrolled by him
Numbers5:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers5:2 Command the Israelites to put out of the camp everyone who is leprous
Numbers5:3 you shall put out both male and female
Numbers5:4 The Israelites did so
Numbers5:5 The LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers5:6 Speak to the Israelites: When a man or a woman wrongs another
Numbers5:7 and shall confess the sin that has been committed. The person shall make full restitution for the wrong
Numbers5:8 If the injured party has no next of kin to whom restitution may be made for the wrong
Numbers5:9 Among all the sacred donations of the Israelites
Numbers5:10 The sacred donations of all are their own; whatever anyone gives to the priest shall be his.
Numbers5:11 The LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers5:12 Speak to the Israelites and say to them: If any man's wife goes astray and is unfaithful to him
Numbers5:13 if a man has had intercourse with her but it is hidden from her husband
Numbers5:14 if a spirit of jealousy comes on him
Numbers5:15 then the man shall bring his wife to the priest. And he shall bring the offering required for her
Numbers5:16 Then the priest shall bring her near
Numbers5:17 the priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel
Numbers5:18 The priest shall set the woman before the LORD
Numbers5:19 Then the priest shall make her take an oath
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray while under your husband's authority
Numbers5:21 -- let the priest make the woman take the oath of the curse and say to the woman-- the LORD make you an execration and an oath among your people
Numbers5:22 now may this water that brings the curse enter your bowels and make your womb discharge
Numbers5:23 Then the priest shall put these curses in writing
Numbers5:24 He shall make the woman drink the water of bitterness that brings the curse
Numbers5:25 The priest shall take the grain offering of jealousy out of the woman's hand
Numbers5:26 and the priest shall take a handful of the grain offering
Numbers5:27 When he has made her drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean
Numbers5:29 This is the law in cases of jealousy
Numbers5:30 or when a spirit of jealousy comes on a man and he is jealous of his wife; then he shall set the woman before the LORD
Numbers5:31 The man shall be free from iniquity
Numbers6:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers6:2 Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When either men or women make a special vow
Numbers6:3 they shall separate themselves from wine and strong drink; they shall drink no wine vinegar or other vinegar
Numbers6:4 All their days as nazirites they shall eat nothing that is produced by the grapevine
Numbers6:5 All the days of their nazirite vow no razor shall come upon the head; until the time is completed for which they separate themselves to the LORD
Numbers6:6 All the days that they separate themselves to the LORD they shall not go near a corpse.
Numbers6:7 Even if their father or mother
Numbers6:8 All their days as nazirites they are holy to the LORD.
Numbers6:9 If someone dies very suddenly nearby
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day they shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting
Numbers6:11 and the priest shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering
Numbers6:12 and separate themselves to the LORD for their days as nazirites
Numbers6:13 This is the law for the nazirites when the time of their consecration has been completed: they shall be brought to the entrance of the tent of meeting
Numbers6:14 and they shall offer their gift to the LORD
Numbers6:15 and a basket of unleavened bread
Numbers6:16 The priest shall present them before the LORD and offer their sin offering and burnt offering
Numbers6:17 and shall offer the ram as a sacrifice of well-being to the LORD
Numbers6:18 Then the nazirites shall shave the consecrated head at the entrance of the tent of meeting
Numbers6:19 The priest shall take the shoulder of the ram
Numbers6:20 Then the priest shall elevate them as an elevation offering before the LORD; they are a holy portion for the priest
Numbers6:21 This is the law for the nazirites who take a vow. Their offering to the LORD must be in accordance with the nazirite vow
Numbers6:22 The LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers6:23 Speak to Aaron and his sons
Numbers6:24 The LORD bless you and keep you;
Numbers6:25 the LORD make his face to shine upon you
Numbers6:26 the LORD lift up his countenance upon you
Numbers6:27 So they shall put my name on the Israelites

Numbers8:1 The LORD spoke to Moses

Numbers8:16 For they are unreservedly given to me from among the Israelites; I have taken them for myself
Numbers8:17 For all the firstborn among the Israelites are mine
Numbers8:18 but I have taken the Levites in place of all the firstborn among the Israelites.
Numbers8:19 Moreover

Numbers8:25 and from the age of fifty years they shall retire from the duty of the service and serve no more.
Numbers8:26 They may assist their brothers in the tent of meeting in carrying out their duties

Numbers11:6 but now our strength is dried up

Numbers15:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers15:2 Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you come into the land you are to inhabit
Numbers15:3 and you make an offering by fire to the LORD from the herd or from the flock-- whether a burnt offering or a sacrifice
Numbers15:4 then whoever presents such an offering to the LORD shall present also a grain offering
Numbers15:5 Moreover
Numbers15:6 For a ram
Numbers15:7 and as a drink offering you shall offer one-third of a hin of wine
Numbers15:8 When you offer a bull as a burnt offering or a sacrifice
Numbers15:9 then you shall present with the bull a grain offering
Numbers15:10 and you shall present as a drink offering half a hin of wine
Numbers15:11 Thus it shall be done for each ox or ram
Numbers15:12 According to the number that you offer
Numbers15:13 Every native Israelite shall do these things in this way
Numbers15:14 An alien who lives with you
Numbers15:15 As for the assembly
Numbers15:16 You and the alien who resides with you shall have the same law and the same ordinance.
Numbers15:17 The LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers15:18 Speak to the Israelites and say to them: After you come into the land to which I am bringing you
Numbers15:19 whenever you eat of the bread of the land
Numbers15:20 From your first batch of dough you shall present a loaf as a donation; you shall present it just as you present a donation from the threshing floor.
Numbers15:21 Throughout your generations you shall give to the LORD a donation from the first of your batch of dough.
Numbers15:22 But if you unintentionally fail to observe all these commandments that the LORD has spoken to Moses--
Numbers15:23 everything that the LORD has commanded you by Moses
Numbers15:24 then if it was done unintentionally without the knowledge of the congregation
Numbers15:25 The priest shall make atonement for all the congregation of the Israelites
Numbers15:26 All the congregation of the Israelites shall be forgiven
Numbers15:27 An individual who sins unintentionally shall present a female goat a year old for a sin offering.
Numbers15:28 And the priest shall make atonement before the LORD for the one who commits an error
Numbers15:29 For both the native among the Israelites and the alien residing among them-- you shall have the same law for anyone who acts in error.
Numbers15:30 But whoever acts high-handedly
Numbers15:31 Because of having despised the word of the LORD and broken his commandment

Numbers15:38 Speak to the Israelites
Numbers15:39 You have the fringe so that
Numbers15:40 So you shall remember and do all my commandments
Numbers15:41 I am the LORD your God
Numbers16:1 Now Korah son of Izhar son of Kohath son of Levi

Numbers16:5 Then he said to Korah and all his company

Numbers16:7 and tomorrow put fire in them
Numbers16:8 Then Moses said to Korah

Numbers16:10 He has allowed you to approach him
Numbers16:11 Therefore you and all your company have gathered together against the LORD. What is Aaron that you rail against him?
Numbers16:12 Moses sent for Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab; but they said
Numbers16:13 Is it too little that you have brought us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey to kill us in the wilderness
Numbers16:14 It is clear you have not brought us into a land flowing with milk and honey
Numbers16:15 Moses was very angry and said to the LORD
Numbers16:16 And Moses said to Korah
Numbers16:17 and let each one of you take his censer

Numbers16:34 All Israel around them fled at their outcry

Numbers16:38 For the censers of these sinners have become holy at the cost of their lives. Make them into hammered plates as a covering for the altar

Numbers18:1 The LORD said to Aaron: You and your sons and your ancestral house with you shall bear responsibility for offenses connected with the sanctuary
Numbers18:2 So bring with you also your brothers of the tribe of Levi
Numbers18:3 They shall perform duties for you and for the whole tent. But they must not approach either the utensils of the sanctuary or the altar
Numbers18:4 They are attached to you in order to perform the duties of the tent of meeting
Numbers18:5 You yourselves shall perform the duties of the sanctuary and the duties of the altar
Numbers18:6 It is I who now take your brother Levites from among the Israelites; they are now yours as a gift
Numbers18:7 But you and your sons with you shall diligently perform your priestly duties in all that concerns the altar and the area behind the curtain. I give your priesthood as a gift; any outsider who approaches shall be put to death.
Numbers18:8 The LORD spoke to Aaron: I have given you charge of the offerings made to me
Numbers18:9 This shall be yours from the most holy things
Numbers18:10 As a most holy thing you shall eat it; every male may eat it; it shall be holy to you.
Numbers18:11 This also is yours: I have given to you
Numbers18:12 All the best of the oil and all the best of the wine and of the grain

Numbers18:22 From now on the Israelites shall no longer approach the tent of meeting
Numbers18:23 But the Levites shall perform the service of the tent of meeting
Numbers18:24 because I have given to the Levites as their portion the tithe of the Israelites

Numbers19:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 This is a statute of the law that the LORD has commanded: Tell the Israelites to bring you a red heifer without defect
Numbers19:3 You shall give it to the priest Eleazar
Numbers19:4 The priest Eleazar shall take some of its blood with his finger and sprinkle it seven times towards the front of the tent of meeting.
Numbers19:5 Then the heifer shall be burned in his sight; its skin
Numbers19:6 The priest shall take cedarwood
Numbers19:7 Then the priest shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Numbers19:8 The one who burns the heifer shall wash his clothes in water and bathe his body in water; he shall remain unclean until evening.
Numbers19:9 Then someone who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer
Numbers19:10 The one who gathers the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening. This shall be a perpetual statute for the Israelites and for the alien residing among them.
Numbers19:11 Those who touch the dead body of any human being shall be unclean seven days.
Numbers19:12 They shall purify themselves with the water on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:13 All who touch a corpse
Numbers19:14 This is the law when someone dies in a tent: everyone who comes into the tent
Numbers19:15 And every open vessel with no cover fastened on it is unclean.
Numbers19:16 Whoever in the open field touches one who has been killed by a sword
Numbers19:17 For the unclean they shall take some ashes of the burnt purification offering
Numbers19:18 then a clean person shall take hyssop
Numbers19:19 The clean person shall sprinkle the unclean ones on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:20 Any who are unclean but do not purify themselves
Numbers19:21 It shall be a perpetual statute for them. The one who sprinkles the water for cleansing shall wash his clothes
Numbers19:22 Whatever the unclean person touches shall be unclean

Numbers31:39 The donkeys were thirty thousand five hundred
Numbers31:40 The persons were sixteen thousand
Numbers31:41 Moses gave the tribute

Numbers35:1 In the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho

Numbers35:10 Speak to the Israelites

Numbers35:12 The cities shall be for you a refuge from the avenger
Numbers35:13 The cities that you designate shall be six cities of refuge for you:
Numbers35:14 you shall designate three cities beyond the Jordan
Numbers35:15 These six cities shall serve as refuge for the Israelites
Numbers35:16 But anyone who strikes another with an iron object
Numbers35:17 Or anyone who strikes another with a stone in hand that could cause death
Numbers35:18 Or anyone who strikes another with a weapon of wood in hand that could cause death
Numbers35:19 The avenger of blood is the one who shall put the murderer to death; when they meet
Numbers35:20 Likewise
Numbers35:21 or in enmity strikes another with the hand
Numbers35:22 But if someone pushes another suddenly without enmity
Numbers35:23 or
Numbers35:24 then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the avenger of blood
Numbers35:25 and the congregation shall rescue the slayer from the avenger of blood. Then the congregation shall send the slayer back to the original city of refuge. The slayer shall live in it until the death of the high priest who was anointed with the holy oil.
Numbers35:26 But if the slayer shall at any time go outside the bounds of the original city of refuge
Numbers35:27 and is found by the avenger of blood outside the bounds of the city of refuge
Numbers35:28 For the slayer must remain in the city of refuge until the death of the high priest; but after the death of the high priest the slayer may return home.
Numbers35:29 These things shall be a statute and ordinance for you throughout your generations wherever you live.
Numbers35:30 If anyone kills another
Numbers35:31 Moreover you shall accept no ransom for the life of a murderer who is subject to the death penalty; a murderer must be put to death.
Numbers35:32 Nor shall you accept ransom for one who has fled to a city of refuge
Numbers35:33 You shall not pollute the land in which you live; for blood pollutes the land
Numbers35:34 You shall not defile the land in which you live

Deuteronomy34:11 He was unequaled for all the signs and wonders that the LORD sent him to perform in the land of Egypt

Judges21:24 So the Israelites departed from there at that time by tribes and families

1Samuel1:2 He had two wives; the name of the one was Hannah

1Samuel1:5 but to Hannah he gave a double portion
1Samuel1:6 Her rival used to provoke her severely

2Samuel22:1 David spoke to the LORD the words of this song on the day when the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies
2Samuel22:2 He said: The LORD is my rock
2Samuel22:3 my God
2Samuel22:4 I call upon the LORD
2Samuel22:5 For the waves of death encompassed me
2Samuel22:6 the cords of Sheol entangled me
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called upon the LORD; to my God I called. From his temple he heard my voice
2Samuel22:8 Then the earth reeled and rocked; the foundations of the heavens trembled and quaked
2Samuel22:9 Smoke went up from his nostrils
2Samuel22:10 He bowed the heavens
2Samuel22:11 He rode on a cherub
2Samuel22:12 He made darkness around him a canopy
2Samuel22:13 Out of the brightness before him coals of fire flamed forth.
2Samuel22:14 The LORD thundered from heaven; the Most High uttered his voice.
2Samuel22:15 He sent out arrows
2Samuel22:16 Then the channels of the sea were seen
2Samuel22:17 He reached from on high
2Samuel22:18 He delivered me from my strong enemy
2Samuel22:19 They came upon me in the day of my calamity
2Samuel22:20 He brought me out into a broad place; he delivered me
2Samuel22:21 The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands he recompensed me.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the LORD
2Samuel22:23 For all his ordinances were before me
2Samuel22:24 I was blameless before him
2Samuel22:25 Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 With the loyal you show yourself loyal; with the blameless you show yourself blameless;
2Samuel22:27 with the pure you show yourself pure
2Samuel22:28 You deliver a humble people
2Samuel22:29 Indeed
2Samuel22:30 By you I can crush a troop
2Samuel22:31 This God-- his way is perfect; the promise of the LORD proves true; he is a shield for all who take refuge in him.
2Samuel22:32 For who is God
2Samuel22:33 The God who has girded me with strength has opened wide my path.
2Samuel22:34 He made my feet like the feet of deer
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hands for war
2Samuel22:36 You have given me the shield of your salvation
2Samuel22:37 You have made me stride freely
2Samuel22:38 I pursued my enemies and destroyed them
2Samuel22:39 I consumed them; I struck them down
2Samuel22:40 For you girded me with strength for the battle; you made my assailants sink under me.
2Samuel22:41 You made my enemies turn their backs to me
2Samuel22:42 They looked
2Samuel22:43 I beat them fine like the dust of the earth
2Samuel22:44 You delivered me from strife with the peoples; you kept me as the head of the nations; people whom I had not known served me.
2Samuel22:45 Foreigners came cringing to me; as soon as they heard of me
2Samuel22:46 Foreigners lost heart
2Samuel22:47 The LORD lives! Blessed be my rock
2Samuel22:48 the God who gave me vengeance and brought down peoples under me
2Samuel22:49 who brought me out from my enemies; you exalted me above my adversaries
2Samuel22:50 For this I will extol you
2Samuel22:51 He is a tower of salvation for his king

1Kings2:6 Act therefore according to your wisdom
1Kings2:7 Deal loyally
1Kings2:8 There is also with you Shimei son of Gera
1Kings2:9 Therefore do not hold him guiltless

1Kings2:11 The time that David reigned over Israel was forty years; he reigned seven years in Hebron
1Kings2:12 So Solomon sat on the throne of his father David; and his kingdom was firmly established.

1Kings2:29 When it was told King Solomon

1Kings2:46 Then the king commanded Benaiah son of Jehoiada; and he went out and struck him down

1Kings3:3 Solomon loved the LORD

1Kings3:5 At Gibeon the LORD appeared to Solomon in a dream by night; and God said
1Kings3:6 And Solomon said
1Kings3:7 And now

1Kings3:9 Give your servant therefore an understanding mind to govern your people
1Kings3:10 It pleased the Lord that Solomon had asked this.
1Kings3:11 God said to him

1Kings3:13 I give you also what you have not asked
1Kings3:14 If you will walk in my ways
1Kings3:15 Then Solomon awoke; it had been a dream. He came to Jerusalem where he stood before the ark of the covenant of the LORD. He offered up burnt offerings and offerings of well-being

1Kings4:1 King Solomon was king over all Israel
1Kings4:2 and these were his high officials: Azariah son of Zadok was the priest;
1Kings4:3 Elihoreph and Ahijah sons of Shisha were secretaries; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder;
1Kings4:4 Benaiah son of Jehoiada was in command of the army; Zadok and Abiathar were priests;
1Kings4:5 Azariah son of Nathan was over the officials; Zabud son of Nathan was priest and king's friend;
1Kings4:6 Ahishar was in charge of the palace; and Adoniram son of Abda was in charge of the forced labor.

1Kings4:18 Shimei son of Ela
1Kings4:19 Geber son of Uri
1Kings4:20 Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sand by the sea; they ate and drank and were happy.
1Kings4:21 Solomon was sovereign over all the kingdoms from the Euphrates to the land of the Philistines
1Kings4:22 Solomon's provision for one day was thirty cors of choice flour
1Kings4:23 ten fat oxen
1Kings4:24 For he had dominion over all the region west of the Euphrates from Tiphsah to Gaza
1Kings4:25 During Solomon's lifetime Judah and Israel lived in safety
1Kings4:26 Solomon also had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots
1Kings4:27 Those officials supplied provisions for King Solomon and for all who came to King Solomon's table
1Kings4:28 They also brought to the required place barley and straw for the horses and swift steeds
1Kings4:29 God gave Solomon very great wisdom
1Kings4:30 so that Solomon's wisdom surpassed the wisdom of all the people of the east
1Kings4:31 He was wiser than anyone else
1Kings4:32 He composed three thousand proverbs
1Kings4:33 He would speak of trees
1Kings4:34 People came from all the nations to hear the wisdom of Solomon; they came from all the kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom.
1Kings5:1 Now King Hiram of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon
1Kings5:2 Solomon sent word to Hiram
1Kings5:3 You know that my father David could not build a house for the name of the LORD his God because of the warfare with which his enemies surrounded him
1Kings5:4 But now the LORD my God has given me rest on every side; there is neither adversary nor misfortune.
1Kings5:5 So I intend to build a house for the name of the LORD my God
1Kings5:6 Therefore command that cedars from the Lebanon be cut for me. My servants will join your servants
1Kings5:7 When Hiram heard the words of Solomon
1Kings5:8 Hiram sent word to Solomon
1Kings5:9 My servants shall bring it down to the sea from the Lebanon; I will make it into rafts to go by sea to the place you indicate. I will have them broken up there for you to take away. And you shall meet my needs by providing food for my household.
1Kings5:10 So Hiram supplied Solomon's every need for timber of cedar and cypress.
1Kings5:11 Solomon in turn gave Hiram twenty thousand cors of wheat as food for his household
1Kings5:12 So the LORD gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings5:13 King Solomon conscripted forced labor out of all Israel; the levy numbered thirty thousand men.
1Kings5:14 He sent them to the Lebanon
1Kings5:15 Solomon also had seventy thousand laborers and eighty thousand stonecutters in the hill country
1Kings5:16 besides Solomon's three thousand three hundred supervisors who were over the work
1Kings5:17 At the king's command
1Kings5:18 So Solomon's builders and Hiram's builders and the Gebalites did the stonecutting and prepared the timber and the stone to build the house.
1Kings6:1 In the four hundred eightieth year after the Israelites came out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 The house that King Solomon built for the LORD was sixty cubits long
1Kings6:3 The vestibule in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits wide
1Kings6:4 For the house he made windows with recessed frames.
1Kings6:5 He also built a structure against the wall of the house
1Kings6:6 The lowest story was five cubits wide
1Kings6:7 The house was built with stone finished at the quarry
1Kings6:8 The entrance for the middle story was on the south side of the house: one went up by winding stairs to the middle story
1Kings6:9 So he built the house
1Kings6:10 He built the structure against the whole house
1Kings6:11 Now the word of the LORD came to Solomon
1Kings6:12 Concerning this house that you are building
1Kings6:13 I will dwell among the children of Israel
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the house
1Kings6:15 He lined the walls of the house on the inside with boards of cedar; from the floor of the house to the rafters of the ceiling
1Kings6:16 He built twenty cubits of the rear of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the rafters
1Kings6:17 The house
1Kings6:18 The cedar within the house had carvings of gourds and open flowers; all was cedar
1Kings6:19 The inner sanctuary he prepared in the innermost part of the house
1Kings6:20 The interior of the inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:21 Solomon overlaid the inside of the house with pure gold
1Kings6:22 Next he overlaid the whole house with gold
1Kings6:23 In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olivewood
1Kings6:24 Five cubits was the length of one wing of the cherub
1Kings6:25 The other cherub also measured ten cubits; both cherubim had the same measure and the same form.
1Kings6:26 The height of one cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 He put the cherubim in the innermost part of the house; the wings of the cherubim were spread out so that a wing of one was touching the one wall
1Kings6:28 He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 He carved the walls of the house all around about with carved engravings of cherubim
1Kings6:30 The floor of the house he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:31 For the entrance to the inner sanctuary he made doors of olivewood; the lintel and the doorposts were five-sided.
1Kings6:32 He covered the two doors of olivewood with carvings of cherubim
1Kings6:33 So also he made for the entrance to the nave doorposts of olivewood
1Kings6:34 and two doors of cypress wood; the two leaves of the one door were folding
1Kings6:35 He carved cherubim
1Kings6:36 He built the inner court with three courses of dressed stone to one course of cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the LORD was laid
1Kings6:38 In the eleventh year
1Kings7:1 Solomon was building his own house thirteen years
1Kings7:2 He built the House of the Forest of the Lebanon one hundred cubits long
1Kings7:3 It was roofed with cedar on the forty-five rafters
1Kings7:4 There were window frames in the three rows
1Kings7:5 All the doorways and doorposts had four-sided frames
1Kings7:6 He made the Hall of Pillars fifty cubits long and thirty cubits wide. There was a porch in front with pillars
1Kings7:7 He made the Hall of the Throne where he was to pronounce judgment
1Kings7:8 His own house where he would reside
1Kings7:9 All these were made of costly stones
1Kings7:10 The foundation was of costly stones
1Kings7:11 There were costly stones above
1Kings7:12 The great court had three courses of dressed stone to one layer of cedar beams all around; so had the inner court of the house of the LORD
1Kings7:13 Now King Solomon invited and received Hiram from Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 He cast two pillars of bronze. Eighteen cubits was the height of the one
1Kings7:16 He also made two capitals of molten bronze
1Kings7:17 There were nets of checker work with wreaths of chain work for the capitals on the tops of the pillars; seven for the one capital
1Kings7:18 He made the columns with two rows around each latticework to cover the capitals that were above the pomegranates; he did the same with the other capital.
1Kings7:19 Now the capitals that were on the tops of the pillars in the vestibule were of lily-work
1Kings7:20 The capitals were on the two pillars and also above the rounded projection that was beside the latticework; there were two hundred pomegranates in rows all around; and so with the other capital.
1Kings7:21 He set up the pillars at the vestibule of the temple; he set up the pillar on the south and called it Jachin; and he set up the pillar on the north and called it Boaz.
1Kings7:22 On the tops of the pillars was lily-work. Thus the work of the pillars was finished.
1Kings7:23 Then he made the molten sea; it was round
1Kings7:24 Under its brim were panels all around it
1Kings7:25 It stood on twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 Its thickness was a handbreadth; its brim was made like the brim of a cup
1Kings7:27 He also made the ten stands of bronze; each stand was four cubits long
1Kings7:28 This was the construction of the stands: they had borders; the borders were within the frames;
1Kings7:29 on the borders that were set in the frames were lions
1Kings7:30 Each stand had four bronze wheels and axles of bronze; at the four corners were supports for a basin. The supports were cast with wreaths at the side of each.
1Kings7:31 Its opening was within the crown whose height was one cubit; its opening was round
1Kings7:32 The four wheels were underneath the borders; the axles of the wheels were in the stands; and the height of a wheel was a cubit and a half.
1Kings7:33 The wheels were made like a chariot wheel; their axles
1Kings7:34 There were four supports at the four corners of each stand; the supports were of one piece with the stands.
1Kings7:35 On the top of the stand there was a round band half a cubit high; on the top of the stand
1Kings7:36 On the surfaces of its stays and on its borders he carved cherubim
1Kings7:37 In this way he made the ten stands; all of them were cast alike
1Kings7:38 He made ten basins of bronze; each basin held forty baths
1Kings7:39 He set five of the stands on the south side of the house
1Kings7:40 Hiram also made the pots
1Kings7:41 the two pillars
1Kings7:42 the four hundred pomegranates for the two latticeworks
1Kings7:43 the ten stands
1Kings7:44 the one sea
1Kings7:45 The pots
1Kings7:46 In the plain of the Jordan the king cast them
1Kings7:47 Solomon left all the vessels unweighed
1Kings7:48 So Solomon made all the vessels that were in the house of the LORD: the golden altar
1Kings7:49 the lampstands of pure gold
1Kings7:50 the cups
1Kings7:51 Thus all the work that King Solomon did on the house of the LORD was finished. Solomon brought in the things that his father David had dedicated
1Kings8:1 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes
1Kings8:2 All the people of Israel assembled to King Solomon at the festival in the month Ethanim
1Kings8:3 And all the elders of Israel came
1Kings8:4 So they brought up the ark of the LORD
1Kings8:5 King Solomon and all the congregation of Israel
1Kings8:6 Then the priests brought the ark of the covenant of the LORD to its place
1Kings8:7 For the cherubim spread out their wings over the place of the ark
1Kings8:8 The poles were so long that the ends of the poles were seen from the holy place in front of the inner sanctuary; but they could not be seen from outside; they are there to this day.
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets of stone that Moses had placed there at Horeb
1Kings8:10 And when the priests came out of the holy place
1Kings8:11 so that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud; for the glory of the LORD filled the house of the LORD.
1Kings8:12 Then Solomon said
1Kings8:13 I have built you an exalted house
1Kings8:14 Then the king turned around and blessed all the assembly of Israel
1Kings8:15 He said
1Kings8:16 'Since the day that I brought my people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 My father David had it in mind to build a house for the name of the LORD
1Kings8:18 But the LORD said to my father David
1Kings8:19 nevertheless you shall not build the house
1Kings8:20 Now the LORD has upheld the promise that he made; for I have risen in the place of my father David; I sit on the throne of Israel
1Kings8:21 There I have provided a place for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in the presence of all the assembly of Israel
1Kings8:23 He said
1Kings8:24 the covenant that you kept for your servant my father David as you declared to him; you promised with your mouth and have this day fulfilled with your hand.
1Kings8:25 Therefore
1Kings8:26 Therefore
1Kings8:27 But will God indeed dwell on the earth? Even heaven and the highest heaven cannot contain you
1Kings8:28 Regard your servant's prayer and his plea
1Kings8:29 that your eyes may be open night and day toward this house
1Kings8:30 Hear the plea of your servant and of your people Israel when they pray toward this place; O hear in heaven your dwelling place; heed and forgive.
1Kings8:31 If someone sins against a neighbor and is given an oath to swear
1Kings8:32 then hear in heaven
1Kings8:33 When your people Israel
1Kings8:34 then hear in heaven
1Kings8:35 When heaven is shut up and there is no rain because they have sinned against you
1Kings8:36 then hear in heaven
1Kings8:37 If there is famine in the land
1Kings8:38 whatever prayer
1Kings8:39 then hear in heaven your dwelling place
1Kings8:40 so that they may fear you all the days that they live in the land that you gave to our ancestors.
1Kings8:41 Likewise when a foreigner
1Kings8:42 -- for they shall hear of your great name
1Kings8:43 then hear in heaven your dwelling place
1Kings8:44 If your people go out to battle against their enemy
1Kings8:45 then hear in heaven their prayer and their plea
1Kings8:46 If they sin against you-- for there is no one who does not sin-- and you are angry with them and give them to an enemy
1Kings8:47 yet if they come to their senses in the land to which they have been taken captive
1Kings8:48 if they repent with all their heart and soul in the land of their enemies
1Kings8:49 then hear in heaven your dwelling place their prayer and their plea
1Kings8:50 and forgive your people who have sinned against you
1Kings8:51 (for they are your people and heritage
1Kings8:52 Let your eyes be open to the plea of your servant
1Kings8:53 For you have separated them from among all the peoples of the earth
1Kings8:54 Now when Solomon finished offering all this prayer and this plea to the LORD
1Kings8:55 he stood and blessed all the assembly of Israel with a loud voice:
1Kings8:56 Blessed be the LORD
1Kings8:57 The LORD our God be with us
1Kings8:58 but incline our hearts to him
1Kings8:59 Let these words of mine
1Kings8:60 so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the LORD is God; there is no other.
1Kings8:61 Therefore devote yourselves completely to the LORD our God
1Kings8:62 Then the king
1Kings8:63 Solomon offered as sacrifices of well-being to the LORD twenty-two thousand oxen and one hundred twenty thousand sheep. So the king and all the people of Israel dedicated the house of the LORD.
1Kings8:64 The same day the king consecrated the middle of the court that was in front of the house of the LORD; for there he offered the burnt offerings and the grain offerings and the fat pieces of the sacrifices of well-being
1Kings8:65 So Solomon held the festival at that time
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away; and they blessed the king
