1Kings10:1 When the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon (fame due to the name of the LORD)
1Kings10:2 She came to Jerusalem with a very great retinue
1Kings10:3 Solomon answered all her questions; there was nothing hidden from the king that he could not explain to her.
1Kings10:4 When the queen of Sheba had observed all the wisdom of Solomon
1Kings10:5 the food of his table
1Kings10:6 So she said to the king
1Kings10:7 but I did not believe the reports until I came and my own eyes had seen it. Not even half had been told me; your wisdom and prosperity far surpass the report that I had heard.
1Kings10:8 Happy are your wives! Happy are these your servants
1Kings10:9 Blessed be the LORD your God
1Kings10:10 Then she gave the king one hundred twenty talents of gold
1Kings10:11 Moreover
1Kings10:12 From the almug wood the king made supports for the house of the LORD
1Kings10:13 Meanwhile King Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba every desire that she expressed
1Kings10:14 The weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred sixty-six talents of gold
1Kings10:15 besides that which came from the traders and from the business of the merchants
1Kings10:16 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of beaten gold; six hundred shekels of gold went into each large shield.
1Kings10:17 He made three hundred shields of beaten gold; three minas of gold went into each shield; and the king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.
1Kings10:18 The king also made a great ivory throne
1Kings10:19 The throne had six steps. The top of the throne was rounded in the back
1Kings10:20 while twelve lions were standing
1Kings10:21 All King Solomon's drinking vessels were of gold
1Kings10:22 For the king had a fleet of ships of Tarshish at sea with the fleet of Hiram. Once every three years the fleet of ships of Tarshish used to come bringing gold
1Kings10:23 Thus King Solomon excelled all the kings of the earth in riches and in wisdom.
1Kings10:24 The whole earth sought the presence of Solomon to hear his wisdom
1Kings10:25 Every one of them brought a present
1Kings10:26 Solomon gathered together chariots and horses; he had fourteen hundred chariots and twelve thousand horses
1Kings10:27 The king made silver as common in Jerusalem as stones
1Kings10:28 Solomon's import of horses was from Egypt and Kue
1Kings10:29 A chariot could be imported from Egypt for six hundred shekels of silver
1Kings11:1 King Solomon loved many foreign women along with the daughter of Pharaoh: Moabite
1Kings11:2 from the nations concerning which the LORD had said to the Israelites
1Kings11:3 Among his wives were seven hundred princesses and three hundred concubines; and his wives turned away his heart.
1Kings11:4 For when Solomon was old
1Kings11:5 For Solomon followed Astarte the goddess of the Sidonians
1Kings11:6 So Solomon did what was evil in the sight of the LORD
1Kings11:7 Then Solomon built a high place for Chemosh the abomination of Moab
1Kings11:8 He did the same for all his foreign wives
1Kings11:9 Then the LORD was angry with Solomon
1Kings11:10 and had commanded him concerning this matter
1Kings11:11 Therefore the LORD said to Solomon
1Kings11:12 Yet for the sake of your father David I will not do it in your lifetime; I will tear it out of the hand of your son.
1Kings11:13 I will not
1Kings11:14 Then the LORD raised up an adversary against Solomon
1Kings11:15 For when David was in Edom
1Kings11:16 (for Joab and all Israel remained there six months
1Kings11:17 but Hadad fled to Egypt with some Edomites who were servants of his father. He was a young boy at that time.
1Kings11:18 They set out from Midian and came to Paran; they took people with them from Paran and came to Egypt
1Kings11:19 Hadad found great favor in the sight of Pharaoh
1Kings11:20 The sister of Tahpenes gave birth by him to his son Genubath
1Kings11:21 When Hadad heard in Egypt that David slept with his ancestors and that Joab the commander of the army was dead
1Kings11:22 But Pharaoh said to him
1Kings11:23 God raised up another adversary against Solomon
1Kings11:24 He gathered followers around him and became leader of a marauding band
1Kings11:25 He was an adversary of Israel all the days of Solomon
1Kings11:26 Jeroboam son of Nebat
1Kings11:27 The following was the reason he rebelled against the king. Solomon built the Millo
1Kings11:28 The man Jeroboam was very able
1Kings11:29 About that time
1Kings11:30 when Ahijah laid hold of the new garment he was wearing and tore it into twelve pieces.
1Kings11:31 He then said to Jeroboam: Take for yourself ten pieces; for thus says the LORD
1Kings11:32 One tribe will remain his
1Kings11:33 This is because he has forsaken me
1Kings11:34 Nevertheless I will not take the whole kingdom away from him but will make him ruler all the days of his life
1Kings11:35 but I will take the kingdom away from his son and give it to you-- that is
1Kings11:36 Yet to his son I will give one tribe
1Kings11:37 I will take you
1Kings11:38 If you will listen to all that I command you
1Kings11:39 For this reason I will punish the descendants of David
1Kings11:40 Solomon sought therefore to kill Jeroboam; but Jeroboam promptly fled to Egypt
1Kings11:41 Now the rest of the acts of Solomon
1Kings11:42 The time that Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel was forty years.
1Kings11:43 Solomon slept with his ancestors and was buried in the city of his father David; and his son Rehoboam succeeded him.

2Kings14:2 He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign
2Kings14:3 He did what was right in the sight of the LORD
2Kings14:4 But the high places were not removed; the people still sacrificed and made offerings on the high places.
2Kings14:5 As soon as the royal power was firmly in his hand he killed his servants who had murdered his father the king.
2Kings14:6 But he did not put to death the children of the murderers; according to what is written in the book of the law of Moses

2Kings16:1 In the seventeenth year of Pekah son of Remaliah
2Kings16:2 Ahaz was twenty years old when he began to reign; he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. He did not do what was right in the sight of the LORD his God
2Kings16:3 but he walked in the way of the kings of Israel. He even made his son pass through fire
2Kings16:4 He sacrificed and made offerings on the high places
2Kings16:5 Then King Rezin of Aram and King Pekah son of Remaliah of Israel came up to wage war on Jerusalem; they besieged Ahaz but could not conquer him.
2Kings16:6 At that time the king of Edom recovered Elath for Edom
2Kings16:7 Ahaz sent messengers to King Tiglath-pileser of Assyria
2Kings16:8 Ahaz also took the silver and gold found in the house of the LORD and in the treasures of the king's house
2Kings16:9 The king of Assyria listened to him; the king of Assyria marched up against Damascus

2Kings17:1 In the twelfth year of King Ahaz of Judah
2Kings17:2 He did what was evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings17:3 King Shalmaneser of Assyria came up against him; Hoshea became his vassal
2Kings17:4 But the king of Assyria found treachery in Hoshea; for he had sent messengers to King So of Egypt
2Kings17:5 Then the king of Assyria invaded all the land and came to Samaria; for three years he besieged it.
2Kings17:6 In the ninth year of Hoshea the king of Assyria captured Samaria; he carried the Israelites away to Assyria. He placed them in Halah
2Kings17:7 This occurred because the people of Israel had sinned against the LORD their God
2Kings17:8 and walked in the customs of the nations whom the LORD drove out before the people of Israel
2Kings17:9 The people of Israel secretly did things that were not right against the LORD their God. They built for themselves high places at all their towns
2Kings17:10 they set up for themselves pillars and sacred poles on every high hill and under every green tree;
2Kings17:11 there they made offerings on all the high places
2Kings17:12 they served idols
2Kings17:13 Yet the LORD warned Israel and Judah by every prophet and every seer
2Kings17:14 They would not listen but were stubborn
2Kings17:15 They despised his statutes
2Kings17:16 They rejected all the commandments of the LORD their God and made for themselves cast images of two calves; they made a sacred pole
2Kings17:17 They made their sons and their daughters pass through fire; they used divination and augury; and they sold themselves to do evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings17:18 Therefore the LORD was very angry with Israel and removed them out of his sight; none was left but the tribe of Judah alone.
2Kings17:19 Judah also did not keep the commandments of the LORD their God but walked in the customs that Israel had introduced.
2Kings17:20 The LORD rejected all the descendants of Israel; he punished them and gave them into the hand of plunderers
2Kings17:21 When he had torn Israel from the house of David
2Kings17:22 The people of Israel continued in all the sins that Jeroboam committed; they did not depart from them
2Kings17:23 until the LORD removed Israel out of his sight
2Kings17:24 The king of Assyria brought people from Babylon
2Kings17:25 When they first settled there
2Kings17:26 So the king of Assyria was told
2Kings17:27 Then the king of Assyria commanded
2Kings17:28 So one of the priests whom they had carried away from Samaria came and lived in Bethel; he taught them how they should worship the LORD.
2Kings17:29 But every nation still made gods of its own and put them in the shrines of the high places that the people of Samaria had made
2Kings17:30 the people of Babylon made Succoth-benoth
2Kings17:31 the Avvites made Nibhaz and Tartak; the Sepharvites burned their children in the fire to Adrammelech and Anammelech
2Kings17:32 They also worshiped the LORD and appointed from among themselves all sorts of people as priests of the high places
2Kings17:33 So they worshiped the LORD but also served their own gods
2Kings17:34 To this day they continue to practice their former customs. They do not worship the LORD and they do not follow the statutes or the ordinances or the law or the commandment that the LORD commanded the children of Jacob
2Kings17:35 The LORD had made a covenant with them and commanded them
2Kings17:36 but you shall worship the LORD
2Kings17:37 The statutes and the ordinances and the law and the commandment that he wrote for you
2Kings17:38 you shall not forget the covenant that I have made with you. You shall not worship other gods
2Kings17:39 but you shall worship the LORD your God; he will deliver you out of the hand of all your enemies.
2Kings17:40 They would not listen
2Kings17:41 So these nations worshiped the LORD
2Kings18:1 In the third year of King Hoshea son of Elah of Israel

2Kings18:3 He did what was right in the sight of the LORD just as his ancestor David had done.

2Kings21:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he began to reign; he reigned fifty-five years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hephzibah.

2Kings21:4 He built altars in the house of the LORD

2Kings21:9 But they did not listen; Manasseh misled them to do more evil than the nations had done that the LORD destroyed before the people of Israel.

1Chronicles10:1 Now the Philistines fought against Israel; and the men of Israel fled before the Philistines
1Chronicles10:2 The Philistines overtook Saul and his sons; and the Philistines killed Jonathan and Abinadab and Malchishua
1Chronicles10:3 The battle pressed hard on Saul; and the archers found him
1Chronicles10:4 Then Saul said to his armor-bearer
1Chronicles10:5 When his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead
1Chronicles10:6 Thus Saul died; he and his three sons and all his house died together.
1Chronicles10:7 When all the men of Israel who were in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead
1Chronicles10:8 The next day when the Philistines came to strip the dead
1Chronicles10:9 They stripped him and took his head and his armor
1Chronicles10:10 They put his armor in the temple of their gods
1Chronicles10:11 But when all Jabesh-gilead heard everything that the Philistines had done to Saul
1Chronicles10:12 all the valiant warriors got up and took away the body of Saul and the bodies of his sons
1Chronicles10:13 So Saul died for his unfaithfulness; he was unfaithful to the LORD in that he did not keep the command of the LORD; moreover
1Chronicles10:14 and did not seek guidance from the LORD. Therefore the LORD put him to death and turned the kingdom over to David son of Jesse.

2Chronicles3:4 The vestibule in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits long
2Chronicles3:5 The nave he lined with cypress

2Chronicles3:8 He made the most holy place; its length

2Chronicles3:13 The wings of these cherubim extended twenty cubits; the cherubim stood on their feet

2Chronicles3:16 He made encircling chains and put them on the tops of the pillars; and he made one hundred pomegranates
2Chronicles3:17 He set up the pillars in front of the temple

2Chronicles30:22 Hezekiah spoke encouragingly to all the Levites who showed good skill in the service of the LORD. So the people ate the food of the festival for seven days
2Chronicles30:23 Then the whole assembly agreed together to keep the festival for another seven days; so they kept it for another seven days with gladness.
2Chronicles30:24 For King Hezekiah of Judah gave the assembly a thousand bulls and seven thousand sheep for offerings
2Chronicles30:25 The whole assembly of Judah
2Chronicles30:26 There was great joy in Jerusalem
2Chronicles30:27 Then the priests and the Levites stood up and blessed the people
2Chronicles31:1 Now when all this was finished
2Chronicles31:2 Hezekiah appointed the divisions of the priests and of the Levites
2Chronicles31:3 The contribution of the king from his own possessions was for the burnt offerings: the burnt offerings of morning and evening
2Chronicles31:4 He commanded the people who lived in Jerusalem to give the portion due to the priests and the Levites
2Chronicles31:5 As soon as the word spread

2Chronicles32:1 After these things and these acts of faithfulness
2Chronicles32:2 When Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had come and intended to fight against Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:3 he planned with his officers and his warriors to stop the flow of the springs that were outside the city; and they helped him.
2Chronicles32:4 A great many people were gathered
2Chronicles32:5 Hezekiah set to work resolutely and built up the entire wall that was broken down
2Chronicles32:6 He appointed combat commanders over the people
2Chronicles32:7 Be strong and of good courage. Do not be afraid or dismayed before the king of Assyria and all the horde that is with him; for there is one greater with us than with him.
2Chronicles32:8 With him is an arm of flesh; but with us is the LORD our God
2Chronicles32:9 After this
2Chronicles32:10 Thus says King Sennacherib of Assyria: On what are you relying
2Chronicles32:11 Is not Hezekiah misleading you
2Chronicles32:12 Was it not this same Hezekiah who took away his high places and his altars and commanded Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:13 Do you not know what I and my ancestors have done to all the peoples of other lands? Were the gods of the nations of those lands at all able to save their lands out of my hand?
2Chronicles32:14 Who among all the gods of those nations that my ancestors utterly destroyed was able to save his people from my hand
2Chronicles32:15 Now therefore do not let Hezekiah deceive you or mislead you in this fashion
2Chronicles32:16 His servants said still more against the Lord GOD and against his servant Hezekiah.
2Chronicles32:17 He also wrote letters to throw contempt on the LORD the God of Israel and to speak against him
2Chronicles32:18 They shouted it with a loud voice in the language of Judah to the people of Jerusalem who were on the wall
2Chronicles32:19 They spoke of the God of Jerusalem as if he were like the gods of the peoples of the earth
2Chronicles32:20 Then King Hezekiah and the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz prayed because of this and cried to heaven.
2Chronicles32:21 And the LORD sent an angel who cut off all the mighty warriors and commanders and officers in the camp of the king of Assyria. So he returned in disgrace to his own land. When he came into the house of his god
2Chronicles32:22 So the LORD saved Hezekiah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem from the hand of King Sennacherib of Assyria and from the hand of all his enemies; he gave them rest on every side.
2Chronicles32:23 Many brought gifts to the LORD in Jerusalem and precious things to King Hezekiah of Judah
2Chronicles32:24 In those days Hezekiah became sick and was at the point of death. He prayed to the LORD
2Chronicles32:25 But Hezekiah did not respond according to the benefit done to him
2Chronicles32:26 Then Hezekiah humbled himself for the pride of his heart
2Chronicles32:27 Hezekiah had very great riches and honor; and he made for himself treasuries for silver
2Chronicles32:28 storehouses also for the yield of grain
2Chronicles32:29 He likewise provided cities for himself
2Chronicles32:30 This same Hezekiah closed the upper outlet of the waters of Gihon and directed them down to the west side of the city of David. Hezekiah prospered in all his works.
2Chronicles32:31 So also in the matter of the envoys of the officials of Babylon
2Chronicles32:32 Now the rest of the acts of Hezekiah
2Chronicles32:33 Hezekiah slept with his ancestors
2Chronicles33:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he began to reign; he reigned fifty-five years in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles33:2 He did what was evil in the sight of the LORD
2Chronicles33:3 For he rebuilt the high places that his father Hezekiah had pulled down
2Chronicles33:4 He built altars in the house of the LORD
2Chronicles33:5 He built altars for all the host of heaven in the two courts of the house of the LORD.
2Chronicles33:6 He made his son pass through fire in the valley of the son of Hinnom
2Chronicles33:7 The carved image of the idol that he had made he set in the house of God
2Chronicles33:8 I will never again remove the feet of Israel from the land that I appointed for your ancestors
2Chronicles33:9 Manasseh misled Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem
2Chronicles33:10 The LORD spoke to Manasseh and to his people
2Chronicles33:11 Therefore the LORD brought against them the commanders of the army of the king of Assyria
2Chronicles33:12 While he was in distress he entreated the favor of the LORD his God and humbled himself greatly before the God of his ancestors.
2Chronicles33:13 He prayed to him
2Chronicles33:14 Afterward he built an outer wall for the city of David west of Gihon
2Chronicles33:15 He took away the foreign gods and the idol from the house of the LORD
2Chronicles33:16 He also restored the altar of the LORD and offered on it sacrifices of well-being and of thanksgiving; and he commanded Judah to serve the LORD the God of Israel.

2Chronicles33:21 Amon was twenty-two years old when he began to reign; he reigned two years in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles33:22 He did what was evil in the sight of the LORD
2Chronicles33:23 He did not humble himself before the LORD
2Chronicles33:24 His servants conspired against him and killed him in his house.
2Chronicles33:25 But the people of the land killed all those who had conspired against King Amon; and the people of the land made his son Josiah king to succeed him.
2Chronicles34:1 Josiah was eight years old when he began to reign; he reigned thirty-one years in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles34:2 He did what was right in the sight of the LORD
2Chronicles34:3 For in the eighth year of his reign
2Chronicles34:4 In his presence they pulled down the altars of the Baals; he demolished the incense altars that stood above them. He broke down the sacred poles and the carved and the cast images; he made dust of them and scattered it over the graves of those who had sacrificed to them.
2Chronicles34:5 He also burned the bones of the priests on their altars
2Chronicles34:6 In the towns of Manasseh
2Chronicles34:7 he broke down the altars
2Chronicles34:8 In the eighteenth year of his reign
2Chronicles34:9 They came to the high priest Hilkiah and delivered the money that had been brought into the house of God
2Chronicles34:10 They delivered it to the workers who had the oversight of the house of the LORD
2Chronicles34:11 They gave it to the carpenters and the builders to buy quarried stone
2Chronicles34:12 The people did the work faithfully. Over them were appointed the Levites Jahath and Obadiah
2Chronicles34:13 were over the burden bearers and directed all who did work in every kind of service; and some of the Levites were scribes
2Chronicles34:14 While they were bringing out the money that had been brought into the house of the LORD
2Chronicles34:15 Hilkiah said to the secretary Shaphan
2Chronicles34:16 Shaphan brought the book to the king
2Chronicles34:17 They have emptied out the money that was found in the house of the LORD and have delivered it into the hand of the overseers and the workers.
2Chronicles34:18 The secretary Shaphan informed the king
2Chronicles34:19 When the king heard the words of the law he tore his clothes.
2Chronicles34:20 Then the king commanded Hilkiah
2Chronicles34:21 Go
2Chronicles34:22 So Hilkiah and those whom the king had sent went to the prophet Huldah
2Chronicles34:23 She declared to them
2Chronicles34:24 Thus says the LORD: I will indeed bring disaster upon this place and upon its inhabitants
2Chronicles34:25 Because they have forsaken me and have made offerings to other gods
2Chronicles34:26 But as to the king of Judah
2Chronicles34:27 because your heart was penitent and you humbled yourself before God when you heard his words against this place and its inhabitants
2Chronicles34:28 I will gather you to your ancestors and you shall be gathered to your grave in peace; your eyes shall not see all the disaster that I will bring on this place and its inhabitants. They took the message back to the king.
2Chronicles34:29 Then the king sent word and gathered together all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.
2Chronicles34:30 The king went up to the house of the LORD
2Chronicles34:31 The king stood in his place and made a covenant before the LORD
2Chronicles34:32 Then he made all who were present in Jerusalem and in Benjamin pledge themselves to it. And the inhabitants of Jerusalem acted according to the covenant of God
2Chronicles34:33 Josiah took away all the abominations from all the territory that belonged to the people of Israel

2Chronicles35:3 He said to the Levites who taught all Israel and who were holy to the LORD

2Chronicles35:19 In the eighteenth year of the reign of Josiah this passover was kept.

2Chronicles36:2 Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he began to reign; he reigned three months in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles36:3 Then the king of Egypt deposed him in Jerusalem and laid on the land a tribute of one hundred talents of silver and one talent of gold.
2Chronicles36:4 The king of Egypt made his brother Eliakim king over Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles36:5 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign; he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. He did what was evil in the sight of the LORD his God.
2Chronicles36:6 Against him King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came up
2Chronicles36:7 Nebuchadnezzar also carried some of the vessels of the house of the LORD to Babylon and put them in his palace in Babylon.

2Chronicles36:9 Jehoiachin was eight years old when he began to reign; he reigned three months and ten days in Jerusalem. He did what was evil in the sight of the LORD.
2Chronicles36:10 In the spring of the year King Nebuchadnezzar sent and brought him to Babylon
2Chronicles36:11 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he began to reign; he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles36:12 He did what was evil in the sight of the LORD his God. He did not humble himself before the prophet Jeremiah who spoke from the mouth of the LORD.

Ezra8:22 For I was ashamed to ask the king for a band of soldiers and cavalry to protect us against the enemy on our way
Ezra8:23 So we fasted and petitioned our God for this
Ezra8:24 Then I set apart twelve of the leading priests: Sherebiah
Ezra8:25 And I weighed out to them the silver and the gold and the vessels
Ezra8:26 I weighed out into their hand six hundred fifty talents of silver
Ezra8:27 twenty gold bowls worth a thousand darics
Ezra8:28 And I said to them
Ezra8:29 Guard them and keep them until you weigh them before the chief priests and the Levites and the heads of families in Israel at Jerusalem
Ezra8:30 So the priests and the Levites took over the silver
Ezra8:31 Then we left the river Ahava on the twelfth day of the first month
Ezra8:32 We came to Jerusalem and remained there three days.
Ezra8:33 On the fourth day
Ezra8:34 The total was counted and weighed
Ezra8:35 At that time those who had come from captivity
Ezra8:36 They also delivered the king's commissions to the king's satraps and to the governors of the province Beyond the River; and they supported the people and the house of God.
Ezra9:1 After these things had been done
Ezra9:2 For they have taken some of their daughters as wives for themselves and for their sons. Thus the holy seed has mixed itself with the peoples of the lands
Ezra9:3 When I heard this
Ezra9:4 Then all who trembled at the words of the God of Israel
Ezra9:5 At the evening sacrifice I got up from my fasting
Ezra9:6 and said
Ezra9:7 From the days of our ancestors to this day we have been deep in guilt
Ezra9:8 But now for a brief moment favor has been shown by the LORD our God
Ezra9:9 For we are slaves; yet our God has not forsaken us in our slavery
Ezra9:10 And now
Ezra9:11 which you commanded by your servants the prophets
Ezra9:12 Therefore do not give your daughters to their sons
Ezra9:13 After all that has come upon us for our evil deeds and for our great guilt
Ezra9:14 shall we break your commandments again and intermarry with the peoples who practice these abominations? Would you not be angry with us until you destroy us without remnant or survivor?
Ezra9:15 O LORD

Ezra10:12 Then all the assembly answered with a loud voice
Ezra10:13 But the people are many

Nehemiah1:1 The words of Nehemiah son of Hacaliah. In the month of Chislev
Nehemiah1:2 one of my brothers
Nehemiah1:3 They replied
Nehemiah1:4 When I heard these words I sat down and wept
Nehemiah1:5 I said

Nehemiah1:7 We have offended you deeply
Nehemiah1:8 Remember the word that you commanded your servant Moses
Nehemiah1:9 but if you return to me and keep my commandments and do them
Nehemiah1:10 They are your servants and your people
Nehemiah1:11 O Lord
Nehemiah2:1 In the month of Nisan
Nehemiah2:2 So the king said to me

Nehemiah2:10 When Sanballat the Horonite and Tobiah the Ammonite official heard this
Nehemiah2:11 So I came to Jerusalem and was there for three days.
Nehemiah2:12 Then I got up during the night
Nehemiah2:13 I went out by night by the Valley Gate past the Dragon's Spring and to the Dung Gate
Nehemiah2:14 Then I went on to the Fountain Gate and to the King's Pool; but there was no place for the animal I was riding to continue.
Nehemiah2:15 So I went up by way of the valley by night and inspected the wall. Then I turned back and entered by the Valley Gate
Nehemiah2:16 The officials did not know where I had gone or what I was doing; I had not yet told the Jews

Nehemiah3:1 Then the high priest Eliashib set to work with his fellow priests and rebuilt the Sheep Gate. They consecrated it and set up its doors; they consecrated it as far as the Tower of the Hundred and as far as the Tower of Hananel.
Nehemiah3:2 And the men of Jericho built next to him. And next to them Zaccur son of Imri built.
Nehemiah3:3 The sons of Hassenaah built the Fish Gate; they laid its beams and set up its doors
Nehemiah3:4 Next to them Meremoth son of Uriah son of Hakkoz made repairs. Next to them Meshullam son of Berechiah son of Meshezabel made repairs. Next to them Zadok son of Baana made repairs.
Nehemiah3:5 Next to them the Tekoites made repairs; but their nobles would not put their shoulders to the work of their Lord.
Nehemiah3:6 Joiada son of Paseah and Meshullam son of Besodeiah repaired the Old Gate; they laid its beams and set up its doors
Nehemiah3:7 Next to them repairs were made by Melatiah the Gibeonite and Jadon the Meronothite-- the men of Gibeon and of Mizpah-- who were under the jurisdiction of the governor of the province Beyond the River.
Nehemiah3:8 Next to them Uzziel son of Harhaiah
Nehemiah3:9 Next to them Rephaiah son of Hur
Nehemiah3:10 Next to them Jedaiah son of Harumaph made repairs opposite his house; and next to him Hattush son of Hashabneiah made repairs.
Nehemiah3:11 Malchijah son of Harim and Hasshub son of Pahath-moab repaired another section and the Tower of the Ovens.
Nehemiah3:12 Next to him Shallum son of Hallohesh
Nehemiah3:13 Hanun and the inhabitants of Zanoah repaired the Valley Gate; they rebuilt it and set up its doors
Nehemiah3:14 Malchijah son of Rechab
Nehemiah3:15 And Shallum son of Col-hozeh
Nehemiah3:16 After him Nehemiah son of Azbuk
Nehemiah3:17 After him the Levites made repairs: Rehum son of Bani; next to him Hashabiah
Nehemiah3:18 After him their kin made repairs: Binnui
Nehemiah3:19 next to him Ezer son of Jeshua
Nehemiah3:20 After him Baruch son of Zabbai repaired another section from the Angle to the door of the house of the high priest Eliashib.
Nehemiah3:21 After him Meremoth son of Uriah son of Hakkoz repaired another section from the door of the house of Eliashib to the end of the house of Eliashib.
Nehemiah3:22 After him the priests
Nehemiah3:23 After them Benjamin and Hasshub made repairs opposite their house. After them Azariah son of Maaseiah son of Ananiah made repairs beside his own house.
Nehemiah3:24 After him Binnui son of Henadad repaired another section
Nehemiah3:25 Palal son of Uzai repaired opposite the Angle and the tower projecting from the upper house of the king at the court of the guard. After him Pedaiah son of Parosh
Nehemiah3:26 and the temple servants living on Ophel made repairs up to a point opposite the Water Gate on the east and the projecting tower.
Nehemiah3:27 After him the Tekoites repaired another section opposite the great projecting tower as far as the wall of Ophel.
Nehemiah3:28 Above the Horse Gate the priests made repairs
Nehemiah3:29 After them Zadok son of Immer made repairs opposite his own house. After him Shemaiah son of Shecaniah
Nehemiah3:30 After him Hananiah son of Shelemiah and Hanun sixth son of Zalaph repaired another section. After him Meshullam son of Berechiah made repairs opposite his living quarters.
Nehemiah3:31 After him Malchijah
Nehemiah3:32 And between the upper room of the corner and the Sheep Gate the goldsmiths and the merchants made repairs.
Nehemiah4:1 Now when Sanballat heard that we were building the wall
Nehemiah4:2 He said in the presence of his associates and of the army of Samaria
Nehemiah4:3 Tobiah the Ammonite was beside him
Nehemiah4:4 Hear
Nehemiah4:5 Do not cover their guilt
Nehemiah4:6 So we rebuilt the wall
Nehemiah4:7 But when Sanballat and Tobiah and the Arabs and the Ammonites and the Ashdodites heard that the repairing of the walls of Jerusalem was going forward and the gaps were beginning to be closed
Nehemiah4:8 and all plotted together to come and fight against Jerusalem and to cause confusion in it.
Nehemiah4:9 So we prayed to our God
Nehemiah4:10 But Judah said
Nehemiah4:11 And our enemies said
Nehemiah4:12 When the Jews who lived near them came
Nehemiah4:13 So in the lowest parts of the space behind the wall
Nehemiah4:14 After I looked these things over
Nehemiah4:15 When our enemies heard that their plot was known to us
Nehemiah4:16 From that day on
Nehemiah4:17 who were building the wall. The burden bearers carried their loads in such a way that each labored on the work with one hand and with the other held a weapon.
Nehemiah4:18 And each of the builders had his sword strapped at his side while he built. The man who sounded the trumpet was beside me.
Nehemiah4:19 And I said to the nobles
Nehemiah4:20 Rally to us wherever you hear the sound of the trumpet. Our God will fight for us.
Nehemiah4:21 So we labored at the work
Nehemiah4:22 I also said to the people at that time
Nehemiah4:23 So neither I nor my brothers nor my servants nor the men of the guard who followed me ever took off our clothes; each kept his weapon in his right hand.
Nehemiah5:1 Now there was a great outcry of the people and of their wives against their Jewish kin.
Nehemiah5:2 For there were those who said
Nehemiah5:3 There were also those who said
Nehemiah5:4 And there were those who said

Nehemiah5:6 I was very angry when I heard their outcry and these complaints.
Nehemiah5:7 After thinking it over
Nehemiah5:8 and said to them

Nehemiah9:1 Now on the twenty-fourth day of this month the people of Israel were assembled with fasting and in sackcloth
Nehemiah9:2 Then those of Israelite descent separated themselves from all foreigners
Nehemiah9:3 They stood up in their place and read from the book of the law of the LORD their God for a fourth part of the day
Nehemiah9:4 Then Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 Then the Levites
Nehemiah9:6 And Ezra said: You are the LORD
Nehemiah9:7 You are the LORD
Nehemiah9:8 and you found his heart faithful before you
Nehemiah9:9 And you saw the distress of our ancestors in Egypt and heard their cry at the Red Sea.
Nehemiah9:10 You performed signs and wonders against Pharaoh and all his servants and all the people of his land
Nehemiah9:11 And you divided the sea before them
Nehemiah9:12 Moreover
Nehemiah9:13 You came down also upon Mount Sinai
Nehemiah9:14 and you made known your holy sabbath to them and gave them commandments and statutes and a law through your servant Moses.
Nehemiah9:15 For their hunger you gave them bread from heaven
Nehemiah9:16 But they and our ancestors acted presumptuously and stiffened their necks and did not obey your commandments;
Nehemiah9:17 they refused to obey
Nehemiah9:18 Even when they had cast an image of a calf for themselves and said
Nehemiah9:19 you in your great mercies did not forsake them in the wilderness; the pillar of cloud that led them in the way did not leave them by day
Nehemiah9:20 You gave your good spirit to instruct them
Nehemiah9:21 Forty years you sustained them in the wilderness so that they lacked nothing; their clothes did not wear out and their feet did not swell.
Nehemiah9:22 And you gave them kingdoms and peoples
Nehemiah9:23 You multiplied their descendants like the stars of heaven
Nehemiah9:24 So the descendants went in and possessed the land
Nehemiah9:25 And they captured fortress cities and a rich land
Nehemiah9:26 Nevertheless they were disobedient and rebelled against you and cast your law behind their backs and killed your prophets
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore you gave them into the hands of their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 But after they had rest
Nehemiah9:29 And you warned them in order to turn them back to your law. Yet they acted presumptuously and did not obey your commandments
Nehemiah9:30 Many years you were patient with them
Nehemiah9:31 Nevertheless
Nehemiah9:32 Now therefore
Nehemiah9:33 You have been just in all that has come upon us
Nehemiah9:34 our kings
Nehemiah9:35 Even in their own kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 Here we are
Nehemiah9:37 Its rich yield goes to the kings whom you have set over us because of our sins; they have power also over our bodies and over our livestock at their pleasure
Nehemiah9:38 Because of all this we make a firm agreement in writing

Esther1:1 This happened in the days of Ahasuerus
Esther1:2 In those days when King Ahasuerus sat on his royal throne in the citadel of Susa
Esther1:3 in the third year of his reign
Esther1:4 while he displayed the great wealth of his kingdom and the splendor and pomp of his majesty for many days
Esther1:5 When these days were completed
Esther1:6 There were white cotton curtains and blue hangings tied with cords of fine linen and purple to silver rings and marble pillars. There were couches of gold and silver on a mosaic pavement of porphyry
Esther1:7 Drinks were served in golden goblets
Esther1:8 Drinking was by flagons
Esther1:9 Furthermore
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti before the king
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king's command conveyed by the eunuchs. At this the king was enraged
Esther1:13 Then the king consulted the sages who knew the laws (for this was the king's procedure toward all who were versed in law and custom
Esther1:14 and those next to him were Carshena
Esther1:15 According to the law
Esther1:16 Then Memucan said in the presence of the king and the officials
Esther1:17 For this deed of the queen will be made known to all women
Esther1:18 This very day the noble ladies of Persia and Media who have heard of the queen's behavior will rebel against the king's officials
Esther1:19 If it pleases the king
Esther1:20 So when the decree made by the king is proclaimed throughout all his kingdom
Esther1:21 This advice pleased the king and the officials
Esther1:22 he sent letters to all the royal provinces
Esther2:1 After these things
Esther2:2 Then the king's servants who attended him said
Esther2:3 And let the king appoint commissioners in all the provinces of his kingdom to gather all the beautiful young virgins to the harem in the citadel of Susa under custody of Hegai
Esther2:4 And let the girl who pleases the king be queen instead of Vashti. This pleased the king
Esther2:5 Now there was a Jew in the citadel of Susa whose name was Mordecai son of Jair son of Shimei son of Kish
Esther2:6 Kish had been carried away from Jerusalem among the captives carried away with King Jeconiah of Judah
Esther2:7 Mordecai had brought up Hadassah
Esther2:8 So when the king's order and his edict were proclaimed
Esther2:9 The girl pleased him and won his favor
Esther2:10 Esther did not reveal her people or kindred
Esther2:11 Every day Mordecai would walk around in front of the court of the harem
Esther2:12 The turn came for each girl to go in to King Ahasuerus
Esther2:13 When the girl went in to the king she was given whatever she asked for to take with her from the harem to the king's palace.
Esther2:14 In the evening she went in; then in the morning she came back to the second harem in custody of Shaashgaz
Esther2:15 When the turn came for Esther daughter of Abihail the uncle of Mordecai
Esther2:16 When Esther was taken to King Ahasuerus in his royal palace in the tenth month
Esther2:17 the king loved Esther more than all the other women; of all the virgins she won his favor and devotion
Esther2:18 Then the king gave a great banquet to all his officials and ministers-- Esther's banquet. He also granted a holiday to the provinces

Esther2:20 Now Esther had not revealed her kindred or her people

Job1:1 There was once a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job. That man was blameless and upright
Job1:2 There were born to him seven sons and three daughters.
Job1:3 He had seven thousand sheep
Job1:4 His sons used to go and hold feasts in one another's houses in turn; and they would send and invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
Job1:5 And when the feast days had run their course

Job1:8 The LORD said to Satan
Job1:9 Then Satan answered the LORD

Job1:18 While he was still speaking

Job1:20 Then Job arose

Job3:1 After this Job opened his mouth and cursed the day of his birth.
Job3:2 Job said:
Job3:3 Let the day perish in which I was born
Job3:4 Let that day be darkness! May God above not seek it
Job3:5 Let gloom and deep darkness claim it. Let clouds settle upon it; let the blackness of the day terrify it.
Job3:6 That night-- let thick darkness seize it! let it not rejoice among the days of the year; let it not come into the number of the months.
Job3:7 Yes
Job3:8 Let those curse it who curse the Sea
Job3:9 Let the stars of its dawn be dark; let it hope for light
Job3:10 because it did not shut the doors of my mother's womb
Job3:11 Why did I not die at birth
Job3:12 Why were there knees to receive me
Job3:13 Now I would be lying down and quiet; I would be asleep; then I would be at rest

Job3:15 or with princes who have gold
Job3:16 Or why was I not buried like a stillborn child
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from troubling
Job3:18 There the prisoners are at ease together; they do not hear the voice of the taskmaster.
Job3:19 The small and the great are there
Job3:20 Why is light given to one in misery
Job3:21 who long for death
Job3:22 who rejoice exceedingly
Job3:23 Why is light given to one who cannot see the way
Job3:24 For my sighing comes like my bread
Job3:25 Truly the thing that I fear comes upon me
Job3:26 I am not at ease

Job4:2 If one ventures a word with you
Job4:3 See
Job4:4 Your words have supported those who were stumbling
Job4:5 But now it has come to you
Job4:6 Is not your fear of God your confidence
Job4:7 Think now
Job4:8 As I have seen
Job4:9 By the breath of God they perish
Job4:10 The roar of the lion
Job4:11 The strong lion perishes for lack of prey
Job4:12 Now a word came stealing to me
Job4:13 Amid thoughts from visions of the night
Job4:14 dread came upon me
Job4:15 A spirit glided past my face; the hair of my flesh bristled.
Job4:16 It stood still
Job4:17 'Can mortals be righteous before God? Can human beings be pure before their Maker?
Job4:18 Even in his servants he puts no trust
Job4:19 how much more those who live in houses of clay
Job4:20 Between morning and evening they are destroyed; they perish forever without any regarding it.
Job4:21 Their tent-cord is plucked up within them
Job5:1 Call now; is there anyone who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
Job5:2 Surely vexation kills the fool
Job5:3 I have seen fools taking root
Job5:4 Their children are far from safety
Job5:5 The hungry eat their harvest
Job5:6 For misery does not come from the earth
Job5:7 but human beings are born to trouble just as sparks fly upward.
Job5:8 As for me
Job5:9 He does great things and unsearchable
Job5:10 He gives rain on the earth and sends waters on the fields;
Job5:11 he sets on high those who are lowly
Job5:12 He frustrates the devices of the crafty
Job5:13 He takes the wise in their own craftiness; and the schemes of the wily are brought to a quick end.
Job5:14 They meet with darkness in the daytime
Job5:15 But he saves the needy from the sword of their mouth
Job5:16 So the poor have hope
Job5:17 How happy is the one whom God reproves; therefore do not despise the discipline of the Almighty.
Job5:18 For he wounds
Job5:19 He will deliver you from six troubles; in seven no harm shall touch you.
Job5:20 In famine he will redeem you from death
Job5:21 You shall be hidden from the scourge of the tongue
Job5:22 At destruction and famine you shall laugh
Job5:23 For you shall be in league with the stones of the field
Job5:24 You shall know that your tent is safe
Job5:25 You shall know that your descendants will be many
Job5:26 You shall come to your grave in ripe old age
Job5:27 See
Job6:1 Then Job answered:
Job6:2 O that my vexation were weighed
Job6:3 For then it would be heavier than the sand of the sea; therefore my words have been rash.
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty are in me; my spirit drinks their poison; the terrors of God are arrayed against me.
Job6:5 Does the wild ass bray over its grass
Job6:6 Can that which is tasteless be eaten without salt
Job6:7 My appetite refuses to touch them; they are like food that is loathsome to me.
Job6:8 O that I might have my request
Job6:9 that it would please God to crush me
Job6:10 This would be my consolation; I would even exult in unrelenting pain; for I have not denied the words of the Holy One.
Job6:11 What is my strength
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones
Job6:13 In truth I have no help in me
Job6:14 Those who withhold kindness from a friend forsake the fear of the Almighty.
Job6:15 My companions are treacherous like a torrent-bed
Job6:16 that run dark with ice
Job6:17 In time of heat they disappear; when it is hot
Job6:18 The caravans turn aside from their course; they go up into the waste
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema look
Job6:20 They are disappointed because they were confident; they come there and are confounded.

Job7:2 Like a slave who longs for the shadow

Job9:35 then I would speak without fear of him
