Job10:3 Does it seem good to you to oppress
Job10:4 Do you have eyes of flesh? Do you see as humans see?
Job10:5 Are your days like the days of mortals
Job10:6 that you seek out my iniquity and search for my sin
Job10:7 although you know that I am not guilty
Job10:8 Your hands fashioned and made me; and now you turn and destroy me.
Job10:9 Remember that you fashioned me like clay; and will you turn me to dust again?
Job10:10 Did you not pour me out like milk and curdle me like cheese?
Job10:11 You clothed me with skin and flesh
Job10:12 You have granted me life and steadfast love
Job10:13 Yet these things you hid in your heart; I know that this was your purpose.
Job10:14 If I sin
Job10:15 If I am wicked
Job10:16 Bold as a lion you hunt me; you repeat your exploits against me.
Job10:17 You renew your witnesses against me
Job10:18 Why did you bring me forth from the womb? Would that I had died before any eye had seen me
Job10:19 and were as though I had not been
Job10:20 Are not the days of my life few? Let me alone
Job10:21 before I go
Job10:22 the land of gloom and chaos
Job11:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered:
Job11:2 Should a multitude of words go unanswered
Job11:3 Should your babble put others to silence
Job11:4 For you say
Job11:5 But oh
Job11:6 and that he would tell you the secrets of wisdom! For wisdom is many-sided. Know then that God exacts of you less than your guilt deserves.
Job11:7 Can you find out the deep things of God? Can you find out the limit of the Almighty?
Job11:8 It is higher than heaven-- what can you do? Deeper than Sheol-- what can you know?
Job11:9 Its measure is longer than the earth
Job11:10 If he passes through
Job11:11 For he knows those who are worthless; when he sees iniquity
Job11:12 But a stupid person will get understanding
Job11:13 If you direct your heart rightly
Job11:14 If iniquity is in your hand
Job11:15 Surely then you will lift up your face without blemish; you will be secure
Job11:16 You will forget your misery; you will remember it as waters that have passed away.
Job11:17 And your life will be brighter than the noonday; its darkness will be like the morning.
Job11:18 And you will have confidence
Job11:19 You will lie down
Job11:20 But the eyes of the wicked will fail; all way of escape will be lost to them
Job12:1 Then Job answered:
Job12:2 No doubt you are the people
Job12:3 But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these?
Job12:4 I am a laughingstock to my friends; I
Job12:5 Those at ease have contempt for misfortune
Job12:6 The tents of robbers are at peace
Job12:7 But ask the animals
Job12:8 ask the plants of the earth
Job12:9 Who among all these does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this?
Job12:10 In his hand is the life of every living thing and the breath of every human being.
Job12:11 Does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food?
Job12:12 Is wisdom with the aged
Job12:13 With God are wisdom and strength; he has counsel and understanding.
Job12:14 If he tears down
Job12:15 If he withholds the waters
Job12:16 With him are strength and wisdom; the deceived and the deceiver are his.
Job12:17 He leads counselors away stripped
Job12:18 He looses the sash of kings
Job12:19 He leads priests away stripped
Job12:20 He deprives of speech those who are trusted
Job12:21 He pours contempt on princes
Job12:22 He uncovers the deeps out of darkness
Job12:23 He makes nations great
Job12:24 He strips understanding from the leaders of the earth
Job12:25 They grope in the dark without light; he makes them stagger like a drunkard.
Job13:1 Look
Job13:2 What you know
Job13:3 But I would speak to the Almighty
Job13:4 As for you
Job13:5 If you would only keep silent
Job13:6 Hear now my reasoning
Job13:7 Will you speak falsely for God
Job13:8 Will you show partiality toward him
Job13:9 Will it be well with you when he searches you out? Or can you deceive him
Job13:10 He will surely rebuke you if in secret you show partiality.
Job13:11 Will not his majesty terrify you
Job13:12 Your maxims are proverbs of ashes
Job13:13 Let me have silence
Job13:14 I will take my flesh in my teeth
Job13:15 See
Job13:16 This will be my salvation
Job13:17 Listen carefully to my words
Job13:18 I have indeed prepared my case; I know that I shall be vindicated.
Job13:19 Who is there that will contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
Job13:20 Only grant two things to me
Job13:21 withdraw your hand far from me
Job13:22 Then call
Job13:23 How many are my iniquities and my sins? Make me know my transgression and my sin.
Job13:24 Why do you hide your face
Job13:25 Will you frighten a windblown leaf and pursue dry chaff?
Job13:26 For you write bitter things against me
Job13:27 You put my feet in the stocks
Job13:28 One wastes away like a rotten thing
Job14:1 A mortal
Job14:2 comes up like a flower and withers
Job14:3 Do you fix your eyes on such a one? Do you bring me into judgment with you?
Job14:4 Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? No one can.
Job14:5 Since their days are determined
Job14:6 look away from them
Job14:7 For there is hope for a tree
Job14:8 Though its root grows old in the earth
Job14:9 yet at the scent of water it will bud and put forth branches like a young plant.
Job14:10 But mortals die
Job14:11 As waters fail from a lake
Job14:12 so mortals lie down and do not rise again; until the heavens are no more
Job14:13 Oh that you would hide me in Sheol
Job14:14 If mortals die
Job14:15 You would call
Job14:16 For then you would not number my steps
Job14:17 my transgression would be sealed up in a bag
Job14:18 But the mountain falls and crumbles away
Job14:19 the waters wear away the stones; the torrents wash away the soil of the earth; so you destroy the hope of mortals.
Job14:20 You prevail forever against them
Job14:21 Their children come to honor
Job14:22 They feel only the pain of their own bodies
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
Job15:2 Should the wise answer with windy knowledge
Job15:3 Should they argue in unprofitable talk
Job15:4 But you are doing away with the fear of God
Job15:5 For your iniquity teaches your mouth
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you
Job15:7 Are you the firstborn of the human race? Were you brought forth before the hills?
Job15:8 Have you listened in the council of God? And do you limit wisdom to yourself?
Job15:9 What do you know that we do not know? What do you understand that is not clear to us?
Job15:10 The gray-haired and the aged are on our side
Job15:11 Are the consolations of God too small for you
Job15:12 Why does your heart carry you away
Job15:13 so that you turn your spirit against God
Job15:14 What are mortals
Job15:15 God puts no trust even in his holy ones
Job15:16 how much less one who is abominable and corrupt
Job15:17 I will show you; listen to me; what I have seen I will declare--
Job15:18 what sages have told
Job15:19 to whom alone the land was given
Job15:20 The wicked writhe in pain all their days
Job15:21 Terrifying sounds are in their ears; in prosperity the destroyer will come upon them.
Job15:22 They despair of returning from darkness
Job15:23 They wander abroad for bread
Job15:24 distress and anguish terrify them; they prevail against them
Job15:25 Because they stretched out their hands against God
Job15:26 running stubbornly against him with a thick-bossed shield;
Job15:27 because they have covered their faces with their fat
Job15:28 they will live in desolate cities
Job15:29 they will not be rich
Job15:30 they will not escape from darkness; the flame will dry up their shoots
Job15:31 Let them not trust in emptiness
Job15:32 It will be paid in full before their time
Job15:33 They will shake off their unripe grape
Job15:34 For the company of the godless is barren
Job15:35 They conceive mischief and bring forth evil and their heart prepares deceit.
Job16:1 Then Job answered:
Job16:2 I have heard many such things; miserable comforters are you all.
Job16:3 Have windy words no limit? Or what provokes you that you keep on talking?
Job16:4 I also could talk as you do
Job16:5 I could encourage you with my mouth
Job16:6 If I speak
Job16:7 Surely now God has worn me out; he has made desolate all my company.
Job16:8 And he has shriveled me up
Job16:9 He has torn me in his wrath
Job16:10 They have gaped at me with their mouths; they have struck me insolently on the cheek; they mass themselves together against me.
Job16:11 God gives me up to the ungodly
Job16:12 I was at ease
Job16:13 his archers surround me. He slashes open my kidneys
Job16:14 He bursts upon me again and again; he rushes at me like a warrior.
Job16:15 I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin
Job16:16 My face is red with weeping
Job16:17 though there is no violence in my hands
Job16:18 O earth
Job16:19 Even now
Job16:20 My friends scorn me; my eye pours out tears to God
Job16:21 that he would maintain the right of a mortal with God
Job16:22 For when a few years have come
Job17:1 My spirit is broken
Job17:2 Surely there are mockers around me
Job17:3 Lay down a pledge for me with yourself; who is there that will give surety for me?
Job17:4 Since you have closed their minds to understanding
Job17:5 Those who denounce friends for reward-- the eyes of their children will fail.
Job17:6 He has made me a byword of the peoples
Job17:7 My eye has grown dim from grief
Job17:8 The upright are appalled at this
Job17:9 Yet the righteous hold to their way
Job17:10 But you
Job17:11 My days are past
Job17:12 They make night into day; 'The light
Job17:13 If I look for Sheol as my house
Job17:14 if I say to the Pit
Job17:15 where then is my hope? Who will see my hope?
Job17:16 Will it go down to the bars of Sheol? Shall we descend together into the dust?
Job18:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered:
Job18:2 How long will you hunt for words? Consider
Job18:3 Why are we counted as cattle? Why are we stupid in your sight?
Job18:4 You who tear yourself in your anger-- shall the earth be forsaken because of you
Job18:5 Surely the light of the wicked is put out
Job18:6 The light is dark in their tent
Job18:7 Their strong steps are shortened
Job18:8 For they are thrust into a net by their own feet
Job18:9 A trap seizes them by the heel; a snare lays hold of them.
Job18:10 A rope is hid for them in the ground
Job18:11 Terrors frighten them on every side
Job18:12 Their strength is consumed by hunger
Job18:13 By disease their skin is consumed
Job18:14 They are torn from the tent in which they trusted
Job18:15 In their tents nothing remains; sulfur is scattered upon their habitations.
Job18:16 Their roots dry up beneath
Job18:17 Their memory perishes from the earth
Job18:18 They are thrust from light into darkness
Job18:19 They have no offspring or descendant among their people
Job18:20 They of the west are appalled at their fate
Job18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the ungodly
Job19:1 Then Job answered:
Job19:2 How long will you torment me
Job19:3 These ten times you have cast reproach upon me; are you not ashamed to wrong me?
Job19:4 And even if it is true that I have erred
Job19:5 If indeed you magnify yourselves against me
Job19:6 know then that God has put me in the wrong
Job19:7 Even when I cry out
Job19:8 He has walled up my way so that I cannot pass
Job19:9 He has stripped my glory from me
Job19:10 He breaks me down on every side
Job19:11 He has kindled his wrath against me
Job19:12 His troops come on together; they have thrown up siegeworks against me
Job19:13 He has put my family far from me
Job19:14 My relatives and my close friends have failed me;
Job19:15 the guests in my house have forgotten me; my serving girls count me as a stranger; I have become an alien in their eyes.
Job19:16 I call to my servant
Job19:17 My breath is repulsive to my wife; I am loathsome to my own family.
Job19:18 Even young children despise me; when I rise
Job19:19 All my intimate friends abhor me
Job19:20 My bones cling to my skin and to my flesh
Job19:21 Have pity on me
Job19:22 Why do you
Job19:23 O that my words were written down! O that they were inscribed in a book!
Job19:24 O that with an iron pen and with lead they were engraved on a rock forever!
Job19:25 For I know that my Redeemer lives
Job19:26 and after my skin has been thus destroyed
Job19:27 whom I shall see on my side
Job19:28 If you say
Job19:29 be afraid of the sword
Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered:
Job20:2 Pay attention! My thoughts urge me to answer
Job20:3 I hear censure that insults me
Job20:4 Do you not know this from of old
Job20:5 that the exulting of the wicked is short
Job20:6 Even though they mount up high as the heavens
Job20:7 they will perish forever like their own dung; those who have seen them will say
Job20:8 They will fly away like a dream
Job20:9 The eye that saw them will see them no more
Job20:10 Their children will seek the favor of the poor
Job20:11 Their bodies
Job20:12 Though wickedness is sweet in their mouth
Job20:13 though they are loath to let it go
Job20:14 yet their food is turned in their stomachs; it is the venom of asps within them.
Job20:15 They swallow down riches and vomit them up again; God casts them out of their bellies.
Job20:16 They will suck the poison of asps; the tongue of a viper will kill them.
Job20:17 They will not look on the rivers
Job20:18 They will give back the fruit of their toil
Job20:19 For they have crushed and abandoned the poor
Job20:20 They knew no quiet in their bellies; in their greed they let nothing escape.
Job20:21 There was nothing left after they had eaten; therefore their prosperity will not endure.
Job20:22 In full sufficiency they will be in distress; all the force of misery will come upon them.
Job20:23 To fill their belly to the full God will send his fierce anger into them
Job20:24 They will flee from an iron weapon; a bronze arrow will strike them through.
Job20:25 It is drawn forth and comes out of their body
Job20:26 Utter darkness is laid up for their treasures; a fire fanned by no one will devour them; what is left in their tent will be consumed.
Job20:27 The heavens will reveal their iniquity
Job20:28 The possessions of their house will be carried away
Job20:29 This is the portion of the wicked from God
Job21:1 Then Job answered:
Job21:2 Listen carefully to my words
Job21:3 Bear with me
Job21:4 As for me
Job21:5 Look at me
Job21:6 When I think of it I am dismayed
Job21:7 Why do the wicked live on
Job21:8 Their children are established in their presence
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from fear
Job21:10 Their bull breeds without fail; their cow calves and never miscarries.
Job21:11 They send out their little ones like a flock
Job21:12 They sing to the tambourine and the lyre
Job21:13 They spend their days in prosperity
Job21:14 They say to God
Job21:15 What is the Almighty
Job21:16 Is not their prosperity indeed their own achievement? The plans of the wicked are repugnant to me.
Job21:17 How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does calamity come upon them? How often does God distribute pains in his anger?
Job21:18 How often are they like straw before the wind
Job21:19 You say
Job21:20 Let their own eyes see their destruction
Job21:21 For what do they care for their household after them
Job21:22 Will any teach God knowledge
Job21:23 One dies in full prosperity
Job21:24 his loins full of milk and the marrow of his bones moist.
Job21:25 Another dies in bitterness of soul
Job21:26 They lie down alike in the dust
Job21:27 Oh
Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you not asked those who travel the roads
Job21:30 that the wicked are spared in the day of calamity
Job21:31 Who declares their way to their face
Job21:32 When they are carried to the grave
Job21:33 The clods of the valley are sweet to them; everyone will follow after
Job21:34 How then will you comfort me with empty nothings? There is nothing left of your answers but falsehood.
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
Job22:2 Can a mortal be of use to God? Can even the wisest be of service to him?
Job22:3 Is it any pleasure to the Almighty if you are righteous
Job22:4 Is it for your piety that he reproves you
Job22:5 Is not your wickedness great? There is no end to your iniquities.
Job22:6 For you have exacted pledges from your family for no reason
Job22:7 You have given no water to the weary to drink
Job22:8 The powerful possess the land
Job22:9 You have sent widows away empty-handed
Job22:10 Therefore snares are around you
Job22:11 or darkness so that you cannot see; a flood of water covers you.
Job22:12 Is not God high in the heavens? See the highest stars
Job22:13 Therefore you say
Job22:14 Thick clouds enwrap him
Job22:15 Will you keep to the old way that the wicked have trod?
Job22:16 They were snatched away before their time; their foundation was washed away by a flood.
Job22:17 They said to God
Job22:18 Yet he filled their houses with good things-- but the plans of the wicked are repugnant to me.
Job22:19 The righteous see it and are glad; the innocent laugh them to scorn
Job22:20 saying
Job22:21 Agree with God
Job22:22 Receive instruction from his mouth
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty
Job22:24 if you treat gold like dust
Job22:25 and if the Almighty is your gold and your precious silver
Job22:26 then you will delight yourself in the Almighty
Job22:27 You will pray to him
Job22:28 You will decide on a matter
Job22:29 When others are humiliated
Job22:30 He will deliver even those who are guilty; they will escape because of the cleanness of your hands.
Job23:1 Then Job answered:
Job23:2 Today also my complaint is bitter; his hand is heavy despite my groaning.
Job23:3 Oh
Job23:4 I would lay my case before him
Job23:5 I would learn what he would answer me
Job23:6 Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? No; but he would give heed to me.
Job23:7 There an upright person could reason with him
Job23:8 If I go forward
Job23:9 on the left he hides
Job23:10 But he knows the way that I take; when he has tested me
Job23:11 My foot has held fast to his steps; I have kept his way and have not turned aside.
Job23:12 I have not departed from the commandment of his lips; I have treasured in my bosom the words of his mouth.
Job23:13 But he stands alone and who can dissuade him? What he desires
Job23:14 For he will complete what he appoints for me; and many such things are in his mind.
Job23:15 Therefore I am terrified at his presence; when I consider
Job23:16 God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me;
Job23:17 If only I could vanish in darkness
Job24:1 Why are times not kept by the Almighty
Job24:2 The wicked remove landmarks; they seize flocks and pasture them.
Job24:3 They drive away the donkey of the orphan; they take the widow's ox for a pledge.
Job24:4 They thrust the needy off the road; the poor of the earth all hide themselves.
Job24:5 Like wild asses in the desert they go out to their toil
Job24:6 They reap in a field not their own and they glean in the vineyard of the wicked.
Job24:7 They lie all night naked
Job24:8 They are wet with the rain of the mountains
Job24:9 There are those who snatch the orphan child from the breast
Job24:10 They go about naked
Job24:11 between their terraces they press out oil; they tread the wine presses
Job24:12 From the city the dying groan
Job24:13 There are those who rebel against the light
Job24:14 The murderer rises at dusk to kill the poor and needy
Job24:15 The eye of the adulterer also waits for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves up; they do not know the light.
Job24:17 For deep darkness is morning to all of them; for they are friends with the terrors of deep darkness.
Job24:18 Swift are they on the face of the waters; their portion in the land is cursed; no treader turns toward their vineyards.
Job24:19 Drought and heat snatch away the snow waters; so does Sheol those who have sinned.
Job24:20 The womb forgets them; the worm finds them sweet; they are no longer remembered; so wickedness is broken like a tree.
Job24:21 They harm the childless woman
Job24:22 Yet God prolongs the life of the mighty by his power; they rise up when they despair of life.
Job24:23 He gives them security
Job24:24 They are exalted a little while
Job24:25 If it is not so
Job25:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered:
Job25:2 Dominion and fear are with God; he makes peace in his high heaven.
Job25:3 Is there any number to his armies? Upon whom does his light not arise?
Job25:4 How then can a mortal be righteous before God? How can one born of woman be pure?
Job25:5 If even the moon is not bright and the stars are not pure in his sight
Job25:6 how much less a mortal
Job26:1 Then Job answered:
Job26:2 How you have helped one who has no power! How you have assisted the arm that has no strength!
Job26:3 How you have counseled one who has no wisdom
Job26:4 With whose help have you uttered words
Job26:5 The shades below tremble
Job26:6 Sheol is naked before God
Job26:7 He stretches out Zaphon over the void
Job26:8 He binds up the waters in his thick clouds
Job26:9 He covers the face of the full moon
Job26:10 He has described a circle on the face of the waters
Job26:11 The pillars of heaven tremble
Job26:12 By his power he stilled the Sea; by his understanding he struck down Rahab.
Job26:13 By his wind the heavens were made fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
Job26:14 These are indeed but the outskirts of his ways; and how small a whisper do we hear of him! But the thunder of his power who can understand?
Job27:1 Job again took up his discourse and said:
Job27:2 As God lives
Job27:3 as long as my breath is in me and the spirit of God is in my nostrils
Job27:4 my lips will not speak falsehood
Job27:5 Far be it from me to say that you are right; until I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
Job27:6 I hold fast my righteousness
Job27:7 May my enemy be like the wicked
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the godless when God cuts them off
Job27:9 Will God hear their cry when trouble comes upon them?
Job27:10 Will they take delight in the Almighty? Will they call upon God at all times?
Job27:11 I will teach you concerning the hand of God; that which is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
Job27:12 All of you have seen it yourselves; why then have you become altogether vain?
Job27:13 This is the portion of the wicked with God
Job27:14 If their children are multiplied
Job27:15 Those who survive them the pestilence buries
Job27:16 Though they heap up silver like dust
Job27:17 they may pile it up
Job27:18 They build their houses like nests
Job27:19 They go to bed with wealth
Job27:20 Terrors overtake them like a flood; in the night a whirlwind carries them off.
Job27:21 The east wind lifts them up and they are gone; it sweeps them out of their place.
Job27:22 It hurls at them without pity; they flee from its power in headlong flight.
Job27:23 It claps its hands at them
Job28:1 Surely there is a mine for silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken out of the earth
Job28:3 Miners put an end to darkness
Job28:4 They open shafts in a valley away from human habitation; they are forgotten by travelers
Job28:5 As for the earth
Job28:6 Its stones are the place of sapphires
Job28:7 That path no bird of prey knows
Job28:8 The proud wild animals have not trodden it; the lion has not passed over it.
Job28:9 They put their hand to the flinty rock
Job28:10 They cut out channels in the rocks
Job28:11 The sources of the rivers they probe; hidden things they bring to light.
Job28:12 But where shall wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Mortals do not know the way to it
Job28:14 The deep says
Job28:15 It cannot be gotten for gold
Job28:16 It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Gold and glass cannot equal it
Job28:18 No mention shall be made of coral or of crystal; the price of wisdom is above pearls.
Job28:19 The chrysolite of Ethiopia cannot compare with it
Job28:20 Where then does wisdom come from? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 It is hidden from the eyes of all living
Job28:22 Abaddon and Death say
Job28:23 God understands the way to it
Job28:24 For he looks to the ends of the earth
Job28:25 When he gave to the wind its weight
Job28:26 when he made a decree for the rain
Job28:27 then he saw it and declared it; he established it
Job28:28 And he said to humankind
Job29:1 Job again took up his discourse and said:
Job29:2 Oh
Job29:3 when his lamp shone over my head
Job29:4 when I was in my prime
Job29:5 when the Almighty was still with me
Job29:6 when my steps were washed with milk
Job29:7 When I went out to the gate of the city
Job29:8 the young men saw me and withdrew
Job29:9 the nobles refrained from talking
Job29:10 the voices of princes were hushed
Job29:11 When the ear heard
Job29:12 because I delivered the poor who cried
Job29:13 The blessing of the wretched came upon me
Job29:14 I put on righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind
Job29:16 I was a father to the needy
Job29:17 I broke the fangs of the unrighteous
Job29:18 Then I thought
Job29:19 my roots spread out to the waters
Job29:20 my glory was fresh with me
Job29:21 They listened to me
Job29:22 After I spoke they did not speak again
Job29:23 They waited for me as for the rain; they opened their mouths as for the spring rain.
Job29:24 I smiled on them when they had no confidence; and the light of my countenance they did not extinguish.
Job29:25 I chose their way
Job30:1 But now they make sport of me
Job30:2 What could I gain from the strength of their hands? All their vigor is gone.
Job30:3 Through want and hard hunger they gnaw the dry and desolate ground
Job30:4 they pick mallow and the leaves of bushes
Job30:5 They are driven out from society; people shout after them as after a thief.
Job30:6 In the gullies of wadis they must live
Job30:7 Among the bushes they bray; under the nettles they huddle together.
Job30:8 A senseless
Job30:9 And now they mock me in song; I am a byword to them.
Job30:10 They abhor me
Job30:11 Because God has loosed my bowstring and humbled me
Job30:12 On my right hand the rabble rise up; they send me sprawling
Job30:13 They break up my path
Job30:14 As through a wide breach they come; amid the crash they roll on.
Job30:15 Terrors are turned upon me; my honor is pursued as by the wind
Job30:16 And now my soul is poured out within me; days of affliction have taken hold of me.
Job30:17 The night racks my bones
Job30:18 With violence he seizes my garment; he grasps me by the collar of my tunic.
Job30:19 He has cast me into the mire
Job30:20 I cry to you and you do not answer me; I stand
Job30:21 You have turned cruel to me; with the might of your hand you persecute me.
Job30:22 You lift me up on the wind
Job30:23 I know that you will bring me to death
Job30:24 Surely one does not turn against the needy
Job30:25 Did I not weep for those whose day was hard? Was not my soul grieved for the poor?
Job30:26 But when I looked for good
Job30:27 My inward parts are in turmoil
Job30:28 I go about in sunless gloom; I stand up in the assembly and cry for help.
Job30:29 I am a brother of jackals
Job30:30 My skin turns black and falls from me
Job30:31 My lyre is turned to mourning
Job31:1 I have made a covenant with my eyes; how then could I look upon a virgin?
Job31:2 What would be my portion from God above
Job31:3 Does not calamity befall the unrighteous
Job31:4 Does he not see my ways
Job31:5 If I have walked with falsehood
Job31:6 let me be weighed in a just balance
Job31:7 if my step has turned aside from the way
Job31:8 then let me sow
Job31:9 If my heart has been enticed by a woman
Job31:10 then let my wife grind for another
Job31:11 For that would be a heinous crime; that would be a criminal offense;
Job31:12 for that would be a fire consuming down to Abaddon
Job31:13 If I have rejected the cause of my male or female slaves
Job31:14 what then shall I do when God rises up? When he makes inquiry
Job31:15 Did not he who made me in the womb make them? And did not one fashion us in the womb?
Job31:16 If I have withheld anything that the poor desired
Job31:17 or have eaten my morsel alone
Job31:18 for from my youth I reared the orphan like a father
Job31:19 if I have seen anyone perish for lack of clothing
Job31:20 whose loins have not blessed me
Job31:21 if I have raised my hand against the orphan
Job31:22 then let my shoulder blade fall from my shoulder
Job31:23 For I was in terror of calamity from God
Job31:24 If I have made gold my trust
Job31:25 if I have rejoiced because my wealth was great
Job31:26 if I have looked at the sun when it shone
Job31:27 and my heart has been secretly enticed
Job31:28 this also would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges
Job31:29 If I have rejoiced at the ruin of those who hated me
Job31:30 I have not let my mouth sin by asking for their lives with a curse--
Job31:31 if those of my tent ever said
Job31:32 the stranger has not lodged in the street; I have opened my doors to the traveler--
Job31:33 if I have concealed my transgressions as others do
Job31:34 because I stood in great fear of the multitude
Job31:35 Oh
Job31:36 Surely I would carry it on my shoulder; I would bind it on me like a crown;
Job31:37 I would give him an account of all my steps; like a prince I would approach him.
Job31:38 If my land has cried out against me
Job31:39 if I have eaten its yield without payment
Job31:40 let thorns grow instead of wheat
Job32:1 So these three men ceased to answer Job
Job32:2 Then Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite
Job32:3 he was angry also at Job's three friends because they had found no answer
Job32:4 Now Elihu had waited to speak to Job
Job32:5 But when Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouths of these three men
Job32:6 Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite answered: I am young in years
Job32:7 I said
Job32:8 But truly it is the spirit in a mortal
Job32:9 It is not the old that are wise
Job32:10 Therefore I say
Job32:11 See
Job32:12 I gave you my attention
Job32:13 Yet do not say
Job32:14 He has not directed his words against me
Job32:15 They are dismayed
Job32:16 And am I to wait
Job32:17 I also will give my answer; I also will declare my opinion.
Job32:18 For I am full of words; the spirit within me constrains me.
Job32:19 My heart is indeed like wine that has no vent; like new wineskins
Job32:20 I must speak
Job32:21 I will not show partiality to any person or use flattery toward anyone.
Job32:22 For I do not know how to flatter-- or my Maker would soon put an end to me!
Job33:1 But now
Job33:2 See
Job33:3 My words declare the uprightness of my heart
Job33:4 The spirit of God has made me
Job33:5 Answer me
Job33:6 See
Job33:7 No fear of me need terrify you; my pressure will not be heavy on you.
Job33:8 Surely
Job33:9 You say
Job33:10 Look
Job33:11 he puts my feet in the stocks
Job33:12 But in this you are not right. I will answer you: God is greater than any mortal.
Job33:13 Why do you contend against him
Job33:14 For God speaks in one way
Job33:15 In a dream
Job33:16 then he opens their ears
Job33:17 that he may turn them aside from their deeds
Job33:18 to spare their souls from the Pit
Job33:19 They are also chastened with pain upon their beds
Job33:20 so that their lives loathe bread
Job33:21 Their flesh is so wasted away that it cannot be seen; and their bones
Job33:22 Their souls draw near the Pit
Job33:23 Then
Job33:24 and he is gracious to that person
Job33:25 let his flesh become fresh with youth; let him return to the days of his youthful vigor.'
Job33:26 Then he prays to God
Job33:27 That person sings to others and says
Job33:28 He has redeemed my soul from going down to the Pit
Job33:29 God indeed does all these things
Job33:30 to bring back their souls from the Pit
Job33:31 Pay heed
Job33:32 If you have anything to say
Job33:33 If not
Job34:1 Then Elihu continued and said:
Job34:2 Hear my words
Job34:3 for the ear tests words as the palate tastes food.
Job34:4 Let us choose what is right; let us determine among ourselves what is good.
Job34:5 For Job has said
Job34:6 in spite of being right I am counted a liar; my wound is incurable
Job34:7 Who is there like Job
Job34:8 who goes in company with evildoers and walks with the wicked?
Job34:9 For he has said
Job34:10 Therefore
Job34:11 For according to their deeds he will repay them
Job34:12 Of a truth
Job34:13 Who gave him charge over the earth and who laid on him the whole world?
Job34:14 If he should take back his spirit to himself
Job34:15 all flesh would perish together
Job34:16 If you have understanding
Job34:17 Shall one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn one who is righteous and mighty
Job34:18 who says to a king
Job34:19 who shows no partiality to nobles
Job34:20 In a moment they die; at midnight the people are shaken and pass away
Job34:21 For his eyes are upon the ways of mortals
Job34:22 There is no gloom or deep darkness where evildoers may hide themselves.
Job34:23 For he has not appointed a time for anyone to go before God in judgment.
Job34:24 He shatters the mighty without investigation
Job34:25 Thus
Job34:26 He strikes them for their wickedness while others look on
Job34:27 because they turned aside from following him
Job34:28 so that they caused the cry of the poor to come to him
Job34:29 When he is quiet
Job34:30 so that the godless should not reign
Job34:31 For has anyone said to God
Job34:32 teach me what I do not see; if I have done iniquity
Job34:33 Will he then pay back to suit you
Job34:34 Those who have sense will say to me
Job34:35 'Job speaks without knowledge
Job34:36 Would that Job were tried to the limit
Job34:37 For he adds rebellion to his sin; he claps his hands among us
Job35:1 Elihu continued and said:
Job35:2 Do you think this to be just? You say
Job35:3 If you ask
Job35:4 I will answer you and your friends with you.
Job35:5 Look at the heavens and see; observe the clouds
Job35:6 If you have sinned
Job35:7 If you are righteous
Job35:8 Your wickedness affects others like you
Job35:9 Because of the multitude of oppressions people cry out; they call for help because of the arm of the mighty.
Job35:10 But no one says
Job35:11 who teaches us more than the animals of the earth
Job35:12 There they cry out
Job35:13 Surely God does not hear an empty cry
Job35:14 How much less when you say that you do not see him
Job35:15 And now
Job35:16 Job opens his mouth in empty talk
Job36:1 Elihu continued and said:
Job36:2 Bear with me a little
Job36:3 I will bring my knowledge from far away
Job36:4 For truly my words are not false; one who is perfect in knowledge is with you.
Job36:5 Surely God is mighty and does not despise any; he is mighty in strength of understanding.
Job36:6 He does not keep the wicked alive
Job36:7 He does not withdraw his eyes from the righteous
Job36:8 And if they are bound in fetters and caught in the cords of affliction
Job36:9 then he declares to them their work and their transgressions
Job36:10 He opens their ears to instruction
Job36:11 If they listen
Job36:12 But if they do not listen
Job36:13 The godless in heart cherish anger; they do not cry for help when he binds them.
Job36:14 They die in their youth
Job36:15 He delivers the afflicted by their affliction
Job36:16 He also allured you out of distress into a broad place where there was no constraint
Job36:17 But you are obsessed with the case of the wicked; judgment and justice seize you.
Job36:18 Beware that wrath does not entice you into scoffing
Job36:19 Will your cry avail to keep you from distress
Job36:20 Do not long for the night
Job36:21 Beware! Do not turn to iniquity; because of that you have been tried by affliction.
Job36:22 See
Job36:23 Who has prescribed for him his way
Job36:24 Remember to extol his work
Job36:25 All people have looked on it; everyone watches it from far away.
Job36:26 Surely God is great
Job36:27 For he draws up the drops of water; he distills his mist in rain
Job36:28 which the skies pour down and drop upon mortals abundantly.
Job36:29 Can anyone understand the spreading of the clouds
Job36:30 See
Job36:31 For by these he governs peoples; he gives food in abundance.
Job36:32 He covers his hands with the lightning
Job36:33 Its crashing tells about him; he is jealous with anger against iniquity.
Job37:1 At this also my heart trembles
Job37:2 Listen
Job37:3 Under the whole heaven he lets it loose
Job37:4 After it his voice roars; he thunders with his majestic voice and he does not restrain the lightnings when his voice is heard.
Job37:5 God thunders wondrously with his voice; he does great things that we cannot comprehend.
Job37:6 For to the snow he says
Job37:7 serves as a sign on everyone's hand
Job37:8 Then the animals go into their lairs and remain in their dens.
Job37:9 From its chamber comes the whirlwind
Job37:10 By the breath of God ice is given
Job37:11 He loads the thick cloud with moisture; the clouds scatter his lightning.
Job37:12 They turn round and round by his guidance
Job37:13 Whether for correction
Job37:14 Hear this
Job37:15 Do you know how God lays his command upon them
Job37:16 Do you know the balancings of the clouds
Job37:17 you whose garments are hot when the earth is still because of the south wind?
Job37:18 Can you
Job37:19 Teach us what we shall say to him; we cannot draw up our case because of darkness.
Job37:20 Should he be told that I want to speak? Did anyone ever wish to be swallowed up?
Job37:21 Now
Job37:22 Out of the north comes golden splendor; around God is awesome majesty.
Job37:23 The Almighty-- we cannot find him; he is great in power and justice
Job37:24 Therefore mortals fear him; he does not regard any who are wise in their own conceit.
Job38:1 Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind:
Job38:2 Who is this that darkens counsel by words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Gird up your loins like a man
Job38:4 Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth? Tell me
Job38:5 Who determined its measurements-- surely you know! Or who stretched the line upon it?
Job38:6 On what were its bases sunk
Job38:7 when the morning stars sang together and all the heavenly beings shouted for joy?
Job38:8 Or who shut in the sea with doors when it burst out from the womb?--
Job38:9 when I made the clouds its garment
Job38:10 and prescribed bounds for it
Job38:11 and said
Job38:12 Have you commanded the morning since your days began
Job38:13 so that it might take hold of the skirts of the earth
Job38:14 It is changed like clay under the seal
Job38:15 Light is withheld from the wicked
Job38:16 Have you entered into the springs of the sea
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed to you
Job38:18 Have you comprehended the expanse of the earth? Declare
Job38:19 Where is the way to the dwelling of light
Job38:20 that you may take it to its territory and that you may discern the paths to its home?
Job38:21 Surely you know
Job38:22 Have you entered the storehouses of the snow
Job38:23 which I have reserved for the time of trouble
Job38:24 What is the way to the place where the light is distributed
Job38:25 Who has cut a channel for the torrents of rain
Job38:26 to bring rain on a land where no one lives
Job38:27 to satisfy the waste and desolate land
Job38:28 Has the rain a father
Job38:29 From whose womb did the ice come forth
Job38:30 The waters become hard like stone
Job38:31 Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades
Job38:32 Can you lead forth the Mazzaroth in their season
Job38:33 Do you know the ordinances of the heavens? Can you establish their rule on the earth?
Job38:34 Can you lift up your voice to the clouds
Job38:35 Can you send forth lightnings
Job38:36 Who has put wisdom in the inward parts
Job38:37 Who has the wisdom to number the clouds? Or who can tilt the waterskins of the heavens
Job38:38 when the dust runs into a mass and the clods cling together?
Job38:39 Can you hunt the prey for the lion
Job38:40 when they crouch in their dens
Job38:41 Who provides for the raven its prey
Job39:1 Do you know when the mountain goats give birth? Do you observe the calving of the deer?
Job39:2 Can you number the months that they fulfill
Job39:3 when they crouch to give birth to their offspring
Job39:4 Their young ones become strong
Job39:5 Who has let the wild ass go free? Who has loosed the bonds of the swift ass
Job39:6 to which I have given the steppe for its home
Job39:7 It scorns the tumult of the city; it does not hear the shouts of the driver.
Job39:8 It ranges the mountains as its pasture
Job39:9 Is the wild ox willing to serve you? Will it spend the night at your crib?
Job39:10 Can you tie it in the furrow with ropes
Job39:11 Will you depend on it because its strength is great
Job39:12 Do you have faith in it that it will return
Job39:13 The ostrich's wings flap wildly
Job39:14 For it leaves its eggs to the earth
Job39:15 forgetting that a foot may crush them
Job39:16 It deals cruelly with its young
Job39:17 because God has made it forget wisdom
Job39:18 When it spreads its plumes aloft
Job39:19 Do you give the horse its might? Do you clothe its neck with mane?
Job39:20 Do you make it leap like the locust? Its majestic snorting is terrible.
Job39:21 It paws violently
Job39:22 It laughs at fear
Job39:23 Upon it rattle the quiver
Job39:24 With fierceness and rage it swallows the ground; it cannot stand still at the sound of the trumpet.
Job39:25 When the trumpet sounds
Job39:26 Is it by your wisdom that the hawk soars
Job39:27 Is it at your command that the eagle mounts up and makes its nest on high?
Job39:28 It lives on the rock and makes its home in the fastness of the rocky crag.
Job39:29 From there it spies the prey; its eyes see it from far away.
Job39:30 Its young ones suck up blood; and where the slain are
Job40:1 And the LORD said to Job:
Job40:2 Shall a faultfinder contend with the Almighty? Anyone who argues with God must respond.
Job40:3 Then Job answered the LORD:
Job40:4 See
Job40:5 I have spoken once
Job40:6 Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind:
Job40:7 Gird up your loins like a man; I will question you
Job40:8 Will you even put me in the wrong? Will you condemn me that you may be justified?
Job40:9 Have you an arm like God
Job40:10 Deck yourself with majesty and dignity; clothe yourself with glory and splendor.
Job40:11 Pour out the overflowings of your anger
Job40:12 Look on all who are proud
Job40:13 Hide them all in the dust together; bind their faces in the world below.
Job40:14 Then I will also acknowledge to you that your own right hand can give you victory.
Job40:15 Look at Behemoth
Job40:16 Its strength is in its loins
Job40:17 It makes its tail stiff like a cedar; the sinews of its thighs are knit together.
Job40:18 Its bones are tubes of bronze
Job40:19 It is the first of the great acts of God-- only its Maker can approach it with the sword.
Job40:20 For the mountains yield food for it where all the wild animals play.
Job40:21 Under the lotus plants it lies
Job40:22 The lotus trees cover it for shade; the willows of the wadi surround it.
Job40:23 Even if the river is turbulent
Job40:24 Can one take it with hooks or pierce its nose with a snare?
Job41:1 Can you draw out Leviathan with a fishhook
Job41:2 Can you put a rope in its nose
Job41:3 Will it make many supplications to you? Will it speak soft words to you?
Job41:4 Will it make a covenant with you to be taken as your servant forever?
Job41:5 Will you play with it as with a bird
Job41:6 Will traders bargain over it? Will they divide it up among the merchants?
Job41:7 Can you fill its skin with harpoons
Job41:8 Lay hands on it; think of the battle; you will not do it again!
Job41:9 Any hope of capturing it will be disappointed; were not even the gods overwhelmed at the sight of it?
Job41:10 No one is so fierce as to dare to stir it up. Who can stand before it?
Job41:11 Who can confront it and be safe?-- under the whole heaven
Job41:12 I will not keep silence concerning its limbs
Job41:13 Who can strip off its outer garment? Who can penetrate its double coat of mail?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of its face? There is terror all around its teeth.
Job41:15 Its back is made of shields in rows
Job41:16 One is so near to another that no air can come between them.
Job41:17 They are joined one to another; they clasp each other and cannot be separated.
Job41:18 Its sneezes flash forth light
Job41:19 From its mouth go flaming torches; sparks of fire leap out.
Job41:20 Out of its nostrils comes smoke
Job41:21 Its breath kindles coals
Job41:22 In its neck abides strength
Job41:23 The folds of its flesh cling together; it is firmly cast and immovable.
Job41:24 Its heart is as hard as stone
Job41:25 When it raises itself up the gods are afraid; at the crashing they are beside themselves.
Job41:26 Though the sword reaches it
Job41:27 It counts iron as straw
Job41:28 The arrow cannot make it flee; slingstones
Job41:29 Clubs are counted as chaff; it laughs at the rattle of javelins.
Job41:30 Its underparts are like sharp potsherds; it spreads itself like a threshing sledge on the mire.
Job41:31 It makes the deep boil like a pot; it makes the sea like a pot of ointment.
Job41:32 It leaves a shining wake behind it; one would think the deep to be white-haired.
Job41:33 On earth it has no equal
Job41:34 It surveys everything that is lofty; it is king over all that are proud.
Job42:1 Then Job answered the LORD:
Job42:2 I know that you can do all things
Job42:3 'Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge?' Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand
Job42:4 'Hear
Job42:5 I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear
Job42:6 therefore I despise myself
Job42:7 After the LORD had spoken these words to Job
Job42:8 Now therefore take seven bulls and seven rams
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did what the LORD had told them; and the LORD accepted Job's prayer.
Job42:10 And the LORD restored the fortunes of Job when he had prayed for his friends; and the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.
Job42:11 Then there came to him all his brothers and sisters and all who had known him before
Job42:12 The LORD blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning; and he had fourteen thousand sheep
Job42:13 He also had seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 He named the first Jemimah
Job42:15 In all the land there were no women so beautiful as Job's daughters; and their father gave them an inheritance along with their brothers.
Job42:16 After this Job lived one hundred and forty years
Job42:17 And Job died

Psalms1:2 but their delight is in the law of the LORD

Psalms1:4 The wicked are not so
Psalms1:5 Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment
Psalms1:6 for the LORD watches over the way of the righteous

Psalms2:2 The kings of the earth set themselves

Psalms6:9 The LORD has heard my supplication; the LORD accepts my prayer.

Psalms8:4 what are human beings that you are mindful of them

Psalms8:6 You have given them dominion over the works of your hands; you have put all things under their feet

Psalms9:3 When my enemies turned back
Psalms9:4 For you have maintained my just cause; you have sat on the throne giving righteous judgment.

Psalms9:6 The enemies have vanished in everlasting ruins; their cities you have rooted out; the very memory of them has perished.
Psalms9:7 But the LORD sits enthroned forever
Psalms9:8 He judges the world with righteousness; he judges the peoples with equity.
Psalms9:9 The LORD is a stronghold for the oppressed
Psalms9:10 And those who know your name put their trust in you
Psalms9:11 Sing praises to the LORD
Psalms9:12 For he who avenges blood is mindful of them; he does not forget the cry of the afflicted.

Psalms9:14 so that I may recount all your praises

Psalms10:4 In the pride of their countenance the wicked say
Psalms10:5 Their ways prosper at all times; your judgments are on high
Psalms10:6 They think in their heart
Psalms10:7 Their mouths are filled with cursing and deceit and oppression; under their tongues are mischief and iniquity.
Psalms10:8 They sit in ambush in the villages; in hiding places they murder the innocent. Their eyes stealthily watch for the helpless;
Psalms10:9 they lurk in secret like a lion in its covert; they lurk that they may seize the poor; they seize the poor and drag them off in their net.
Psalms10:10 They stoop
Psalms10:11 They think in their heart
Psalms10:12 Rise up
Psalms10:13 Why do the wicked renounce God
Psalms10:14 But you do see! Indeed you note trouble and grief

Psalms10:16 The LORD is king forever and ever; the nations shall perish from his land.

Psalms10:18 to do justice for the orphan and the oppressed

Psalms18:3 I call upon the LORD
Psalms18:4 The cords of death encompassed me; the torrents of perdition assailed me;
Psalms18:5 the cords of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.
Psalms18:6 In my distress I called upon the LORD; to my God I cried for help. From his temple he heard my voice
Psalms18:7 Then the earth reeled and rocked; the foundations also of the mountains trembled and quaked
Psalms18:8 Smoke went up from his nostrils
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens
Psalms18:10 He rode on a cherub
Psalms18:11 He made darkness his covering around him
Psalms18:12 Out of the brightness before him there broke through his clouds hailstones and coals of fire.
Psalms18:13 The LORD also thundered in the heavens
Psalms18:14 And he sent out his arrows
Psalms18:15 Then the channels of the sea were seen
Psalms18:16 He reached down from on high
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy
Psalms18:18 They confronted me in the day of my calamity; but the LORD was my support.
Psalms18:19 He brought me out into a broad place; he delivered me
Psalms18:20 The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands he recompensed me.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of the LORD
Psalms18:22 For all his ordinances were before me
Psalms18:23 I was blameless before him
Psalms18:24 Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness
Psalms18:25 With the loyal you show yourself loyal; with the blameless you show yourself blameless;
Psalms18:26 with the pure you show yourself pure; and with the crooked you show yourself perverse.
Psalms18:27 For you deliver a humble people
Psalms18:28 It is you who light my lamp; the LORD
Psalms18:29 By you I can crush a troop
Psalms18:30 This God-- his way is perfect; the promise of the LORD proves true; he is a shield for all who take refuge in him.
Psalms18:31 For who is God except the LORD? And who is a rock besides our God?--
Psalms18:32 the God who girded me with strength
Psalms18:33 He made my feet like the feet of a deer
Psalms18:34 He trains my hands for war
Psalms18:35 You have given me the shield of your salvation
Psalms18:36 You gave me a wide place for my steps under me
Psalms18:37 I pursued my enemies and overtook them; and did not turn back until they were consumed.
Psalms18:38 I struck them down
Psalms18:39 For you girded me with strength for the battle; you made my assailants sink under me.
Psalms18:40 You made my enemies turn their backs to me
Psalms18:41 They cried for help
Psalms18:42 I beat them fine
Psalms18:43 You delivered me from strife with the peoples; you made me head of the nations; people whom I had not known served me.
Psalms18:44 As soon as they heard of me they obeyed me; foreigners came cringing to me.
Psalms18:45 Foreigners lost heart

Psalms18:48 who delivered me from my enemies; indeed

Psalms22:3 Yet you are holy
Psalms22:4 In you our ancestors trusted; they trusted
Psalms22:5 To you they cried
Psalms22:6 But I am a worm
Psalms22:7 All who see me mock at me; they make mouths at me

Psalms22:10 On you I was cast from my birth
Psalms22:11 Do not be far from me

Psalms22:13 they open wide their mouths at me

Psalms22:19 But you

Psalms35:6 Let their way be dark and slippery
Psalms35:7 For without cause they hid their net for me; without cause they dug a pit for my life.

Psalms38:1 O LORD
Psalms38:2 For your arrows have sunk into me
Psalms38:3 There is no soundness in my flesh because of your indignation; there is no health in my bones because of my sin.
Psalms38:4 For my iniquities have gone over my head; they weigh like a burden too heavy for me.
Psalms38:5 My wounds grow foul and fester because of my foolishness;
Psalms38:6 I am utterly bowed down and prostrate; all day long I go around mourning.
Psalms38:7 For my loins are filled with burning
Psalms38:8 I am utterly spent and crushed; I groan because of the tumult of my heart.
Psalms38:9 O Lord
Psalms38:10 My heart throbs
Psalms38:11 My friends and companions stand aloof from my affliction
Psalms38:12 Those who seek my life lay their snares; those who seek to hurt me speak of ruin
Psalms38:13 But I am like the deaf
Psalms38:14 Truly
Psalms38:15 But it is for you
Psalms38:16 For I pray
Psalms38:17 For I am ready to fall
Psalms38:18 I confess my iniquity; I am sorry for my sin.
Psalms38:19 Those who are my foes without cause are mighty
Psalms38:20 Those who render me evil for good are my adversaries because I follow after good.
Psalms38:21 Do not forsake me
Psalms38:22 make haste to help me
Psalms39:1 I said
Psalms39:2 I was silent and still; I held my peace to no avail; my distress grew worse
Psalms39:3 my heart became hot within me. While I mused

Psalms39:6 Surely everyone goes about like a shadow. Surely for nothing they are in turmoil; they heap up
Psalms39:7 And now

Psalms39:11 You chastise mortals in punishment for sin

Psalms39:13 Turn your gaze away from me
Psalms40:1 I waited patiently for the LORD; he inclined to me and heard my cry.
Psalms40:2 He drew me up from the desolate pit
Psalms40:3 He put a new song in my mouth
Psalms40:4 Happy are those who make the LORD their trust
Psalms40:5 You have multiplied
Psalms40:6 Sacrifice and offering you do not desire
Psalms40:7 Then I said
Psalms40:8 I delight to do your will
Psalms40:9 I have told the glad news of deliverance in the great congregation; see
Psalms40:10 I have not hidden your saving help within my heart
Psalms40:11 Do not
Psalms40:12 For evils have encompassed me without number; my iniquities have overtaken me
Psalms40:13 Be pleased

Psalms40:17 As for me
Psalms41:1 Happy are those who consider the poor; the LORD delivers them in the day of trouble.
Psalms41:2 The LORD protects them and keeps them alive; they are called happy in the land. You do not give them up to the will of their enemies.
Psalms41:3 The LORD sustains them on their sickbed; in their illness you heal all their infirmities.
Psalms41:4 As for me
Psalms41:5 My enemies wonder in malice when I will die
Psalms41:6 And when they come to see me
Psalms41:7 All who hate me whisper together about me; they imagine the worst for me.
Psalms41:8 They think that a deadly thing has fastened on me
Psalms41:9 Even my bosom friend in whom I trusted
Psalms41:10 But you
Psalms41:11 By this I know that you are pleased with me; because my enemy has not triumphed over me.
Psalms41:12 But you have upheld me because of my integrity
Psalms41:13 Blessed be the LORD

Psalms42:2 My soul thirsts for God
Psalms42:3 My tears have been my food day and night

Psalms43:2 For you are the God in whom I take refuge; why have you cast me off? Why must I walk about mournfully because of the oppression of the enemy?

Psalms44:1 We have heard with our ears
Psalms44:2 you with your own hand drove out the nations
Psalms44:3 for not by their own sword did they win the land
Psalms44:4 You are my King and my God; you command victories for Jacob.
Psalms44:5 Through you we push down our foes; through your name we tread down our assailants.
Psalms44:6 For not in my bow do I trust
Psalms44:7 But you have saved us from our foes
Psalms44:8 In God we have boasted continually
Psalms44:9 Yet you have rejected us and abased us
Psalms44:10 You made us turn back from the foe
Psalms44:11 You have made us like sheep for slaughter
Psalms44:12 You have sold your people for a trifle
Psalms44:13 You have made us the taunt of our neighbors
Psalms44:14 You have made us a byword among the nations
Psalms44:15 All day long my disgrace is before me
Psalms44:16 at the words of the taunters and revilers
Psalms44:17 All this has come upon us
Psalms44:18 Our heart has not turned back
Psalms44:19 yet you have broken us in the haunt of jackals
Psalms44:20 If we had forgotten the name of our God
Psalms44:21 would not God discover this? For he knows the secrets of the heart.
Psalms44:22 Because of you we are being killed all day long
Psalms44:23 Rouse yourself! Why do you sleep
Psalms44:24 Why do you hide your face? Why do you forget our affliction and oppression?
Psalms44:25 For we sink down to the dust; our bodies cling to the ground.

Psalms45:4 In your majesty ride on victoriously for the cause of truth and to defend the right; let your right hand teach you dread deeds.

Psalms45:7 you love righteousness and hate wickedness. Therefore God

Psalms69:1 Save me

Psalms69:3 I am weary with my crying; my throat is parched. My eyes grow dim with waiting for my God.

Psalms69:11 When I made sackcloth my clothing
Psalms69:12 I am the subject of gossip for those who sit in the gate
Psalms69:13 But as for me
Psalms69:14 rescue me from sinking in the mire; let me be delivered from my enemies and from the deep waters.
Psalms69:15 Do not let the flood sweep over me
Psalms69:16 Answer me
Psalms69:17 Do not hide your face from your servant
Psalms69:18 Draw near to me
Psalms69:19 You know the insults I receive
Psalms69:20 Insults have broken my heart
Psalms69:21 They gave me poison for food
Psalms69:22 Let their table be a trap for them
Psalms69:23 Let their eyes be darkened so that they cannot see
Psalms69:24 Pour out your indignation upon them
Psalms69:25 May their camp be a desolation; let no one live in their tents.
Psalms69:26 For they persecute those whom you have struck down
Psalms69:27 Add guilt to their guilt; may they have no acquittal from you.
Psalms69:28 Let them be blotted out of the book of the living; let them not be enrolled among the righteous.
Psalms69:29 But I am lowly and in pain; let your salvation
Psalms69:30 I will praise the name of God with a song; I will magnify him with thanksgiving.
Psalms69:31 This will please the LORD more than an ox or a bull with horns and hoofs.
Psalms69:32 Let the oppressed see it and be glad; you who seek God
Psalms69:33 For the LORD hears the needy
Psalms69:34 Let heaven and earth praise him
Psalms69:35 For God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah; and his servants shall live there and possess it;

Psalms72:2 May he judge your people with righteousness
Psalms72:3 May the mountains yield prosperity for the people
Psalms72:4 May he defend the cause of the poor of the people
Psalms72:5 May he live while the sun endures
Psalms72:6 May he be like rain that falls on the mown grass
Psalms72:7 In his days may righteousness flourish and peace abound
Psalms72:8 May he have dominion from sea to sea
Psalms72:9 May his foes bow down before him
Psalms72:10 May the kings of Tarshish and of the isles render him tribute
Psalms72:11 May all kings fall down before him
Psalms72:12 For he delivers the needy when they call
Psalms72:13 He has pity on the weak and the needy
Psalms72:14 From oppression and violence he redeems their life; and precious is their blood in his sight.
Psalms72:15 Long may he live! May gold of Sheba be given to him. May prayer be made for him continually
Psalms72:16 May there be abundance of grain in the land; may it wave on the tops of the mountains; may its fruit be like Lebanon; and may people blossom in the cities like the grass of the field.
Psalms72:17 May his name endure forever
Psalms72:18 Blessed be the LORD
Psalms72:19 Blessed be his glorious name forever; may his glory fill the whole earth. Amen and Amen.

Psalms73:1 Truly God is good to the upright
Psalms73:2 But as for me
Psalms73:3 For I was envious of the arrogant; I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
Psalms73:4 For they have no pain; their bodies are sound and sleek.
Psalms73:5 They are not in trouble as others are; they are not plagued like other people.
Psalms73:6 Therefore pride is their necklace; violence covers them like a garment.
Psalms73:7 Their eyes swell out with fatness; their hearts overflow with follies.
Psalms73:8 They scoff and speak with malice; loftily they threaten oppression.
Psalms73:9 They set their mouths against heaven
Psalms73:10 Therefore the people turn and praise them
Psalms73:11 And they say
Psalms73:12 Such are the wicked; always at ease
Psalms73:13 All in vain I have kept my heart clean and washed my hands in innocence.
Psalms73:14 For all day long I have been plagued
Psalms73:15 If I had said

Psalms73:18 Truly you set them in slippery places; you make them fall to ruin.
Psalms73:19 How they are destroyed in a moment
Psalms73:20 They are like a dream when one awakes; on awaking you despise their phantoms.
Psalms73:21 When my soul was embittered
Psalms73:22 I was stupid and ignorant; I was like a brute beast toward you.
Psalms73:23 Nevertheless I am continually with you; you hold my right hand.
Psalms73:24 You guide me with your counsel
Psalms73:25 Whom have I in heaven but you? And there is nothing on earth that I desire other than you.

Psalms73:27 Indeed
Psalms73:28 But for me it is good to be near God; I have made the Lord GOD my refuge

Psalms77:1 I cry aloud to God
Psalms77:2 In the day of my trouble I seek the Lord; in the night my hand is stretched out without wearying; my soul refuses to be comforted.
Psalms77:3 I think of God
Psalms77:4 You keep my eyelids from closing; I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
Psalms77:5 I consider the days of old
Psalms77:6 I commune with my heart in the night; I meditate and search my spirit:
Psalms77:7 Will the Lord spurn forever
Psalms77:8 Has his steadfast love ceased forever? Are his promises at an end for all time?
Psalms77:9 Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has he in anger shut up his compassion? Selah
Psalms77:10 And I say
Psalms77:11 I will call to mind the deeds of the LORD; I will remember your wonders of old.

Psalms77:13 Your way

Psalms77:19 Your way was through the sea

Psalms78:1 Give ear
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter dark sayings from of old
Psalms78:3 things that we have heard and known

Psalms78:5 He established a decree in Jacob
Psalms78:6 that the next generation might know them
Psalms78:7 so that they should set their hope in God
Psalms78:8 and that they should not be like their ancestors
Psalms78:9 The Ephraimites
Psalms78:10 They did not keep God's covenant
Psalms78:11 They forgot what he had done
Psalms78:12 In the sight of their ancestors he worked marvels in the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea and let them pass through it
Psalms78:14 In the daytime he led them with a cloud
Psalms78:15 He split rocks open in the wilderness
Psalms78:16 He made streams come out of the rock
Psalms78:17 Yet they sinned still more against him
Psalms78:18 They tested God in their heart by demanding the food they craved.
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God
Psalms78:20 Even though he struck the rock so that water gushed out and torrents overflowed
Psalms78:21 Therefore
Psalms78:22 because they had no faith in God
Psalms78:23 Yet he commanded the skies above
Psalms78:24 he rained down on them manna to eat
Psalms78:25 Mortals ate of the bread of angels; he sent them food in abundance.
Psalms78:26 He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens
Psalms78:27 he rained flesh upon them like dust
Psalms78:28 he let them fall within their camp
Psalms78:29 And they ate and were well filled
Psalms78:30 But before they had satisfied their craving
Psalms78:31 the anger of God rose against them and he killed the strongest of them
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this they still sinned; they did not believe in his wonders.
Psalms78:33 So he made their days vanish like a breath
Psalms78:34 When he killed them
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 But they flattered him with their mouths; they lied to him with their tongues.
Psalms78:37 Their heart was not steadfast toward him; they were not true to his covenant.
Psalms78:38 Yet he
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were but flesh
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness and grieved him in the desert!
Psalms78:41 They tested God again and again
Psalms78:42 They did not keep in mind his power
Psalms78:43 when he displayed his signs in Egypt
Psalms78:44 He turned their rivers to blood
Psalms78:45 He sent among them swarms of flies
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to the caterpillar
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their vines with hail
Psalms78:48 He gave over their cattle to the hail
Psalms78:49 He let loose on them his fierce anger
Psalms78:50 He made a path for his anger; he did not spare them from death
Psalms78:51 He struck all the firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 Then he led out his people like sheep
Psalms78:53 He led them in safety
Psalms78:54 And he brought them to his holy hill
Psalms78:55 He drove out nations before them; he apportioned them for a possession and settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.
Psalms78:56 Yet they tested the Most High God
Psalms78:57 but turned away and were faithless like their ancestors; they twisted like a treacherous bow.
Psalms78:58 For they provoked him to anger with their high places; they moved him to jealousy with their idols.
Psalms78:59 When God heard
Psalms78:60 He abandoned his dwelling at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 and delivered his power to captivity
Psalms78:62 He gave his people to the sword
Psalms78:63 Fire devoured their young men
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as from sleep
Psalms78:66 He put his adversaries to rout; he put them to everlasting disgrace.

Psalms78:68 but he chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 He built his sanctuary like the high heavens

Psalms78:72 With upright heart he tended them

Psalms89:31 if they violate my statutes and do not keep my commandments

Psalms89:33 but I will not remove from him my steadfast love
Psalms89:34 I will not violate my covenant
Psalms89:35 Once and for all I have sworn by my holiness; I will not lie to David.
Psalms89:36 His line shall continue forever
Psalms89:37 It shall be established forever like the moon
Psalms89:38 But now you have spurned and rejected him; you are full of wrath against your anointed.
Psalms89:39 You have renounced the covenant with your servant; you have defiled his crown in the dust.
Psalms89:40 You have broken through all his walls; you have laid his strongholds in ruins.
Psalms89:41 All who pass by plunder him; he has become the scorn of his neighbors.

Psalms89:43 Moreover

Psalms90:1 Lord
Psalms90:2 Before the mountains were brought forth
Psalms90:3 You turn us back to dust
Psalms90:4 For a thousand years in your sight are like yesterday when it is past
Psalms90:5 You sweep them away; they are like a dream

Psalms147:1 Praise the LORD! How good it is to sing praises to our God; for he is gracious
Psalms147:2 The LORD builds up Jerusalem; he gathers the outcasts of Israel.
Psalms147:3 He heals the brokenhearted
Psalms147:4 He determines the number of the stars; he gives to all of them their names.
Psalms147:5 Great is our Lord
Psalms147:6 The LORD lifts up the downtrodden; he casts the wicked to the ground.
Psalms147:7 Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; make melody to our God on the lyre.
Psalms147:8 He covers the heavens with clouds
Psalms147:9 He gives to the animals their food
Psalms147:10 His delight is not in the strength of the horse
Psalms147:11 but the LORD takes pleasure in those who fear him
Psalms147:12 Praise the LORD
Psalms147:13 For he strengthens the bars of your gates; he blesses your children within you.
Psalms147:14 He grants peace within your borders; he fills you with the finest of wheat.
Psalms147:15 He sends out his command to the earth; his word runs swiftly.
Psalms147:16 He gives snow like wool; he scatters frost like ashes.
Psalms147:17 He hurls down hail like crumbs-- who can stand before his cold?
Psalms147:18 He sends out his word
Psalms147:19 He declares his word to Jacob
Psalms147:20 He has not dealt thus with any other nation; they do not know his ordinances. Praise the LORD!

Proverbs1:1 The proverbs of Solomon son of David
Proverbs1:2 For learning about wisdom and instruction
Proverbs1:3 for gaining instruction in wise dealing
Proverbs1:4 to teach shrewdness to the simple
Proverbs1:5 Let the wise also hear and gain in learning
Proverbs1:6 to understand a proverb and a figure
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and instruction.
Proverbs1:8 Hear
Proverbs1:9 for they are a fair garland for your head
Proverbs1:10 My child
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 like Sheol let us swallow them alive and whole
Proverbs1:13 We shall find all kinds of costly things; we shall fill our houses with booty.
Proverbs1:14 Throw in your lot among us; we will all have one purse--
Proverbs1:15 my child
Proverbs1:16 for their feet run to evil
Proverbs1:17 For in vain is the net baited while the bird is looking on;
Proverbs1:18 yet they lie in wait-- to kill themselves! and set an ambush-- for their own lives!
Proverbs1:19 Such is the end of all who are greedy for gain; it takes away the life of its possessors.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom cries out in the street; in the squares she raises her voice.
Proverbs1:21 At the busiest corner she cries out; at the entrance of the city gates she speaks:
Proverbs1:22 How long
Proverbs1:23 Give heed to my reproof; I will pour out my thoughts to you; I will make my words known to you.
Proverbs1:24 Because I have called and you refused
Proverbs1:25 and because you have ignored all my counsel and would have none of my reproof
Proverbs1:26 I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when panic strikes you
Proverbs1:27 when panic strikes you like a storm
Proverbs1:28 Then they will call upon me
Proverbs1:29 Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the LORD
Proverbs1:30 would have none of my counsel
Proverbs1:31 therefore they shall eat the fruit of their way and be sated with their own devices.
Proverbs1:32 For waywardness kills the simple
Proverbs1:33 but those who listen to me will be secure and will live at ease
Proverbs2:1 My child
Proverbs2:2 making your ear attentive to wisdom and inclining your heart to understanding;
Proverbs2:3 if you indeed cry out for insight
Proverbs2:4 if you seek it like silver
Proverbs2:5 then you will understand the fear of the LORD and find the knowledge of God.
Proverbs2:6 For the LORD gives wisdom; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding;
Proverbs2:7 he stores up sound wisdom for the upright; he is a shield to those who walk blamelessly
Proverbs2:8 guarding the paths of justice and preserving the way of his faithful ones.
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand righteousness and justice and equity
Proverbs2:10 for wisdom will come into your heart
Proverbs2:11 prudence will watch over you; and understanding will guard you.
Proverbs2:12 It will save you from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 who forsake the paths of uprightness to walk in the ways of darkness
Proverbs2:14 who rejoice in doing evil and delight in the perverseness of evil;
Proverbs2:15 those whose paths are crooked
Proverbs2:16 You will be saved from the loose woman
Proverbs2:17 who forsakes the partner of her youth and forgets her sacred covenant;
Proverbs2:18 for her way leads down to death
Proverbs2:19 those who go to her never come back
Proverbs2:20 Therefore walk in the way of the good
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will abide in the land
Proverbs2:22 but the wicked will be cut off from the land

Proverbs3:2 for length of days and years of life and abundant welfare they will give you.
Proverbs3:3 Do not let loyalty and faithfulness forsake you; bind them around your neck
Proverbs3:4 So you will find favor and good repute in the sight of God and of people.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the LORD with all your heart
Proverbs3:6 In all your ways acknowledge him

Proverbs3:10 then your barns will be filled with plenty

Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than jewels
Proverbs3:16 Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are ways of pleasantness
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her; those who hold her fast are called happy.

Proverbs3:28 Do not say to your neighbor

Proverbs7:7 and I saw among the simple ones
Proverbs7:8 passing along the street near her corner
Proverbs7:9 in the twilight
Proverbs7:10 Then a woman comes toward him

Proverbs7:12 now in the street
Proverbs7:13 She seizes him and kisses him
Proverbs7:14 I had to offer sacrifices
Proverbs7:15 so now I have come out to meet you
Proverbs7:16 I have decked my couch with coverings
Proverbs7:17 I have perfumed my bed with myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey.
Proverbs7:20 He took a bag of money with him; he will not come home until full moon.

Proverbs7:22 Right away he follows her
Proverbs7:23 until an arrow pierces its entrails. He is like a bird rushing into a snare

Proverbs7:27 Her house is the way to Sheol

Proverbs8:36 but those who miss me injure themselves; all who hate me love death.

Proverbs27:8 Like a bird that strays from its nest is one who strays from home.
Proverbs27:9 Perfume and incense make the heart glad
Proverbs27:10 Do not forsake your friend or the friend of your parent; do not go to the house of your kindred in the day of your calamity. Better is a neighbor who is nearby than kindred who are far away.
Proverbs27:11 Be wise
Proverbs27:12 The clever see danger and hide; but the simple go on
Proverbs27:13 Take the garment of one who has given surety for a stranger; seize the pledge given as surety for foreigners.
Proverbs27:14 Whoever blesses a neighbor with a loud voice
Proverbs27:15 A continual dripping on a rainy day and a contentious wife are alike;
Proverbs27:16 to restrain her is to restrain the wind or to grasp oil in the right hand.
Proverbs27:17 Iron sharpens iron
Proverbs27:18 Anyone who tends a fig tree will eat its fruit
Proverbs27:19 Just as water reflects the face

Proverbs27:26 the lambs will provide your clothing

Proverbs28:1 The wicked flee when no one pursues
Proverbs28:2 When a land rebels it has many rulers; but with an intelligent ruler there is lasting order.

Proverbs28:5 The evil do not understand justice

Proverbs30:1 The words of Agur son of Jakeh. An oracle. Thus says the man: I am weary
Proverbs30:2 Surely I am too stupid to be human; I do not have human understanding.
Proverbs30:3 I have not learned wisdom
Proverbs30:4 Who has ascended to heaven and come down? Who has gathered the wind in the hollow of the hand? Who has wrapped up the waters in a garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is the person's name? And what is the name of the person's child? Surely you know!
Proverbs30:5 Every word of God proves true; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
Proverbs30:6 Do not add to his words
Proverbs30:7 Two things I ask of you; do not deny them to me before I die:
Proverbs30:8 Remove far from me falsehood and lying; give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with the food that I need
Proverbs30:9 or I shall be full
Proverbs30:10 Do not slander a servant to a master

Proverbs30:14 There are those whose teeth are swords

Proverbs30:16 Sheol

Proverbs30:19 the way of an eagle in the sky
Proverbs30:20 This is the way of an adulteress: she eats
Proverbs30:21 Under three things the earth trembles; under four it cannot bear up:
Proverbs30:22 a slave when he becomes king
Proverbs30:23 an unloved woman when she gets a husband
Proverbs30:24 Four things on earth are small
Proverbs30:25 the ants are a people without strength

Proverbs30:27 the locusts have no king

Proverbs30:33 For as pressing milk produces curds

Proverbs31:2 No
Proverbs31:3 Do not give your strength to women
Proverbs31:4 It is not for kings
