Proverbs31:7 let them drink and forget their poverty
Proverbs31:8 Speak out for those who cannot speak
Proverbs31:9 Speak out
Proverbs31:10 A capable wife who can find? She is far more precious than jewels.
Proverbs31:11 The heart of her husband trusts in her
Proverbs31:12 She does him good
Proverbs31:13 She seeks wool and flax
Proverbs31:14 She is like the ships of the merchant
Proverbs31:15 She rises while it is still night and provides food for her household and tasks for her servant-girls.
Proverbs31:16 She considers a field and buys it; with the fruit of her hands she plants a vineyard.
Proverbs31:17 She girds herself with strength
Proverbs31:18 She perceives that her merchandise is profitable. Her lamp does not go out at night.
Proverbs31:19 She puts her hands to the distaff
Proverbs31:20 She opens her hand to the poor
Proverbs31:21 She is not afraid for her household when it snows
Proverbs31:22 She makes herself coverings; her clothing is fine linen and purple.
Proverbs31:23 Her husband is known in the city gates
Proverbs31:24 She makes linen garments and sells them; she supplies the merchant with sashes.
Proverbs31:25 Strength and dignity are her clothing
Proverbs31:26 She opens her mouth with wisdom
Proverbs31:27 She looks well to the ways of her household
Proverbs31:28 Her children rise up and call her happy; her husband too
Proverbs31:29 Many women have done excellently
Proverbs31:30 Charm is deceitful
Proverbs31:31 Give her a share in the fruit of her hands
Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the Teacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What do people gain from all the toil at which they toil under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 A generation goes
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises and the sun goes down
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind blows to the south
Ecclesiastes1:7 All streams run to the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are wearisome; more than one can express; the eye is not satisfied with seeing
Ecclesiastes1:9 What has been is what will be
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there a thing of which it is said
Ecclesiastes1:11 The people of long ago are not remembered
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 applied my mind to seek and to search out by wisdom all that is done under heaven; it is an unhappy business that God has given to human beings to be busy with.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I saw all the deeds that are done under the sun; and see
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is crooked cannot be made straight
Ecclesiastes1:16 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes1:17 And I applied my mind to know wisdom and to know madness and folly. I perceived that this also is but a chasing after wind.
Ecclesiastes1:18 For in much wisdom is much vexation
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said of laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I searched with my mind how to cheer my body with wine-- my mind still guiding me with wisdom-- and how to lay hold on folly
Ecclesiastes2:4 I made great works; I built houses and planted vineyards for myself;
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made myself gardens and parks
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made myself pools from which to water the forest of growing trees.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought male and female slaves
Ecclesiastes2:8 I also gathered for myself silver and gold and the treasure of kings and of the provinces; I got singers
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I became great and surpassed all who were before me in Jerusalem; also my wisdom remained with me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 Whatever my eyes desired I did not keep from them; I kept my heart from no pleasure
Ecclesiastes2:11 Then I considered all that my hands had done and the toil I had spent in doing it
Ecclesiastes2:12 So I turned to consider wisdom and madness and folly; for what can the one do who comes after the king? Only what has already been done.
Ecclesiastes2:13 Then I saw that wisdom excels folly as light excels darkness.
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise have eyes in their head
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:16 For there is no enduring remembrance of the wise or of fools
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I hated life
Ecclesiastes2:18 I hated all my toil in which I had toiled under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 -- and who knows whether they will be wise or foolish? Yet they will be master of all for which I toiled and used my wisdom under the sun. This also is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:20 So I turned and gave my heart up to despair concerning all the toil of my labors under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:21 because sometimes one who has toiled with wisdom and knowledge and skill must leave all to be enjoyed by another who did not toil for it. This also is vanity and a great evil.
Ecclesiastes2:22 What do mortals get from all the toil and strain with which they toil under the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 For all their days are full of pain
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for mortals than to eat and drink
Ecclesiastes2:25 for apart from him who can eat or who can have enjoyment?
Ecclesiastes2:26 For to the one who pleases him God gives wisdom and knowledge and joy; but to the sinner he gives the work of gathering and heaping
Ecclesiastes3:1 For everything there is a season
Ecclesiastes3:2 a time to be born
Ecclesiastes3:3 a time to kill
Ecclesiastes3:4 a time to weep
Ecclesiastes3:5 a time to throw away stones
Ecclesiastes3:6 a time to seek
Ecclesiastes3:7 a time to tear
Ecclesiastes3:8 a time to love
Ecclesiastes3:9 What gain have the workers from their toil?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the business that God has given to everyone to be busy with.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything suitable for its time; moreover he has put a sense of past and future into their minds
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing better for them than to be happy and enjoy themselves as long as they live;
Ecclesiastes3:13 moreover
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that whatever God does endures forever; nothing can be added to it
Ecclesiastes3:15 That which is
Ecclesiastes3:16 Moreover I saw under the sun that in the place of justice
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said in my heart with regard to human beings that God is testing them to show that they are but animals.
Ecclesiastes3:19 For the fate of humans and the fate of animals is the same; as one dies
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go to one place; all are from the dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows whether the human spirit goes upward and the spirit of animals goes downward to the earth?
Ecclesiastes3:22 So I saw that there is nothing better than that all should enjoy their work
Ecclesiastes4:1 Again I saw all the oppressions that are practiced under the sun. Look
Ecclesiastes4:2 And I thought the dead
Ecclesiastes4:3 but better than both is the one who has not yet been
Ecclesiastes4:4 Then I saw that all toil and all skill in work come from one person's envy of another. This also is vanity and a chasing after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:5 Fools fold their hands and consume their own flesh.
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better is a handful with quiet than two handfuls with toil
Ecclesiastes4:7 Again
Ecclesiastes4:8 the case of solitary individuals
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if they fall
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again
Ecclesiastes4:12 And though one might prevail against another
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better is a poor but wise youth than an old but foolish king
Ecclesiastes4:14 One can indeed come out of prison to reign
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw all the living who
Ecclesiastes4:16 there was no end to all those people whom he led. Yet those who come later will not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and a chasing after wind.
Ecclesiastes5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God; to draw near to listen is better than the sacrifice offered by fools; for they do not know how to keep from doing evil.
Ecclesiastes5:2 Never be rash with your mouth
Ecclesiastes5:3 For dreams come with many cares
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you make a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not fulfill it.
Ecclesiastes5:6 Do not let your mouth lead you into sin
Ecclesiastes5:7 With many dreams come vanities and a multitude of words; but fear God.
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see in a province the oppression of the poor and the violation of justice and right
Ecclesiastes5:9 But all things considered
Ecclesiastes5:10 The lover of money will not be satisfied with money; nor the lover of wealth
Ecclesiastes5:11 When goods increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 Sweet is the sleep of laborers
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a grievous ill that I have seen under the sun: riches were kept by their owners to their hurt
Ecclesiastes5:14 and those riches were lost in a bad venture; though they are parents of children
Ecclesiastes5:15 As they came from their mother's womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 This also is a grievous ill: just as they came
Ecclesiastes5:17 Besides
Ecclesiastes5:18 This is what I have seen to be good: it is fitting to eat and drink and find enjoyment in all the toil with which one toils under the sun the few days of the life God gives us; for this is our lot.
Ecclesiastes5:19 Likewise all to whom God gives wealth and possessions and whom he enables to enjoy them
Ecclesiastes5:20 For they will scarcely brood over the days of their lives
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is an evil that I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 those to whom God gives wealth
Ecclesiastes6:3 A man may beget a hundred children
Ecclesiastes6:4 For it comes into vanity and goes into darkness
Ecclesiastes6:5 moreover it has not seen the sun or known anything; yet it finds rest rather than he.
Ecclesiastes6:6 Even though he should live a thousand years twice over
Ecclesiastes6:7 All human toil is for the mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 For what advantage have the wise over fools? And what do the poor have who know how to conduct themselves before the living?
Ecclesiastes6:9 Better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of desire; this also is vanity and a chasing after wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatever has come to be has already been named
Ecclesiastes6:11 The more words
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for mortals while they live the few days of their vain life
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than precious ointment
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to the house of mourning than to go to the house of feasting; for this is the end of everyone
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning; but the heart of fools is in the house of mirth.

Ecclesiastes7:6 For like the crackling of thorns under a pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 Surely oppression makes the wise foolish

Ecclesiastes7:12 For the protection of wisdom is like the protection of money

Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity be joyful
Ecclesiastes7:15 In my vain life I have seen everything; there are righteous people who perish in their righteousness
Ecclesiastes7:16 Do not be too righteous
Ecclesiastes7:17 Do not be too wicked
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good that you should take hold of the one
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom gives strength to the wise more than ten rulers that are in a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 Surely there is no one on earth so righteous as to do good without ever sinning.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Do not give heed to everything that people say
Ecclesiastes7:22 your heart knows that many times you have yourself cursed others.

Isaiah1:1 The vision of Isaiah son of Amoz
Isaiah1:2 Hear
Isaiah1:3 The ox knows its owner
Isaiah1:4 Ah
Isaiah1:5 Why do you seek further beatings? Why do you continue to rebel? The whole head is sick
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even to the head
Isaiah1:7 Your country lies desolate
Isaiah1:8 And daughter Zion is left like a booth in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 If the LORD of hosts had not left us a few survivors
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the LORD
Isaiah1:11 What to me is the multitude of your sacrifices? says the LORD; I have had enough of burnt offerings of rams and the fat of fed beasts; I do not delight in the blood of bulls
Isaiah1:12 When you come to appear before me
Isaiah1:13 bringing offerings is futile; incense is an abomination to me. New moon and sabbath and calling of convocation-- I cannot endure solemn assemblies with iniquity.
Isaiah1:14 Your new moons and your appointed festivals my soul hates; they have become a burden to me
Isaiah1:15 When you stretch out your hands
Isaiah1:16 Wash yourselves; make yourselves clean; remove the evil of your doings from before my eyes; cease to do evil
Isaiah1:17 learn to do good; seek justice
Isaiah1:18 Come now
Isaiah1:19 If you are willing and obedient
Isaiah1:20 but if you refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 How the faithful city has become a whore! She that was full of justice
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross
Isaiah1:23 Your princes are rebels and companions of thieves. Everyone loves a bribe and runs after gifts. They do not defend the orphan
Isaiah1:24 Therefore says the Sovereign
Isaiah1:25 I will turn my hand against you; I will smelt away your dross as with lye and remove all your alloy.
Isaiah1:26 And I will restore your judges as at the first
Isaiah1:27 Zion shall be redeemed by justice
Isaiah1:28 But rebels and sinners shall be destroyed together
Isaiah1:29 For you shall be ashamed of the oaks in which you delighted; and you shall blush for the gardens that you have chosen.
Isaiah1:30 For you shall be like an oak whose leaf withers
Isaiah1:31 The strong shall become like tinder

Isaiah2:5 O house of Jacob
Isaiah2:6 For you have forsaken the ways of your people
Isaiah2:7 Their land is filled with silver and gold

Isaiah2:9 And so people are humbled
Isaiah2:10 Enter into the rock

Isaiah2:13 against all the cedars of Lebanon
Isaiah2:14 against all the high mountains
Isaiah2:15 against every high tower
Isaiah2:16 against all the ships of Tarshish

Isaiah2:18 The idols shall utterly pass away.

Isaiah7:19 And they will all come and settle in the steep ravines
Isaiah7:20 On that day the Lord will shave with a razor hired beyond the River-- with the king of Assyria-- the head and the hair of the feet
Isaiah7:21 On that day one will keep alive a young cow and two sheep
Isaiah7:22 and will eat curds because of the abundance of milk that they give; for everyone that is left in the land shall eat curds and honey.
Isaiah7:23 On that day every place where there used to be a thousand vines
Isaiah7:24 With bow and arrows one will go there
Isaiah7:25 and as for all the hills that used to be hoed with a hoe

Isaiah19:1 An oracle concerning Egypt. See

Isaiah19:6 its canals will become foul
Isaiah19:7 There will be bare places by the Nile
Isaiah19:8 Those who fish will mourn; all who cast hooks in the Nile will lament
Isaiah19:9 The workers in flax will be in despair
Isaiah19:10 Its weavers will be dismayed
Isaiah19:11 The princes of Zoan are utterly foolish; the wise counselors of Pharaoh give stupid counsel. How can you say to Pharaoh
Isaiah19:12 Where now are your sages? Let them tell you and make known what the LORD of hosts has planned against Egypt.
Isaiah19:13 The princes of Zoan have become fools
Isaiah19:14 The LORD has poured into them a spirit of confusion; and they have made Egypt stagger in all its doings as a drunkard staggers around in vomit.
Isaiah19:15 Neither head nor tail
Isaiah19:16 On that day the Egyptians will be like women
Isaiah19:17 And the land of Judah will become a terror to the Egyptians; everyone to whom it is mentioned will fear because of the plan that the LORD of hosts is planning against them.
Isaiah19:18 On that day there will be five cities in the land of Egypt that speak the language of Canaan and swear allegiance to the LORD of hosts. One of these will be called the City of the Sun.
Isaiah19:19 On that day there will be an altar to the LORD in the center of the land of Egypt
Isaiah19:20 It will be a sign and a witness to the LORD of hosts in the land of Egypt; when they cry to the LORD because of oppressors

Isaiah21:4 My mind reels

Isaiah22:1 The oracle concerning the valley of vision. What do you mean that you have gone up
Isaiah22:2 you that are full of shoutings
Isaiah22:3 Your rulers have all fled together; they were captured without the use of a bow. All of you who were found were captured
Isaiah22:4 Therefore I said: Look away from me
Isaiah22:5 For the Lord GOD of hosts has a day of tumult and trampling and confusion in the valley of vision
Isaiah22:6 Elam bore the quiver with chariots and cavalry
Isaiah22:7 Your choicest valleys were full of chariots
Isaiah22:8 He has taken away the covering of Judah. On that day you looked to the weapons of the House of the Forest
Isaiah22:9 and you saw that there were many breaches in the city of David
Isaiah22:10 You counted the houses of Jerusalem
Isaiah22:11 You made a reservoir between the two walls for the water of the old pool. But you did not look to him who did it
Isaiah22:12 In that day the Lord GOD of hosts called to weeping and mourning
Isaiah22:13 but instead there was joy and festivity
Isaiah22:14 The LORD of hosts has revealed himself in my ears: Surely this iniquity will not be forgiven you until you die

Isaiah22:16 What right do you have here? Who are your relatives here
Isaiah22:17 The LORD is about to hurl you away violently
Isaiah22:18 whirl you round and round
Isaiah22:19 I will thrust you from your office

Isaiah22:21 and will clothe him with your robe and bind your sash on him. I will commit your authority to his hand

Isaiah22:23 I will fasten him like a peg in a secure place
Isaiah22:24 And they will hang on him the whole weight of his ancestral house

Isaiah23:11 He has stretched out his hand over the sea

Isaiah24:11 There is an outcry in the streets for lack of wine; all joy has reached its eventide; the gladness of the earth is banished.

Isaiah24:13 For thus it shall be on the earth and among the nations

Jeremiah1:1 The words of Jeremiah son of Hilkiah
Jeremiah1:2 to whom the word of the LORD came in the days of King Josiah son of Amon of Judah
Jeremiah1:3 It came also in the days of King Jehoiakim son of Josiah of Judah
Jeremiah1:4 Now the word of the LORD came to me saying
Jeremiah1:5 Before I formed you in the womb I knew you
Jeremiah1:6 Then I said
Jeremiah1:7 But the LORD said to me
Jeremiah1:8 Do not be afraid of them
Jeremiah1:9 Then the LORD put out his hand and touched my mouth; and the LORD said to me
Jeremiah1:10 See
Jeremiah1:11 The word of the LORD came to me
Jeremiah1:12 Then the LORD said to me
Jeremiah1:13 The word of the LORD came to me a second time
Jeremiah1:14 Then the LORD said to me: Out of the north disaster shall break out on all the inhabitants of the land.
Jeremiah1:15 For now I am calling all the tribes of the kingdoms of the north
Jeremiah1:16 And I will utter my judgments against them
Jeremiah1:17 But you
Jeremiah1:18 And I for my part have made you today a fortified city
Jeremiah1:19 They will fight against you; but they shall not prevail against you
Jeremiah2:1 The word of the LORD came to me
Jeremiah2:2 Go and proclaim in the hearing of Jerusalem
Jeremiah2:3 Israel was holy to the LORD
Jeremiah2:4 Hear the word of the LORD
Jeremiah2:5 Thus says the LORD: What wrong did your ancestors find in me that they went far from me
Jeremiah2:6 They did not say
Jeremiah2:7 I brought you into a plentiful land to eat its fruits and its good things. But when you entered you defiled my land
Jeremiah2:8 The priests did not say
Jeremiah2:9 Therefore once more I accuse you
Jeremiah2:10 Cross to the coasts of Cyprus and look
Jeremiah2:11 Has a nation changed its gods
Jeremiah2:12 Be appalled
Jeremiah2:13 for my people have committed two evils: they have forsaken me
Jeremiah2:14 Is Israel a slave? Is he a homeborn servant? Why then has he become plunder?
Jeremiah2:15 The lions have roared against him
Jeremiah2:16 Moreover
Jeremiah2:17 Have you not brought this upon yourself by forsaking the LORD your God
Jeremiah2:18 What then do you gain by going to Egypt
Jeremiah2:19 Your wickedness will punish you
Jeremiah2:20 For long ago you broke your yoke and burst your bonds
Jeremiah2:21 Yet I planted you as a choice vine
Jeremiah2:22 Though you wash yourself with lye and use much soap
Jeremiah2:23 How can you say
Jeremiah2:24 a wild ass at home in the wilderness
Jeremiah2:25 Keep your feet from going unshod and your throat from thirst. But you said
Jeremiah2:26 As a thief is shamed when caught
Jeremiah2:27 who say to a tree
Jeremiah2:28 But where are your gods that you made for yourself? Let them come
Jeremiah2:29 Why do you complain against me? You have all rebelled against me
Jeremiah2:30 In vain I have struck down your children; they accepted no correction. Your own sword devoured your prophets like a ravening lion.
Jeremiah2:31 And you
Jeremiah2:32 Can a girl forget her ornaments
Jeremiah2:33 How well you direct your course to seek lovers! So that even to wicked women you have taught your ways.
Jeremiah2:34 Also on your skirts is found the lifeblood of the innocent poor
Jeremiah2:35 you say
Jeremiah2:36 How lightly you gad about
Jeremiah2:37 From there also you will come away with your hands on your head; for the LORD has rejected those in whom you trust

Jeremiah3:20 Instead

Jeremiah5:15 I am going to bring upon you a nation from far away

Jeremiah14:4 because the ground is cracked. Because there has been no rain on the land the farmers are dismayed; they cover their heads.

Jeremiah16:2 You shall not take a wife
Jeremiah16:3 For thus says the LORD concerning the sons and daughters who are born in this place

Jeremiah16:5 For thus says the LORD: Do not enter the house of mourning

Jeremiah16:15 but As the LORD lives who brought the people of Israel up out of the land of the north and out of all the lands where he had driven them. For I will bring them back to their own land that I gave to their ancestors.

Jeremiah16:17 For my eyes are on all their ways; they are not hidden from my presence
Jeremiah16:18 And I will doubly repay their iniquity and their sin

Jeremiah17:1 The sin of Judah is written with an iron pen; with a diamond point it is engraved on the tablet of their hearts
Jeremiah17:2 while their children remember their altars and their sacred poles
Jeremiah17:3 on the mountains in the open country. Your wealth and all your treasures I will give for spoil as the price of your sin throughout all your territory.
Jeremiah17:4 By your own act you shall lose the heritage that I gave you
Jeremiah17:5 Thus says the LORD: Cursed are those who trust in mere mortals and make mere flesh their strength
Jeremiah17:6 They shall be like a shrub in the desert
Jeremiah17:7 Blessed are those who trust in the LORD
Jeremiah17:8 They shall be like a tree planted by water
Jeremiah17:9 The heart is devious above all else; it is perverse-- who can understand it?
Jeremiah17:10 I the LORD test the mind and search the heart
Jeremiah17:11 Like the partridge hatching what it did not lay
Jeremiah17:12 O glorious throne
Jeremiah17:13 O hope of Israel! O LORD! All who forsake you shall be put to shame; those who turn away from you shall be recorded in the underworld
Jeremiah17:14 Heal me
Jeremiah17:15 See how they say to me
Jeremiah17:16 But I have not run away from being a shepherd in your service
Jeremiah17:17 Do not become a terror to me; you are my refuge in the day of disaster;
Jeremiah17:18 Let my persecutors be shamed
Jeremiah17:19 Thus said the LORD to me: Go and stand in the People's Gate
Jeremiah17:20 and say to them: Hear the word of the LORD
Jeremiah17:21 Thus says the LORD: For the sake of your lives
Jeremiah17:22 And do not carry a burden out of your houses on the sabbath or do any work
Jeremiah17:23 Yet they did not listen or incline their ear; they stiffened their necks and would not hear or receive instruction.

Jeremiah23:10 For the land is full of adulterers; because of the curse the land mourns

Jeremiah23:12 Therefore their way shall be to them like slippery paths in the darkness
Jeremiah23:13 In the prophets of Samaria I saw a disgusting thing: they prophesied by Baal and led my people Israel astray.
Jeremiah23:14 But in the prophets of Jerusalem I have seen a more shocking thing: they commit adultery and walk in lies; they strengthen the hands of evildoers

Jeremiah23:17 They keep saying to those who despise the word of the LORD

Jeremiah23:19 Look
Jeremiah23:20 The anger of the LORD will not turn back until he has executed and accomplished the intents of his mind. In the latter days you will understand it clearly.
Jeremiah23:21 I did not send the prophets
Jeremiah23:22 But if they had stood in my council
Jeremiah23:23 Am I a God near by
Jeremiah23:24 Who can hide in secret places so that I cannot see them? says the LORD. Do I not fill heaven and earth? says the LORD.

Jeremiah23:26 How long? Will the hearts of the prophets ever turn back-- those who prophesy lies

Jeremiah32:34 They set up their abominations in the house that bears my name

Jeremiah32:37 See

Jeremiah32:39 I will give them one heart and one way

Jeremiah32:41 I will rejoice in doing good to them

Jeremiah49:23 Concerning Damascus. Hamath and Arpad are confounded
Jeremiah49:24 Damascus has become feeble
Jeremiah49:25 How the famous city is forsaken
Jeremiah49:26 Therefore her young men shall fall in her squares
Jeremiah49:27 And I will kindle a fire at the wall of Damascus
Jeremiah49:28 Concerning Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor that King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon defeated. Thus says the LORD: Rise up
Jeremiah49:29 Take their tents and their flocks
Jeremiah49:30 Flee
Jeremiah49:31 Rise up
Jeremiah49:32 Their camels shall become booty
Jeremiah49:33 Hazor shall become a lair of jackals
Jeremiah49:34 The word of the LORD that came to the prophet Jeremiah concerning Elam
Jeremiah49:35 Thus says the LORD of hosts: I am going to break the bow of Elam
Jeremiah49:36 and I will bring upon Elam the four winds from the four quarters of heaven; and I will scatter them to all these winds
Jeremiah49:37 I will terrify Elam before their enemies
Jeremiah49:38 and I will set my throne in Elam
Jeremiah49:39 But in the latter days I will restore the fortunes of Elam
Jeremiah50:1 The word that the LORD spoke concerning Babylon
Jeremiah50:2 Declare among the nations and proclaim
Jeremiah50:3 For out of the north a nation has come up against her; it shall make her land a desolation
Jeremiah50:4 In those days and in that time
Jeremiah50:5 They shall ask the way to Zion
Jeremiah50:6 My people have been lost sheep; their shepherds have led them astray
Jeremiah50:7 All who found them have devoured them
Jeremiah50:8 Flee from Babylon
Jeremiah50:9 For I am going to stir up and bring against Babylon a company of great nations from the land of the north; and they shall array themselves against her; from there she shall be taken. Their arrows are like the arrows of a skilled warrior who does not return empty-handed.
Jeremiah50:10 Chaldea shall be plundered; all who plunder her shall be sated
Jeremiah50:11 Though you rejoice
Jeremiah50:12 your mother shall be utterly shamed
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the wrath of the LORD she shall not be inhabited
Jeremiah50:14 Take up your positions around Babylon
Jeremiah50:15 Raise a shout against her from all sides
Jeremiah50:16 Cut off from Babylon the sower
Jeremiah50:17 Israel is a hunted sheep driven away by lions. First the king of Assyria devoured it
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore
Jeremiah50:19 I will restore Israel to its pasture
Jeremiah50:20 In those days and at that time
Jeremiah50:21 Go up to the land of Merathaim; go up against her
Jeremiah50:22 The noise of battle is in the land
Jeremiah50:23 How the hammer of the whole earth is cut down and broken! How Babylon has become a horror among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 You set a snare for yourself and you were caught
Jeremiah50:25 The LORD has opened his armory
Jeremiah50:26 Come against her from every quarter; open her granaries; pile her up like heaps of grain
Jeremiah50:27 Kill all her bulls
Jeremiah50:28 Listen! Fugitives and refugees from the land of Babylon are coming to declare in Zion the vengeance of the LORD our God
Jeremiah50:29 Summon archers against Babylon
Jeremiah50:30 Therefore her young men shall fall in her squares
Jeremiah50:31 I am against you
Jeremiah50:32 The arrogant one shall stumble and fall
Jeremiah50:33 Thus says the LORD of hosts: The people of Israel are oppressed
Jeremiah50:34 Their Redeemer is strong; the LORD of hosts is his name. He will surely plead their cause
Jeremiah50:35 A sword against the Chaldeans
Jeremiah50:36 A sword against the diviners
Jeremiah50:37 A sword against her horses and against her chariots
Jeremiah50:38 A drought against her waters
Jeremiah50:39 Therefore wild animals shall live with hyenas in Babylon
Jeremiah50:40 As when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighbors
Jeremiah50:41 Look
Jeremiah50:42 They wield bow and spear
Jeremiah50:43 The king of Babylon heard news of them
Jeremiah50:44 Like a lion coming up from the thickets of the Jordan against a perennial pasture
Jeremiah50:45 Therefore hear the plan that the LORD has made against Babylon
Jeremiah50:46 At the sound of the capture of Babylon the earth shall tremble
Jeremiah51:1 Thus says the LORD: I am going to stir up a destructive wind against Babylon and against the inhabitants of Leb-qamai;
Jeremiah51:2 and I will send winnowers to Babylon
Jeremiah51:3 Let not the archer bend his bow
Jeremiah51:4 They shall fall down slain in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 Israel and Judah have not been forsaken by their God
Jeremiah51:6 Flee from the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon was a golden cup in the LORD's hand
Jeremiah51:8 Suddenly Babylon has fallen and is shattered; wail for her! Bring balm for her wound; perhaps she may be healed.
Jeremiah51:9 We tried to heal Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 The LORD has brought forth our vindication; come
Jeremiah51:11 Sharpen the arrows! Fill the quivers! The LORD has stirred up the spirit of the kings of the Medes
Jeremiah51:12 Raise a standard against the walls of Babylon; make the watch strong; post sentinels; prepare the ambushes; for the LORD has both planned and done what he spoke concerning the inhabitants of Babylon.
Jeremiah51:13 You who live by mighty waters
Jeremiah51:14 The LORD of hosts has sworn by himself: Surely I will fill you with troops like a swarm of locusts
Jeremiah51:15 It is he who made the earth by his power
Jeremiah51:16 When he utters his voice there is a tumult of waters in the heavens
Jeremiah51:17 Everyone is stupid and without knowledge; goldsmiths are all put to shame by their idols; for their images are false
Jeremiah51:18 They are worthless
Jeremiah51:19 Not like these is the LORD
Jeremiah51:20 You are my war club
Jeremiah51:21 with you I smash the horse and its rider; with you I smash the chariot and the charioteer;
Jeremiah51:22 with you I smash man and woman; with you I smash the old man and the boy; with you I smash the young man and the girl;
Jeremiah51:23 with you I smash shepherds and their flocks; with you I smash farmers and their teams; with you I smash governors and deputies.
Jeremiah51:24 I will repay Babylon and all the inhabitants of Chaldea before your very eyes for all the wrong that they have done in Zion
Jeremiah51:25 I am against you
Jeremiah51:26 No stone shall be taken from you for a corner and no stone for a foundation
Jeremiah51:27 Raise a standard in the land
Jeremiah51:28 Prepare the nations for war against her
Jeremiah51:29 The land trembles and writhes
Jeremiah51:30 The warriors of Babylon have given up fighting
Jeremiah51:31 One runner runs to meet another
Jeremiah51:32 the fords have been seized
Jeremiah51:33 For thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah51:34 King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon has devoured me
Jeremiah51:35 May my torn flesh be avenged on Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore thus says the LORD: I am going to defend your cause and take vengeance for you. I will dry up her sea and make her fountain dry;
Jeremiah51:37 and Babylon shall become a heap of ruins
Jeremiah51:38 Like lions they shall roar together; they shall growl like lions' whelps.
Jeremiah51:39 When they are inflamed
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How Sheshach is taken
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has risen over Babylon; she has been covered by its tumultuous waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities have become an object of horror
Jeremiah51:44 I will punish Bel in Babylon
Jeremiah51:45 Come out of her
Jeremiah51:46 Do not be fainthearted or fearful at the rumors heard in the land-- one year one rumor comes
Jeremiah51:47 Assuredly
Jeremiah51:48 Then the heavens and the earth
Jeremiah51:49 Babylon must fall for the slain of Israel
Jeremiah51:50 You survivors of the sword
Jeremiah51:51 We are put to shame
Jeremiah51:52 Therefore the time is surely coming
Jeremiah51:53 Though Babylon should mount up to heaven
Jeremiah51:54 Listen!-- a cry from Babylon! A great crashing from the land of the Chaldeans!
Jeremiah51:55 For the LORD is laying Babylon waste
Jeremiah51:56 for a destroyer has come against her
Jeremiah51:57 I will make her officials and her sages drunk
Jeremiah51:58 Thus says the LORD of hosts: The broad wall of Babylon shall be leveled to the ground
Jeremiah51:59 The word that the prophet Jeremiah commanded Seraiah son of Neriah son of Mahseiah
Jeremiah51:60 Jeremiah wrote in a scroll all the disasters that would come on Babylon
Jeremiah51:61 And Jeremiah said to Seraiah: When you come to Babylon
Jeremiah51:62 and say
Jeremiah51:63 When you finish reading this scroll
Jeremiah51:64 and say
Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he began to reign; he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
Jeremiah52:2 He did what was evil in the sight of the LORD
Jeremiah52:3 Indeed
Jeremiah52:4 And in the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 So the city was besieged until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 On the ninth day of the fourth month the famine became so severe in the city that there was no food for the people of the land.
Jeremiah52:7 Then a breach was made in the city wall; and all the soldiers fled and went out from the city by night by the way of the gate between the two walls
Jeremiah52:8 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king
Jeremiah52:9 Then they captured the king
Jeremiah52:10 The king of Babylon killed the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
Jeremiah52:11 He put out the eyes of Zedekiah
Jeremiah52:12 In the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 He burned the house of the LORD
Jeremiah52:14 All the army of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah52:15 Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried into exile some of the poorest of the people and the rest of the people who were left in the city and the deserters who had defected to the king of Babylon
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left some of the poorest people of the land to be vinedressers and tillers of the soil.
Jeremiah52:17 The pillars of bronze that were in the house of the LORD
Jeremiah52:18 They took away the pots
Jeremiah52:19 The captain of the guard took away the small bowls also
Jeremiah52:20 As for the two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 As for the pillars
Jeremiah52:22 Upon it was a capital of bronze; the height of the one capital was five cubits; latticework and pomegranates
Jeremiah52:23 There were ninety-six pomegranates on the sides; all the pomegranates encircling the latticework numbered one hundred.
Jeremiah52:24 The captain of the guard took the chief priest Seraiah
Jeremiah52:25 and from the city he took an officer who had been in command of the soldiers
Jeremiah52:26 Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them
Jeremiah52:27 And the king of Babylon struck them down
Jeremiah52:28 This is the number of the people whom Nebuchadrezzar took into exile: in the seventh year
Jeremiah52:29 in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar he took into exile from Jerusalem eight hundred thirty-two persons;
Jeremiah52:30 in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadrezzar
Jeremiah52:31 In the thirty-seventh year of the exile of King Jehoiachin of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 he spoke kindly to him
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin put aside his prison clothes
Jeremiah52:34 For his allowance
Lamentations1:1 How lonely sits the city that once was full of people! How like a widow she has become
Lamentations1:2 She weeps bitterly in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into exile with suffering and hard servitude; she lives now among the nations
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her foes have become the masters
Lamentations1:6 From daughter Zion has departed all her majesty. Her princes have become like stags that find no pasture; they fled without strength before the pursuer.
Lamentations1:7 Jerusalem remembers
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem sinned grievously
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness was in her skirts; she took no thought of her future; her downfall was appalling
Lamentations1:10 Enemies have stretched out their hands over all her precious things; she has even seen the nations invade her sanctuary
Lamentations1:11 All her people groan as they search for bread; they trade their treasures for food to revive their strength. Look
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 From on high he sent fire; it went deep into my bones; he spread a net for my feet; he turned me back; he has left me stunned
Lamentations1:14 My transgressions were bound into a yoke; by his hand they were fastened together; they weigh on my neck
Lamentations1:15 The LORD has rejected all my warriors in the midst of me; he proclaimed a time against me to crush my young men; the Lord has trodden as in a wine press the virgin daughter Judah.
Lamentations1:16 For these things I weep; my eyes flow with tears; for a comforter is far from me
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretches out her hands
Lamentations1:18 The LORD is in the right
Lamentations1:19 I called to my lovers but they deceived me; my priests and elders perished in the city while seeking food to revive their strength.
Lamentations1:20 See
Lamentations1:21 They heard how I was groaning
Lamentations1:22 Let all their evil doing come before you; and deal with them as you have dealt with me because of all my transgressions; for my groans are many and my heart is faint.
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord in his anger has humiliated daughter Zion! He has thrown down from heaven to earth the splendor of Israel; he has not remembered his footstool in the day of his anger.
Lamentations2:2 The Lord has destroyed without mercy all the dwellings of Jacob; in his wrath he has broken down the strongholds of daughter Judah; he has brought down to the ground in dishonor the kingdom and its rulers.
Lamentations2:3 He has cut down in fierce anger all the might of Israel; he has withdrawn his right hand from them in the face of the enemy; he has burned like a flaming fire in Jacob
Lamentations2:4 He has bent his bow like an enemy
Lamentations2:5 The Lord has become like an enemy; he has destroyed Israel; He has destroyed all its palaces
Lamentations2:6 He has broken down his booth like a garden
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has scorned his altar
Lamentations2:8 The LORD determined to lay in ruins the wall of daughter Zion; he stretched the line; he did not withhold his hand from destroying; he caused rampart and wall to lament; they languish together.
Lamentations2:9 Her gates have sunk into the ground; he has ruined and broken her bars; her king and princes are among the nations; guidance is no more
Lamentations2:10 The elders of daughter Zion sit on the ground in silence; they have thrown dust on their heads and put on sackcloth; the young girls of Jerusalem have bowed their heads to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes are spent with weeping; my stomach churns; my bile is poured out on the ground because of the destruction of my people
Lamentations2:12 They cry to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 What can I say for you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets have seen for you false and deceptive visions; they have not exposed your iniquity to restore your fortunes
Lamentations2:15 All who pass along the way clap their hands at you; they hiss and wag their heads at daughter Jerusalem; Is this the city that was called the perfection of beauty
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies open their mouths against you; they hiss
Lamentations2:17 The LORD has done what he purposed
Lamentations2:18 Cry aloud to the Lord! O wall of daughter Zion! Let tears stream down like a torrent day and night! Give yourself no rest
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 Look
Lamentations2:21 The young and the old are lying on the ground in the streets; my young women and my young men have fallen by the sword; in the day of your anger you have killed them
Lamentations2:22 You invited my enemies from all around as if for a day of festival; and on the day of the anger of the LORD no one escaped or survived; those whom I bore and reared my enemy has destroyed.
Lamentations3:1 I am one who has seen affliction under the rod of God's wrath;
Lamentations3:2 he has driven and brought me into darkness without any light;
Lamentations3:3 against me alone he turns his hand
Lamentations3:4 He has made my flesh and my skin waste away
Lamentations3:5 he has besieged and enveloped me with bitterness and tribulation;
Lamentations3:6 he has made me sit in darkness like the dead of long ago.
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me about so that I cannot escape; he has put heavy chains on me;
Lamentations3:8 though I call and cry for help
Lamentations3:9 he has blocked my ways with hewn stones
Lamentations3:10 He is a bear lying in wait for me
Lamentations3:11 he led me off my way and tore me to pieces; he has made me desolate;
Lamentations3:12 he bent his bow and set me as a mark for his arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He shot into my vitals the arrows of his quiver;
Lamentations3:14 I have become the laughingstock of all my people
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 He has made my teeth grind on gravel
Lamentations3:17 my soul is bereft of peace; I have forgotten what happiness is;
Lamentations3:18 so I say
Lamentations3:19 The thought of my affliction and my homelessness is wormwood and gall!
Lamentations3:20 My soul continually thinks of it and is bowed down within me.
Lamentations3:21 But this I call to mind
Lamentations3:22 The steadfast love of the LORD never ceases
Lamentations3:23 they are new every morning; great is your faithfulness.
Lamentations3:24 The LORD is my portion
Lamentations3:25 The LORD is good to those who wait for him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that one should wait quietly for the salvation of the LORD.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for one to bear the yoke in youth
Lamentations3:28 to sit alone in silence when the Lord has imposed it
Lamentations3:29 to put one's mouth to the dust (there may yet be hope)
Lamentations3:30 to give one's cheek to the smiter
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not reject forever.
Lamentations3:32 Although he causes grief
Lamentations3:33 for he does not willingly afflict or grieve anyone.
Lamentations3:34 When all the prisoners of the land are crushed under foot
Lamentations3:35 when human rights are perverted in the presence of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 when one's case is subverted-- does the Lord not see it?
Lamentations3:37 Who can command and have it done
Lamentations3:38 Is it not from the mouth of the Most High that good and bad come?
Lamentations3:39 Why should any who draw breath complain about the punishment of their sins?
Lamentations3:40 Let us test and examine our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our hearts as well as our hands to God in heaven.
Lamentations3:42 We have transgressed and rebelled
Lamentations3:43 You have wrapped yourself with anger and pursued us
Lamentations3:44 you have wrapped yourself with a cloud so that no prayer can pass through.
Lamentations3:45 You have made us filth and rubbish among the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have opened their mouths against us;
Lamentations3:47 panic and pitfall have come upon us
Lamentations3:48 My eyes flow with rivers of tears because of the destruction of my people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes will flow without ceasing
Lamentations3:50 until the LORD from heaven looks down and sees.
Lamentations3:51 My eyes cause me grief at the fate of all the young women in my city.
Lamentations3:52 Those who were my enemies without cause have hunted me like a bird;
Lamentations3:53 they flung me alive into a pit and hurled stones on me;
Lamentations3:54 water closed over my head; I said
Lamentations3:55 I called on your name
Lamentations3:56 you heard my plea
Lamentations3:57 You came near when I called on you; you said
Lamentations3:58 You have taken up my cause
Lamentations3:59 You have seen the wrong done to me
Lamentations3:60 You have seen all their malice
Lamentations3:61 You have heard their taunts
Lamentations3:62 The whispers and murmurs of my assailants are against me all day long.
Lamentations3:63 Whether they sit or rise-- see
Lamentations3:64 Pay them back for their deeds
Lamentations3:65 Give them anguish of heart; your curse be on them!
Lamentations3:66 Pursue them in anger and destroy them from under the LORD's heavens.
Lamentations4:1 How the gold has grown dim
Lamentations4:2 The precious children of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even the jackals offer the breast and nurse their young
Lamentations4:4 The tongue of the infant sticks to the roof of its mouth for thirst; the children beg for food
Lamentations4:5 Those who feasted on delicacies perish in the streets; those who were brought up in purple cling to ash heaps.
Lamentations4:6 For the chastisement of my people has been greater than the punishment of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her princes were purer than snow
Lamentations4:8 Now their visage is blacker than soot; they are not recognized in the streets. Their skin has shriveled on their bones; it has become as dry as wood.
Lamentations4:9 Happier were those pierced by the sword than those pierced by hunger
Lamentations4:10 The hands of compassionate women have boiled their own children; they became their food in the destruction of my people.
Lamentations4:11 The LORD gave full vent to his wrath; he poured out his hot anger
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth did not believe
Lamentations4:13 It was for the sins of her prophets and the iniquities of her priests
Lamentations4:14 Blindly they wandered through the streets
Lamentations4:15 Away! Unclean! people shouted at them; Away! Away! Do not touch! So they became fugitives and wanderers; it was said among the nations
Lamentations4:16 The LORD himself has scattered them
Lamentations4:17 Our eyes failed
Lamentations4:18 They dogged our steps so that we could not walk in our streets; our end drew near; our days were numbered; for our end had come.
Lamentations4:19 Our pursuers were swifter than the eagles in the heavens; they chased us on the mountains
Lamentations4:20 The LORD's anointed
Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 The punishment of your iniquity
Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have become orphans
Lamentations5:4 We must pay for the water we drink; the wood we get must be bought.
Lamentations5:5 With a yoke on our necks we are hard driven; we are weary
Lamentations5:6 We have made a pact with Egypt and Assyria
Lamentations5:7 Our ancestors sinned; they are no more
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; there is no one to deliver us from their hand.
Lamentations5:9 We get our bread at the peril of our lives
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is black as an oven from the scorching heat of famine.
Lamentations5:11 Women are raped in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes are hung up by their hands; no respect is shown to the elders.
Lamentations5:13 Young men are compelled to grind
Lamentations5:14 The old men have left the city gate
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our hearts has ceased; our dancing has been turned to mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown has fallen from our head; woe to us
Lamentations5:17 Because of this our hearts are sick
Lamentations5:18 because of Mount Zion
Lamentations5:19 But you
Lamentations5:20 Why have you forgotten us completely? Why have you forsaken us these many days?
Lamentations5:21 Restore us to yourself
Lamentations5:22 unless you have utterly rejected us
Ezekiel1:1 In the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 On the fifth day of the month (it was the fifth year of the exile of King Jehoiachin)
Ezekiel1:3 the word of the LORD came to the priest Ezekiel son of Buzi
Ezekiel1:4 As I looked
Ezekiel1:5 In the middle of it was something like four living creatures. This was their appearance: they were of human form.
Ezekiel1:6 Each had four faces
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight
Ezekiel1:8 Under their wings on their four sides they had human hands. And the four had their faces and their wings thus:
Ezekiel1:9 their wings touched one another; each of them moved straight ahead
Ezekiel1:10 As for the appearance of their faces: the four had the face of a human being
Ezekiel1:11 such were their faces. Their wings were spread out above; each creature had two wings
Ezekiel1:12 Each moved straight ahead; wherever the spirit would go
Ezekiel1:13 In the middle of the living creatures there was something that looked like burning coals of fire
Ezekiel1:14 The living creatures darted to and fro
Ezekiel1:15 As I looked at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 As for the appearance of the wheels and their construction: their appearance was like the gleaming of beryl; and the four had the same form
Ezekiel1:17 When they moved
Ezekiel1:18 Their rims were tall and awesome
Ezekiel1:19 When the living creatures moved
Ezekiel1:20 Wherever the spirit would go
Ezekiel1:21 When they moved
Ezekiel1:22 Over the heads of the living creatures there was something like a dome
Ezekiel1:23 Under the dome their wings were stretched out straight
Ezekiel1:24 When they moved
Ezekiel1:25 And there came a voice from above the dome over their heads; when they stopped
Ezekiel1:26 And above the dome over their heads there was something like a throne
Ezekiel1:27 Upward from what appeared like the loins I saw something like gleaming amber
Ezekiel1:28 Like the bow in a cloud on a rainy day
Ezekiel2:1 He said to me: O mortal

Ezekiel2:7 You shall speak my words to them
Ezekiel2:8 But you
Ezekiel2:9 I looked
Ezekiel2:10 He spread it before me; it had writing on the front and on the back

Ezekiel3:16 At the end of seven days
Ezekiel3:17 Mortal

Ezekiel3:19 But if you warn the wicked
Ezekiel3:20 Again
Ezekiel3:21 If
Ezekiel3:22 Then the hand of the LORD was upon me there; and he said to me
Ezekiel3:23 So I rose up and went out into the valley; and the glory of the LORD stood there
Ezekiel3:24 The spirit entered into me
Ezekiel3:25 As for you
Ezekiel3:26 and I will make your tongue cling to the roof of your mouth
Ezekiel3:27 But when I speak with you

Ezekiel4:2 and put siegeworks against it
Ezekiel4:3 Then take an iron plate and place it as an iron wall between you and the city; set your face toward it
Ezekiel4:4 Then lie on your left side
Ezekiel4:5 For I assign to you a number of days
Ezekiel4:6 When you have completed these
Ezekiel4:7 You shall set your face toward the siege of Jerusalem
Ezekiel4:8 See
Ezekiel4:9 And you
Ezekiel4:10 The food that you eat shall be twenty shekels a day by weight; at fixed times you shall eat it.

Ezekiel4:12 You shall eat it as a barley-cake

Ezekiel4:14 Then I said
Ezekiel4:15 Then he said to me
Ezekiel4:16 Then he said to me
Ezekiel4:17 Lacking bread and water
Ezekiel5:1 And you
Ezekiel5:2 One third of the hair you shall burn in the fire inside the city
Ezekiel5:3 Then you shall take from these a small number
Ezekiel5:4 From these
Ezekiel5:5 Thus says the Lord GOD: This is Jerusalem; I have set her in the center of the nations
Ezekiel5:6 But she has rebelled against my ordinances and my statutes
Ezekiel5:7 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Because you are more turbulent than the nations that are all around you
Ezekiel5:8 therefore thus says the Lord GOD: I
Ezekiel5:9 And because of all your abominations
Ezekiel5:10 Surely
Ezekiel5:11 Therefore
Ezekiel5:12 One third of you shall die of pestilence or be consumed by famine among you; one third shall fall by the sword around you; and one third I will scatter to every wind and will unsheathe the sword after them.
Ezekiel5:13 My anger shall spend itself
Ezekiel5:14 Moreover I will make you a desolation and an object of mocking among the nations around you
Ezekiel5:15 You shall be a mockery and a taunt
Ezekiel5:16 when I loose against you my deadly arrows of famine
Ezekiel5:17 I will send famine and wild animals against you

Ezekiel6:9 Those of you who escape shall remember me among the nations where they are carried captive
Ezekiel6:10 And they shall know that I am the LORD; I did not threaten in vain to bring this disaster upon them.

Ezekiel6:13 And you shall know that I am the LORD

Ezekiel16:12 a ring on your nose
Ezekiel16:13 You were adorned with gold and silver

Ezekiel16:16 You took some of your garments
Ezekiel16:17 You also took your beautiful jewels of my gold and my silver that I had given you
Ezekiel16:18 and you took your embroidered garments to cover them
Ezekiel16:19 Also my bread that I gave you-- I fed you with choice flour and oil and honey-- you set it before them as a pleasing odor; and so it was
Ezekiel16:20 You took your sons and your daughters
Ezekiel16:21 You slaughtered my children and delivered them up as an offering to them.

Ezekiel16:23 After all your wickedness (woe

Ezekiel16:26 You played the whore with the Egyptians
Ezekiel16:27 Therefore I stretched out my hand against you
Ezekiel16:28 You played the whore with the Assyrians
Ezekiel16:29 You multiplied your whoring with Chaldea
Ezekiel16:30 How sick is your heart
Ezekiel16:31 building your platform at the head of every street
Ezekiel16:32 Adulterous wife

Ezekiel16:35 Therefore
Ezekiel16:36 Thus says the Lord GOD
Ezekiel16:37 therefore

Ezekiel16:40 They shall bring up a mob against you

Ezekiel16:42 So I will satisfy my fury on you
Ezekiel16:43 Because you have not remembered the days of your youth
Ezekiel16:44 See
Ezekiel16:45 You are the daughter of your mother
Ezekiel16:46 Your elder sister is Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 You not only followed their ways
Ezekiel16:48 As I live
Ezekiel16:49 This was the guilt of your sister Sodom: she and her daughters had pride
Ezekiel16:50 They were haughty
Ezekiel16:51 Samaria has not committed half your sins; you have committed more abominations than they
Ezekiel16:52 Bear your disgrace
Ezekiel16:53 I will restore their fortunes
Ezekiel16:54 in order that you may bear your disgrace and be ashamed of all that you have done
Ezekiel16:55 As for your sisters

Ezekiel16:58 You must bear the penalty of your lewdness and your abominations

Ezekiel16:62 I will establish my covenant with you

Ezekiel21:29 Offering false visions for you

Ezekiel21:31 I will pour out my indignation upon you
Ezekiel21:32 You shall be fuel for the fire
Ezekiel22:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel22:2 You
Ezekiel22:3 You shall say
Ezekiel22:4 You have become guilty by the blood that you have shed
Ezekiel22:5 Those who are near and those who are far from you will mock you
Ezekiel22:6 The princes of Israel in you
Ezekiel22:7 Father and mother are treated with contempt in you; the alien residing within you suffers extortion; the orphan and the widow are wronged in you.
Ezekiel22:8 You have despised my holy things
Ezekiel22:9 In you are those who slander to shed blood
Ezekiel22:10 In you they uncover their fathers' nakedness; in you they violate women in their menstrual periods.
Ezekiel22:11 One commits abomination with his neighbor's wife; another lewdly defiles his daughter-in-law; another in you defiles his sister
Ezekiel22:12 In you
Ezekiel22:13 See
Ezekiel22:14 Can your courage endure
Ezekiel22:15 I will scatter you among the nations and disperse you through the countries
Ezekiel22:16 And I shall be profaned through you in the sight of the nations; and you shall know that I am the LORD.
Ezekiel22:17 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel22:18 Mortal
Ezekiel22:19 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Because you have all become dross
Ezekiel22:20 As one gathers silver
Ezekiel22:21 I will gather you and blow upon you with the fire of my wrath
Ezekiel22:22 As silver is melted in a smelter
Ezekiel22:23 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel22:24 Mortal
Ezekiel22:25 Its princes within it are like a roaring lion tearing the prey; they have devoured human lives; they have taken treasure and precious things; they have made many widows within it.
Ezekiel22:26 Its priests have done violence to my teaching and have profaned my holy things; they have made no distinction between the holy and the common
Ezekiel22:27 Its officials within it are like wolves tearing the prey
Ezekiel22:28 Its prophets have smeared whitewash on their behalf
Ezekiel22:29 The people of the land have practiced extortion and committed robbery; they have oppressed the poor and needy
Ezekiel22:30 And I sought for anyone among them who would repair the wall and stand in the breach before me on behalf of the land
Ezekiel22:31 Therefore I have poured out my indignation upon them; I have consumed them with the fire of my wrath; I have returned their conduct upon their heads
Ezekiel23:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel23:2 Mortal
Ezekiel23:3 they played the whore in Egypt; they played the whore in their youth; their breasts were caressed there
Ezekiel23:4 Oholah was the name of the elder and Oholibah the name of her sister. They became mine
Ezekiel23:5 Oholah played the whore while she was mine; she lusted after her lovers the Assyrians
Ezekiel23:6 clothed in blue
Ezekiel23:7 She bestowed her favors upon them
Ezekiel23:8 She did not give up her whorings that she had practiced since Egypt; for in her youth men had lain with her and fondled her virgin bosom and poured out their lust upon her.
Ezekiel23:9 Therefore I delivered her into the hands of her lovers
Ezekiel23:10 These uncovered her nakedness; they seized her sons and her daughters; and they killed her with the sword. Judgment was executed upon her
Ezekiel23:11 Her sister Oholibah saw this
Ezekiel23:12 She lusted after the Assyrians
Ezekiel23:13 And I saw that she was defiled; they both took the same way.
Ezekiel23:14 But she carried her whorings further; she saw male figures carved on the wall
Ezekiel23:15 with belts around their waists
Ezekiel23:16 When she saw them she lusted after them
Ezekiel23:17 And the Babylonians came to her into the bed of love
Ezekiel23:18 When she carried on her whorings so openly and flaunted her nakedness
Ezekiel23:19 Yet she increased her whorings
Ezekiel23:20 and lusted after her paramours there
Ezekiel23:21 Thus you longed for the lewdness of your youth
Ezekiel23:22 Therefore
Ezekiel23:23 the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans

Ezekiel23:25 I will direct my indignation against you
Ezekiel23:26 They shall also strip you of your clothes and take away your fine jewels.
Ezekiel23:27 So I will put an end to your lewdness and your whoring brought from the land of Egypt; you shall not long for them
Ezekiel23:28 For thus says the Lord GOD: I will deliver you into the hands of those whom you hate
Ezekiel23:29 and they shall deal with you in hatred
Ezekiel23:30 have brought this upon you
Ezekiel23:31 You have gone the way of your sister; therefore I will give her cup into your hand.
Ezekiel23:32 Thus says the Lord GOD: You shall drink your sister's cup
Ezekiel23:33 You shall be filled with drunkenness and sorrow. A cup of horror and desolation is the cup of your sister Samaria;
Ezekiel23:34 you shall drink it and drain it out
Ezekiel23:35 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Because you have forgotten me and cast me behind your back
Ezekiel23:36 The LORD said to me: Mortal
Ezekiel23:37 For they have committed adultery
Ezekiel23:38 Moreover this they have done to me: they have defiled my sanctuary on the same day and profaned my sabbaths.
Ezekiel23:39 For when they had slaughtered their children for their idols
Ezekiel23:40 They even sent for men to come from far away
Ezekiel23:41 you sat on a stately couch
Ezekiel23:42 The sound of a raucous multitude was around her
Ezekiel23:43 Then I said
Ezekiel23:44 For they have gone in to her
Ezekiel23:45 But righteous judges shall declare them guilty of adultery and of bloodshed; because they are adulteresses and blood is on their hands.

Ezekiel23:47 The assembly shall stone them and with their swords they shall cut them down; they shall kill their sons and their daughters
Ezekiel23:48 Thus will I put an end to lewdness in the land
Ezekiel23:49 They shall repay you for your lewdness

Ezekiel26:1 In the eleventh year
Ezekiel26:2 Mortal
Ezekiel26:3 therefore
Ezekiel26:4 They shall destroy the walls of Tyre and break down its towers. I will scrape its soil from it and make it a bare rock.
Ezekiel26:5 It shall become
Ezekiel26:6 and its daughter-towns in the country shall be killed by the sword. Then they shall know that I am the LORD.
Ezekiel26:7 For thus says the Lord GOD: I will bring against Tyre from the north King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon
Ezekiel26:8 Your daughter-towns in the country he shall put to the sword. He shall set up a siege wall against you
Ezekiel26:9 He shall direct the shock of his battering rams against your walls and break down your towers with his axes.
Ezekiel26:10 His horses shall be so many that their dust shall cover you. At the noise of cavalry
Ezekiel26:11 With the hoofs of his horses he shall trample all your streets. He shall put your people to the sword
Ezekiel26:12 They will plunder your riches and loot your merchandise; they shall break down your walls and destroy your fine houses. Your stones and timber and soil they shall cast into the water.
Ezekiel26:13 I will silence the music of your songs; the sound of your lyres shall be heard no more.
Ezekiel26:14 I will make you a bare rock; you shall be a place for spreading nets. You shall never again be rebuilt

Ezekiel26:17 And they shall raise a lamentation over you
Ezekiel26:18 Now the coastlands tremble on the day of your fall; the coastlands by the sea are dismayed at your passing.
Ezekiel26:19 For thus says the Lord GOD: When I make you a city laid waste
Ezekiel26:20 then I will thrust you down with those who descend into the Pit
Ezekiel26:21 I will bring you to a dreadful end
Ezekiel27:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel27:2 Now you
Ezekiel27:3 and say to Tyre
Ezekiel27:4 Your borders are in the heart of the seas; your builders made perfect your beauty.
Ezekiel27:5 They made all your planks of fir trees from Senir; they took a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.
Ezekiel27:6 From oaks of Bashan they made your oars; they made your deck of pines from the coasts of Cyprus
Ezekiel27:7 Of fine embroidered linen from Egypt was your sail
Ezekiel27:8 The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers; skilled men of Zemer were within you
Ezekiel27:9 The elders of Gebal and its artisans were within you
Ezekiel27:10 Paras and Lud and Put were in your army
Ezekiel27:11 Men of Arvad and Helech were on your walls all around; men of Gamad were at your towers. They hung their quivers all around your walls; they made perfect your beauty.
Ezekiel27:12 Tarshish did business with you out of the abundance of your great wealth; silver
Ezekiel27:13 Javan
Ezekiel27:14 Beth-togarmah exchanged for your wares horses
Ezekiel27:15 The Rhodians traded with you; many coastlands were your own special markets; they brought you in payment ivory tusks and ebony.
Ezekiel27:16 Edom did business with you because of your abundant goods; they exchanged for your wares turquoise
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel traded with you; they exchanged for your merchandise wheat from Minnith
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus traded with you for your abundant goods-- because of your great wealth of every kind-- wine of Helbon
Ezekiel27:19 Vedan and Javan from Uzal entered into trade for your wares; wrought iron
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan traded with you in saddlecloths for riding.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your favored dealers in lambs
Ezekiel27:22 The merchants of Sheba and Raamah traded with you; they exchanged for your wares the best of all kinds of spices
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 These traded with you in choice garments
Ezekiel27:25 The ships of Tarshish traveled for you in your trade. So you were filled and heavily laden in the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:26 Your rowers have brought you into the high seas. The east wind has wrecked you in the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:27 Your riches
Ezekiel27:28 At the sound of the cry of your pilots the countryside shakes
Ezekiel27:29 and down from their ships come all that handle the oar. The mariners and all the pilots of the sea stand on the shore
Ezekiel27:30 and wail aloud over you
Ezekiel27:31 they make themselves bald for you
Ezekiel27:32 In their wailing they raise a lamentation for you
Ezekiel27:33 When your wares came from the seas
Ezekiel27:34 Now you are wrecked by the seas
Ezekiel27:35 All the inhabitants of the coastlands are appalled at you; and their kings are horribly afraid
Ezekiel27:36 The merchants among the peoples hiss at you; you have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more forever.

Ezekiel31:1 In the eleventh year
Ezekiel31:2 Mortal
Ezekiel31:3 Consider Assyria
Ezekiel31:4 The waters nourished it
Ezekiel31:5 So it towered high above all the trees of the field; its boughs grew large and its branches long
Ezekiel31:6 All the birds of the air made their nests in its boughs; under its branches all the animals of the field gave birth to their young; and in its shade all great nations lived.
Ezekiel31:7 It was beautiful in its greatness
Ezekiel31:8 The cedars in the garden of God could not rival it
Ezekiel31:9 I made it beautiful with its mass of branches
Ezekiel31:10 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Because it towered high and set its top among the clouds
Ezekiel31:11 I gave it into the hand of the prince of the nations; he has dealt with it as its wickedness deserves. I have cast it out.
Ezekiel31:12 Foreigners from the most terrible of the nations have cut it down and left it. On the mountains and in all the valleys its branches have fallen
Ezekiel31:13 On its fallen trunk settle all the birds of the air
Ezekiel31:14 All this is in order that no trees by the waters may grow to lofty height or set their tops among the clouds
Ezekiel31:15 Thus says the Lord GOD: On the day it went down to Sheol I closed the deep over it and covered it; I restrained its rivers
Ezekiel31:16 I made the nations quake at the sound of its fall
Ezekiel31:17 They also went down to Sheol with it
Ezekiel31:18 Which among the trees of Eden was like you in glory and in greatness? Now you shall be brought down with the trees of Eden to the world below; you shall lie among the uncircumcised

Ezekiel36:5 therefore thus says the Lord GOD: I am speaking in my hot jealousy against the rest of the nations
Ezekiel36:6 Therefore prophesy concerning the land of Israel
Ezekiel36:7 therefore thus says the Lord GOD: I swear that the nations that are all around you shall themselves suffer insults.
Ezekiel36:8 But you
Ezekiel36:9 See now
Ezekiel36:10 and I will multiply your population
Ezekiel36:11 and I will multiply human beings and animals upon you. They shall increase and be fruitful; and I will cause you to be inhabited as in your former times
Ezekiel36:12 I will lead people upon you-- my people Israel-- and they shall possess you
Ezekiel36:13 Thus says the Lord GOD: Because they say to you
Ezekiel36:14 therefore you shall no longer devour people and no longer bereave your nation of children
Ezekiel36:15 and no longer will I let you hear the insults of the nations

Ezekiel36:21 But I had concern for my holy name

Ezekiel36:24 I will take you from the nations
Ezekiel36:25 I will sprinkle clean water upon you
Ezekiel36:26 A new heart I will give you
Ezekiel36:27 I will put my spirit within you
Ezekiel36:28 Then you shall live in the land that I gave to your ancestors; and you shall be my people
Ezekiel36:29 I will save you from all your uncleannesses
Ezekiel36:30 I will make the fruit of the tree and the produce of the field abundant
Ezekiel36:31 Then you shall remember your evil ways
Ezekiel36:32 It is not for your sake that I will act
Ezekiel36:33 Thus says the Lord GOD: On the day that I cleanse you from all your iniquities
Ezekiel36:34 The land that was desolate shall be tilled
Ezekiel36:35 And they will say
Ezekiel36:36 Then the nations that are left all around you shall know that I
Ezekiel36:37 Thus says the Lord GOD: I will also let the house of Israel ask me to do this for them: to increase their population like a flock.
Ezekiel36:38 Like the flock for sacrifices

Ezekiel39:1 And you

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of King Jehoiakim of Judah
Daniel1:2 The Lord let King Jehoiakim of Judah fall into his power
Daniel1:3 Then the king commanded his palace master Ashpenaz to bring some of the Israelites of the royal family and of the nobility
Daniel1:4 young men without physical defect and handsome
Daniel1:5 The king assigned them a daily portion of the royal rations of food and wine. They were to be educated for three years
Daniel1:6 Among them were Daniel
Daniel1:7 The palace master gave them other names: Daniel he called Belteshazzar
Daniel1:8 But Daniel resolved that he would not defile himself with the royal rations of food and wine; so he asked the palace master to allow him not to defile himself.
Daniel1:9 Now God allowed Daniel to receive favor and compassion from the palace master.
Daniel1:10 The palace master said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 Then Daniel asked the guard whom the palace master had appointed over Daniel
Daniel1:12 Please test your servants for ten days. Let us be given vegetables to eat and water to drink.
Daniel1:13 You can then compare our appearance with the appearance of the young men who eat the royal rations
Daniel1:14 So he agreed to this proposal and tested them for ten days.
Daniel1:15 At the end of ten days it was observed that they appeared better and fatter than all the young men who had been eating the royal rations.
Daniel1:16 So the guard continued to withdraw their royal rations and the wine they were to drink
Daniel1:17 To these four young men God gave knowledge and skill in every aspect of literature and wisdom; Daniel also had insight into all visions and dreams.
Daniel1:18 At the end of the time that the king had set for them to be brought in
Daniel1:19 and the king spoke with them. And among them all

Daniel11:34 When they fall victim
Daniel11:35 Some of the wise shall fall

Daniel11:37 He shall pay no respect to the gods of his ancestors

Daniel11:41 He shall come into the beautiful land
Daniel11:42 He shall stretch out his hand against the countries
Daniel11:43 He shall become ruler of the treasures of gold and of silver

Hosea9:12 Even if they bring up children
Hosea9:13 Once I saw Ephraim as a young palm planted in a lovely meadow
Hosea9:14 Give them
Hosea9:15 Every evil of theirs began at Gilgal; there I came to hate them. Because of the wickedness of their deeds I will drive them out of my house. I will love them no more; all their officials are rebels.
Hosea9:16 Ephraim is stricken
Hosea9:17 Because they have not listened to him
Hosea10:1 Israel is a luxuriant vine that yields its fruit. The more his fruit increased the more altars he built; as his country improved
Hosea10:2 Their heart is false; now they must bear their guilt. The LORD will break down their altars
Hosea10:3 For now they will say: We have no king
Hosea10:4 They utter mere words; with empty oaths they make covenants; so litigation springs up like poisonous weeds in the furrows of the field.
Hosea10:5 The inhabitants of Samaria tremble for the calf of Beth-aven. Its people shall mourn for it
Hosea10:6 The thing itself shall be carried to Assyria as tribute to the great king. Ephraim shall be put to shame
Hosea10:7 Samaria's king shall perish like a chip on the face of the waters.
Hosea10:8 The high places of Aven
Hosea10:9 Since the days of Gibeah you have sinned
Hosea10:10 I will come against the wayward people to punish them; and nations shall be gathered against them when they are punished for their double iniquity.
Hosea10:11 Ephraim was a trained heifer that loved to thresh
Hosea10:12 Sow for yourselves righteousness; reap steadfast love; break up your fallow ground; for it is time to seek the LORD
Hosea10:13 You have plowed wickedness
Hosea10:14 therefore the tumult of war shall rise against your people
Hosea10:15 Thus it shall be done to you

Hosea13:1 When Ephraim spoke
Hosea13:2 And now they keep on sinning and make a cast image for themselves
Hosea13:3 Therefore they shall be like the morning mist or like the dew that goes away early

Hosea14:3 Assyria shall not save us; we will not ride upon horses; we will say no more

Hosea14:5 I will be like the dew to Israel; he shall blossom like the lily
Hosea14:6 His shoots shall spread out; his beauty shall be like the olive tree
Hosea14:7 They shall again live beneath my shadow

Hosea14:9 Those who are wise understand these things; those who are discerning know them. For the ways of the LORD are right
Joel1:1 The word of the LORD that came to Joel son of Pethuel:
Joel1:2 Hear this
Joel1:3 Tell your children of it

Joel1:5 Wake up
Joel1:6 For a nation has invaded my land
Joel1:7 It has laid waste my vines

Joel1:13 Put on sackcloth and lament

Joel1:20 Even the wild animals cry to you because the watercourses are dried up

Joel2:5 As with the rumbling of chariots

Joel2:8 They do not jostle one another
Joel2:9 They leap upon the city
Joel2:10 The earth quakes before them

Joel2:12 Yet even now
Joel2:13 rend your hearts and not your clothing. Return to the LORD

Joel2:23 O children of Zion

Joel2:28 Then afterward I will pour out my spirit on all flesh; your sons and your daughters shall prophesy
Joel2:29 Even on the male and female slaves

Joel2:31 The sun shall be turned to darkness
Joel2:32 Then everyone who calls on the name of the LORD shall be saved; for in Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there shall be those who escape

Amos4:8 so two or three towns wandered to one town to drink water
Amos4:9 I struck you with blight and mildew; I laid waste your gardens and your vineyards; the locust devoured your fig trees and your olive trees; yet you did not return to me

Amos4:12 Therefore thus I will do to you

Obadiah1:1 The vision of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord GOD concerning Edom: We have heard a report from the LORD
Obadiah1:2 I will surely make you least among the nations; you shall be utterly despised.
Obadiah1:3 Your proud heart has deceived you
Obadiah1:4 Though you soar aloft like the eagle
Obadiah1:5 If thieves came to you
Obadiah1:6 How Esau has been pillaged
Obadiah1:7 All your allies have deceived you
Obadiah1:8 On that day
Obadiah1:9 Your warriors shall be shattered
Obadiah1:10 For the slaughter and violence done to your brother Jacob
Obadiah1:11 On the day that you stood aside
Obadiah1:12 But you should not have gloated over your brother on the day of his misfortune; you should not have rejoiced over the people of Judah on the day of their ruin; you should not have boasted on the day of distress.
Obadiah1:13 You should not have entered the gate of my people on the day of their calamity; you should not have joined in the gloating over Judah's disaster on the day of his calamity; you should not have looted his goods on the day of his calamity.
Obadiah1:14 You should not have stood at the crossings to cut off his fugitives; you should not have handed over his survivors on the day of distress.
Obadiah1:15 For the day of the LORD is near against all the nations. As you have done
Obadiah1:16 For as you have drunk on my holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 But on Mount Zion there shall be those that escape
Obadiah1:18 The house of Jacob shall be a fire
Obadiah1:19 Those of the Negeb shall possess Mount Esau
Obadiah1:20 The exiles of the Israelites who are in Halah shall possess Phoenicia as far as Zarephath; and the exiles of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad shall possess the towns of the Negeb.
Obadiah1:21 Those who have been saved shall go up to Mount Zion to rule Mount Esau; and the kingdom shall be the LORD's.
Jonah1:1 Now the word of the LORD came to Jonah son of Amittai
Jonah1:2 Go at once to Nineveh
Jonah1:3 But Jonah set out to flee to Tarshish from the presence of the LORD. He went down to Joppa and found a ship going to Tarshish; so he paid his fare and went on board
Jonah1:4 But the LORD hurled a great wind upon the sea
Jonah1:5 Then the mariners were afraid
Jonah1:6 The captain came and said to him
Jonah1:7 The sailors said to one another
Jonah1:8 Then they said to him
Jonah1:9 I am a Hebrew
Jonah1:10 Then the men were even more afraid
Jonah1:11 Then they said to him
Jonah1:12 He said to them
Jonah1:13 Nevertheless the men rowed hard to bring the ship back to land
Jonah1:14 Then they cried out to the LORD
Jonah1:15 So they picked Jonah up and threw him into the sea; and the sea ceased from its raging.
Jonah1:16 Then the men feared the LORD even more
Jonah1:17 But the LORD provided a large fish to swallow up Jonah; and Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.
