Micah6:10 Can I forget the treasures of wickedness in the house of the wicked
Micah6:11 Can I tolerate wicked scales and a bag of dishonest weights?
Micah6:12 Your wealthy are full of violence; your inhabitants speak lies
Micah6:13 Therefore I have begun to strike you down
Micah6:14 You shall eat
Micah6:15 You shall sow
Micah6:16 For you have kept the statutes of Omri and all the works of the house of Ahab
Micah7:1 Woe is me! For I have become like one who
Micah7:2 The faithful have disappeared from the land
Micah7:3 Their hands are skilled to do evil; the official and the judge ask for a bribe
Micah7:4 The best of them is like a brier
Micah7:5 Put no trust in a friend
Micah7:6 for the son treats the father with contempt
Micah7:7 But as for me
Micah7:8 Do not rejoice over me
Micah7:9 I must bear the indignation of the LORD
Micah7:10 Then my enemy will see
Micah7:11 A day for the building of your walls! In that day the boundary shall be far extended.
Micah7:12 In that day they will come to you from Assyria to Egypt
Micah7:13 But the earth will be desolate because of its inhabitants
Micah7:14 Shepherd your people with your staff
Micah7:15 As in the days when you came out of the land of Egypt
Micah7:16 The nations shall see and be ashamed of all their might; they shall lay their hands on their mouths; their ears shall be deaf;
Micah7:17 they shall lick dust like a snake
Micah7:18 Who is a God like you
Micah7:19 He will again have compassion upon us; he will tread our iniquities under foot. You will cast all our sins into the depths of the sea.
Micah7:20 You will show faithfulness to Jacob and unswerving loyalty to Abraham
Nahum1:1 An oracle concerning Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum of Elkosh.
Nahum1:2 A jealous and avenging God is the LORD
Nahum1:3 The LORD is slow to anger but great in power
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and makes it dry
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake before him
Nahum1:6 Who can stand before his indignation? Who can endure the heat of his anger? His wrath is poured out like fire
Nahum1:7 The LORD is good
Nahum1:8 even in a rushing flood. He will make a full end of his adversaries
Nahum1:9 Why do you plot against the LORD? He will make an end; no adversary will rise up twice.
Nahum1:10 Like thorns they are entangled
Nahum1:11 From you one has gone out who plots evil against the LORD
Nahum1:12 Thus says the LORD
Nahum1:13 And now I will break off his yoke from you and snap the bonds that bind you.
Nahum1:14 The LORD has commanded concerning you: Your name shall be perpetuated no longer; from the house of your gods I will cut off the carved image and the cast image. I will make your grave
Nahum1:15 Look! On the mountains the feet of one who brings good tidings
Nahum2:1 A shatterer has come up against you. Guard the ramparts; watch the road; gird your loins; collect all your strength.
Nahum2:2 (For the LORD is restoring the majesty of Jacob
Nahum2:3 The shields of his warriors are red; his soldiers are clothed in crimson. The metal on the chariots flashes on the day when he musters them; the chargers prance.
Nahum2:4 The chariots race madly through the streets
Nahum2:5 He calls his officers; they stumble as they come forward; they hasten to the wall
Nahum2:6 The river gates are opened
Nahum2:7 It is decreed that the city be exiled
Nahum2:8 Nineveh is like a pool whose waters run away. Halt! Halt!-- but no one turns back.
Nahum2:9 Plunder the silver
Nahum2:10 Devastation
Nahum2:11 What became of the lions' den
Nahum2:12 The lion has torn enough for his whelps and strangled prey for his lionesses; he has filled his caves with prey and his dens with torn flesh.
Nahum2:13 See
Nahum3:1 Ah! City of bloodshed
Nahum3:2 The crack of whip and rumble of wheel
Nahum3:3 Horsemen charging
Nahum3:4 Because of the countless debaucheries of the prostitute
Nahum3:5 I am against you
Nahum3:6 I will throw filth at you and treat you with contempt
Nahum3:7 Then all who see you will shrink from you and say
Nahum3:8 Are you better than Thebes that sat by the Nile
Nahum3:9 Ethiopia was her strength
Nahum3:10 Yet she became an exile
Nahum3:11 You also will be drunken
Nahum3:12 All your fortresses are like fig trees with first-ripe figs-- if shaken they fall into the mouth of the eater.
Nahum3:13 Look at your troops: they are women in your midst. The gates of your land are wide open to your foes; fire has devoured the bars of your gates.
Nahum3:14 Draw water for the siege
Nahum3:15 There the fire will devour you
Nahum3:16 You increased your merchants more than the stars of the heavens. The locust sheds its skin and flies away.
Nahum3:17 Your guards are like grasshoppers
Nahum3:18 Your shepherds are asleep
Nahum3:19 There is no assuaging your hurt
Habakkuk1:1 The oracle that the prophet Habakkuk saw.
Habakkuk1:2 O LORD
Habakkuk1:3 Why do you make me see wrongdoing and look at trouble? Destruction and violence are before me; strife and contention arise.
Habakkuk1:4 So the law becomes slack and justice never prevails. The wicked surround the righteous-- therefore judgment comes forth perverted.
Habakkuk1:5 Look at the nations
Habakkuk1:6 For I am rousing the Chaldeans
Habakkuk1:7 Dread and fearsome are they; their justice and dignity proceed from themselves.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards
Habakkuk1:9 They all come for violence
Habakkuk1:10 At kings they scoff
Habakkuk1:11 Then they sweep by like the wind; they transgress and become guilty; their own might is their god!
Habakkuk1:12 Are you not from of old
Habakkuk1:13 Your eyes are too pure to behold evil
Habakkuk1:14 You have made people like the fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 The enemy brings all of them up with a hook; he drags them out with his net
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore he sacrifices to his net and makes offerings to his seine; for by them his portion is lavish
Habakkuk1:17 Is he then to keep on emptying his net
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand at my watchpost
Habakkuk2:2 Then the LORD answered me and said: Write the vision; make it plain on tablets
Habakkuk2:3 For there is still a vision for the appointed time; it speaks of the end
Habakkuk2:4 Look at the proud! Their spirit is not right in them
Habakkuk2:5 Moreover
Habakkuk2:6 Shall not everyone taunt such people and
Habakkuk2:7 Will not your own creditors suddenly rise
Habakkuk2:8 Because you have plundered many nations
Habakkuk2:9 Alas for you who get evil gain for your houses
Habakkuk2:10 You have devised shame for your house by cutting off many peoples; you have forfeited your life.
Habakkuk2:11 The very stones will cry out from the wall
Habakkuk2:12 Alas for you who build a town by bloodshed
Habakkuk2:13 Is it not from the LORD of hosts that peoples labor only to feed the flames
Habakkuk2:14 But the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD
Habakkuk2:15 Alas for you who make your neighbors drink
Habakkuk2:16 You will be sated with contempt instead of glory. Drink
Habakkuk2:17 For the violence done to Lebanon will overwhelm you; the destruction of the animals will terrify you-- because of human bloodshed and violence to the earth
Habakkuk2:18 What use is an idol once its maker has shaped it-- a cast image
Habakkuk2:19 Alas for you who say to the wood
Habakkuk2:20 But the LORD is in his holy temple; let all the earth keep silence before him!
Habakkuk3:1 A prayer of the prophet Habakkuk according to Shigionoth.
Habakkuk3:2 O LORD
Habakkuk3:3 God came from Teman
Habakkuk3:4 The brightness was like the sun; rays came forth from his hand
Habakkuk3:5 Before him went pestilence
Habakkuk3:6 He stopped and shook the earth; he looked and made the nations tremble. The eternal mountains were shattered; along his ancient pathways the everlasting hills sank low.
Habakkuk3:7 I saw the tents of Cushan under affliction; the tent-curtains of the land of Midian trembled.
Habakkuk3:8 Was your wrath against the rivers
Habakkuk3:9 You brandished your naked bow
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains saw you
Habakkuk3:11 the moon stood still in its exalted place
Habakkuk3:12 In fury you trod the earth
Habakkuk3:13 You came forth to save your people
Habakkuk3:14 You pierced with their own arrows the head of his warriors
Habakkuk3:15 You trampled the sea with your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I hear
Habakkuk3:17 Though the fig tree does not blossom
Habakkuk3:18 yet I will rejoice in the LORD; I will exult in the God of my salvation.
Habakkuk3:19 GOD

Matthew1:1 An account of the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah

Matthew1:3 and Judah the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar
Matthew1:4 and Aram the father of Aminadab
Matthew1:5 and Salmon the father of Boaz by Rahab
Matthew1:6 and Jesse the father of King David. And David was the father of Solomon by the wife of Uriah
Matthew1:7 and Solomon the father of Rehoboam
Matthew1:8 and Asaph the father of Jehoshaphat
Matthew1:9 and Uzziah the father of Jotham
Matthew1:10 and Hezekiah the father of Manasseh
Matthew1:11 and Josiah the father of Jechoniah and his brothers
Matthew1:12 And after the deportation to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Salathiel
Matthew1:13 and Zerubbabel the father of Abiud
Matthew1:14 and Azor the father of Zadok
Matthew1:15 and Eliud the father of Eleazar
Matthew1:16 and Jacob the father of Joseph the husband of Mary
Matthew1:17 So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; and from David to the deportation to Babylon

Matthew1:23 Look

Matthew1:25 but had no marital relations with her until she had borne a son; and he named him Jesus.

Matthew13:23 But as for what was sown on good soil
Matthew13:24 He put before them another parable: The kingdom of heaven may be compared to someone who sowed good seed in his field;
Matthew13:25 but while everybody was asleep
Matthew13:26 So when the plants came up and bore grain
Matthew13:27 And the slaves of the householder came and said to him
Matthew13:28 He answered
Matthew13:29 But he replied
Matthew13:30 Let both of them grow together until the harvest; and at harvest time I will tell the reapers
Matthew13:31 He put before them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and sowed in his field;
Matthew13:32 it is the smallest of all the seeds
Matthew13:33 He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman took and mixed in with three measures of flour until all of it was leavened.
Matthew13:34 Jesus told the crowds all these things in parables; without a parable he told them nothing.
Matthew13:35 This was to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet: I will open my mouth to speak in parables; I will proclaim what has been hidden from the foundation of the world.
Matthew13:36 Then he left the crowds and went into the house. And his disciples approached him
Matthew13:37 He answered
Matthew13:38 the field is the world
Matthew13:39 and the enemy who sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the age
Matthew13:40 Just as the weeds are collected and burned up with fire
Matthew13:41 The Son of Man will send his angels
Matthew13:42 and they will throw them into the furnace of fire
Matthew13:43 Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Let anyone with ears listen!
Matthew13:44 The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field
Matthew13:45 Again
Matthew13:46 on finding one pearl of great value
Matthew13:47 Again
Matthew13:48 when it was full
Matthew13:49 So it will be at the end of the age. The angels will come out and separate the evil from the righteous
Matthew13:50 and throw them into the furnace of fire
Matthew13:51 Have you understood all this? They answered
Matthew13:52 And he said to them
Matthew13:53 When Jesus had finished these parables

Matthew13:56 And are not all his sisters with us? Where then did this man get all this?

Matthew13:58 And he did not do many deeds of power there

Matthew27:31 After mocking him

Matthew27:35 And when they had crucified him

Matthew27:39 Those who passed by derided him
Matthew27:40 and saying

Matthew27:43 He trusts in God; let God deliver him now
Matthew27:44 The bandits who were crucified with him also taunted him in the same way.

Matthew27:47 When some of the bystanders heard it
Matthew27:48 At once one of them ran and got a sponge
Matthew27:49 But the others said

Matthew27:51 At that moment the curtain of the temple was torn in two
Matthew27:52 The tombs also were opened
Matthew27:53 After his resurrection they came out of the tombs and entered the holy city and appeared to many.
Matthew27:54 Now when the centurion and those with him
Matthew27:55 Many women were also there
Matthew27:56 Among them were Mary Magdalene
Matthew27:57 When it was evening

Matthew27:60 and laid it in his own new tomb
Matthew27:61 Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there

John5:25 Very truly
John5:26 For just as the Father has life in himself
John5:27 and he has given him authority to execute judgment

John5:33 You sent messengers to John
John5:34 Not that I accept such human testimony
John5:35 He was a burning and shining lamp
John5:36 But I have a testimony greater than John's. The works that the Father has given me to complete
John5:37 And the Father who sent me has himself testified on my behalf. You have never heard his voice or seen his form
John5:38 and you do not have his word abiding in you
John5:39 You search the scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; and it is they that testify on my behalf.
John5:40 Yet you refuse to come to me to have life.
John5:41 I do not accept glory from human beings.
John5:42 But I know that you do not have the love of God in you.
John5:43 I have come in my Father's name
John5:44 How can you believe when you accept glory from one another and do not seek the glory that comes from the one who alone is God?

John5:46 If you believed Moses

John15:1 I am the true vine
John15:2 He removes every branch in me that bears no fruit. Every branch that bears fruit he prunes to make it bear more fruit.
John15:3 You have already been cleansed by the word that I have spoken to you.
John15:4 Abide in me as I abide in you. Just as the branch cannot bear fruit by itself unless it abides in the vine
John15:5 I am the vine

Acts27:1 When it was decided that we were to sail for Italy

Acts27:5 After we had sailed across the sea that is off Cilicia and Pamphylia
Acts27:6 There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship bound for Italy and put us on board.

Acts27:8 Sailing past it with difficulty
Acts27:9 Since much time had been lost and sailing was now dangerous
Acts27:10 saying
Acts27:11 But the centurion paid more attention to the pilot and to the owner of the ship than to what Paul said.
Acts27:12 Since the harbor was not suitable for spending the winter
Acts27:13 When a moderate south wind began to blow
Acts27:14 But soon a violent wind
Acts27:15 Since the ship was caught and could not be turned head-on into the wind
Acts27:16 By running under the lee of a small island called Cauda we were scarcely able to get the ship's boat under control.
Acts27:17 After hoisting it up they took measures to undergird the ship; then
Acts27:18 We were being pounded by the storm so violently that on the next day they began to throw the cargo overboard
Acts27:19 and on the third day with their own hands they threw the ship's tackle overboard.
Acts27:20 When neither sun nor stars appeared for many days
Acts27:21 Since they had been without food for a long time

Acts27:24 and he said
Acts27:25 So keep up your courage
Acts27:26 But we will have to run aground on some island.
Acts27:27 When the fourteenth night had come
Acts27:28 So they took soundings and found twenty fathoms; a little farther on they took soundings again and found fifteen fathoms.
Acts27:29 Fearing that we might run on the rocks
Acts27:30 But when the sailors tried to escape from the ship and had lowered the boat into the sea

Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 which he promised beforehand through his prophets in the holy scriptures
Romans1:3 the gospel concerning his Son
Romans1:4 and was declared to be Son of God with power according to the spirit of holiness by resurrection from the dead
Romans1:5 through whom we have received grace and apostleship to bring about the obedience of faith among all the Gentiles for the sake of his name
Romans1:6 including yourselves who are called to belong to Jesus Christ
Romans1:7 To all God's beloved in Rome
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 For God
Romans1:10 asking that by God's will I may somehow at last succeed in coming to you.
Romans1:11 For I am longing to see you so that I may share with you some spiritual gift to strengthen you--
Romans1:12 or rather so that we may be mutually encouraged by each other's faith
Romans1:13 I want you to know
Romans1:14 I am a debtor both to Greeks and to barbarians
Romans1:15 -- hence my eagerness to proclaim the gospel to you also who are in Rome.
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of the gospel; it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who has faith
Romans1:17 For in it the righteousness of God is revealed through faith for faith; as it is written
Romans1:18 For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and wickedness of those who by their wickedness suppress the truth.
Romans1:19 For what can be known about God is plain to them
Romans1:20 Ever since the creation of the world his eternal power and divine nature
Romans1:21 for though they knew God
Romans1:22 Claiming to be wise
Romans1:23 and they exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling a mortal human being or birds or four-footed animals or reptiles.
Romans1:24 Therefore God gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to impurity
Romans1:25 because they exchanged the truth about God for a lie and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator
Romans1:26 For this reason God gave them up to degrading passions. Their women exchanged natural intercourse for unnatural
Romans1:27 and in the same way also the men
Romans1:28 And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God
Romans1:29 They were filled with every kind of wickedness
Romans1:30 slanderers
Romans1:31 foolish
Romans1:32 They know God's decree
Romans2:1 Therefore you have no excuse
Romans2:2 You say
Romans2:3 Do you imagine
Romans2:4 Or do you despise the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience? Do you not realize that God's kindness is meant to lead you to repentance?
Romans2:5 But by your hard and impenitent heart you are storing up wrath for yourself on the day of wrath
Romans2:6 For he will repay according to each one's deeds:
Romans2:7 to those who by patiently doing good seek for glory and honor and immortality
Romans2:8 while for those who are self-seeking and who obey not the truth but wickedness
Romans2:9 There will be anguish and distress for everyone who does evil
Romans2:10 but glory and honor and peace for everyone who does good
Romans2:11 For God shows no partiality.
Romans2:12 All who have sinned apart from the law will also perish apart from the law
Romans2:13 For it is not the hearers of the law who are righteous in God's sight
Romans2:14 When Gentiles

Romans2:16 on the day when

Romans2:19 and if you are sure that you are a guide to the blind
Romans2:20 a corrector of the foolish
Romans2:21 you
Romans2:22 You that forbid adultery
Romans2:23 You that boast in the law
Romans2:24 For

Romans2:26 So

Romans2:28 For a person is not a Jew who is one outwardly

Romans3:1 Then what advantage has the Jew? Or what is the value of circumcision?
Romans3:2 Much
Romans3:3 What if some were unfaithful? Will their faithlessness nullify the faithfulness of God?
Romans3:4 By no means! Although everyone is a liar
Romans3:5 But if our injustice serves to confirm the justice of God
Romans3:6 By no means! For then how could God judge the world?
Romans3:7 But if through my falsehood God's truthfulness abounds to his glory
Romans3:8 And why not say (as some people slander us by saying that we say)
Romans3:9 What then? Are we any better off? No
Romans3:10 as it is written: There is no one who is righteous
Romans3:11 there is no one who has understanding
Romans3:12 All have turned aside
Romans3:13 Their throats are opened graves; they use their tongues to deceive. The venom of vipers is under their lips.
Romans3:14 Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness.
Romans3:15 Their feet are swift to shed blood;
Romans3:16 ruin and misery are in their paths
Romans3:17 and the way of peace they have not known.
Romans3:18 There is no fear of God before their eyes.
Romans3:19 Now we know that whatever the law says
Romans3:20 For no human being will be justified in his sight by deeds prescribed by the law
Romans3:21 But now
Romans3:22 the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ for all who believe. For there is no distinction
Romans3:23 since all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God;
Romans3:24 they are now justified by his grace as a gift
Romans3:25 whom God put forward as a sacrifice of atonement by his blood
Romans3:26 it was to prove at the present time that he himself is righteous and that he justifies the one who has faith in Jesus.
Romans3:27 Then what becomes of boasting? It is excluded. By what law? By that of works? No
Romans3:28 For we hold that a person is justified by faith apart from works prescribed by the law.
Romans3:29 Or is God the God of Jews only? Is he not the God of Gentiles also? Yes
Romans3:30 since God is one; and he will justify the circumcised on the ground of faith and the uncircumcised through that same faith.
Romans3:31 Do we then overthrow the law by this faith? By no means! On the contrary
Romans4:1 What then are we to say was gained by Abraham
Romans4:2 For if Abraham was justified by works
Romans4:3 For what does the scripture say? Abraham believed God
Romans4:4 Now to one who works
Romans4:5 But to one who without works trusts him who justifies the ungodly
Romans4:6 So also David speaks of the blessedness of those to whom God reckons righteousness apart from works:
Romans4:7 Blessed are those whose iniquities are forgiven
Romans4:8 blessed is the one against whom the Lord will not reckon sin.
Romans4:9 Is this blessedness
Romans4:10 How then was it reckoned to him? Was it before or after he had been circumcised? It was not after
Romans4:11 He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. The purpose was to make him the ancestor of all who believe without being circumcised and who thus have righteousness reckoned to them
Romans4:12 and likewise the ancestor of the circumcised who are not only circumcised but who also follow the example of the faith that our ancestor Abraham had before he was circumcised.
Romans4:13 For the promise that he would inherit the world did not come to Abraham or to his descendants through the law but through the righteousness of faith.
Romans4:14 If it is the adherents of the law who are to be the heirs
Romans4:15 For the law brings wrath; but where there is no law
Romans4:16 For this reason it depends on faith
Romans4:17 as it is written
Romans4:18 Hoping against hope
Romans4:19 He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body
Romans4:20 No distrust made him waver concerning the promise of God
Romans4:21 being fully convinced that God was able to do what he had promised.
Romans4:22 Therefore his faith was reckoned to him as righteousness.
Romans4:23 Now the words
Romans4:24 but for ours also. It will be reckoned to us who believe in him who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead
Romans4:25 who was handed over to death for our trespasses and was raised for our justification.
Romans5:1 Therefore
Romans5:2 through whom we have obtained access to this grace in which we stand; and we boast in our hope of sharing the glory of God.
Romans5:3 And not only that
Romans5:4 and endurance produces character
Romans5:5 and hope does not disappoint us
Romans5:6 For while we were still weak
Romans5:7 Indeed
Romans5:8 But God proves his love for us in that while we still were sinners Christ died for us.
Romans5:9 Much more surely then
Romans5:10 For if while we were enemies
Romans5:11 But more than that
Romans5:12 Therefore
Romans5:13 sin was indeed in the world before the law
Romans5:14 Yet death exercised dominion from Adam to Moses
Romans5:15 But the free gift is not like the trespass. For if the many died through the one man's trespass
Romans5:16 And the free gift is not like the effect of the one man's sin. For the judgment following one trespass brought condemnation
Romans5:17 If
Romans5:18 Therefore just as one man's trespass led to condemnation for all
Romans5:19 For just as by the one man's disobedience the many were made sinners
Romans5:20 But law came in
Romans5:21 so that
Romans6:1 What then are we to say? Should we continue in sin in order that grace may abound?
Romans6:2 By no means! How can we who died to sin go on living in it?
Romans6:3 Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
Romans6:4 Therefore we have been buried with him by baptism into death
Romans6:5 For if we have been united with him in a death like his
Romans6:6 We know that our old self was crucified with him so that the body of sin might be destroyed
Romans6:7 For whoever has died is freed from sin.
Romans6:8 But if we have died with Christ
Romans6:9 We know that Christ
Romans6:10 The death he died
Romans6:11 So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus.
Romans6:12 Therefore

Romans6:14 For sin will have no dominion over you
Romans6:15 What then? Should we sin because we are not under law but under grace? By no means!

Romans6:17 But thanks be to God that you
Romans6:18 and that you
Romans6:19 I am speaking in human terms because of your natural limitations. For just as you once presented your members as slaves to impurity and to greater and greater iniquity

Romans6:22 But now that you have been freed from sin and enslaved to God
Romans6:23 For the wages of sin is death

Romans7:16 Now if I do what I do not want
Romans7:17 But in fact it is no longer I that do it
Romans7:18 For I know that nothing good dwells within me

Romans8:1 There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
Romans8:2 For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and of death.
Romans8:3 For God has done what the law
Romans8:4 so that the just requirement of the law might be fulfilled in us
Romans8:5 For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh
Romans8:6 To set the mind on the flesh is death
Romans8:7 For this reason the mind that is set on the flesh is hostile to God; it does not submit to God's law-- indeed it cannot
Romans8:8 and those who are in the flesh cannot please God.
Romans8:9 But you are not in the flesh; you are in the Spirit
Romans8:10 But if Christ is in you
Romans8:11 If the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you
Romans8:12 So then

Romans8:14 For all who are led by the Spirit of God are children of God.

Romans8:17 and if children
Romans8:18 I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory about to be revealed to us.
Romans8:19 For the creation waits with eager longing for the revealing of the children of God;
Romans8:20 for the creation was subjected to futility
Romans8:21 that the creation itself will be set free from its bondage to decay and will obtain the freedom of the glory of the children of God.
Romans8:22 We know that the whole creation has been groaning in labor pains until now;
Romans8:23 and not only the creation
Romans8:24 For in hope we were saved. Now hope that is seen is not hope. For who hopes for what is seen?
Romans8:25 But if we hope for what we do not see

Romans8:27 And God
Romans8:28 We know that all things work together for good for those who love God

Romans8:32 He who did not withhold his own Son

1Corinthians7:7 I wish that all were as I myself am. But each has a particular gift from God
1Corinthians7:8 To the unmarried and the widows I say that it is well for them to remain unmarried as I am.
1Corinthians7:9 But if they are not practicing self-control
1Corinthians7:10 To the married I give this command-- not I but the Lord-- that the wife should not separate from her husband
1Corinthians7:11 (but if she does separate
1Corinthians7:12 To the rest I say-- I and not the Lord-- that if any believer has a wife who is an unbeliever
1Corinthians7:13 And if any woman has a husband who is an unbeliever
1Corinthians7:14 For the unbelieving husband is made holy through his wife
1Corinthians7:15 But if the unbelieving partner separates
1Corinthians7:16 Wife
1Corinthians7:17 However that may be
1Corinthians7:18 Was anyone at the time of his call already circumcised? Let him not seek to remove the marks of circumcision. Was anyone at the time of his call uncircumcised? Let him not seek circumcision.
1Corinthians7:19 Circumcision is nothing
1Corinthians7:20 Let each of you remain in the condition in which you were called.
1Corinthians7:21 Were you a slave when called? Do not be concerned about it. Even if you can gain your freedom
1Corinthians7:22 For whoever was called in the Lord as a slave is a freed person belonging to the Lord
1Corinthians7:23 You were bought with a price; do not become slaves of human masters.
1Corinthians7:24 In whatever condition you were called
1Corinthians7:25 Now concerning virgins
1Corinthians7:26 I think that
1Corinthians7:27 Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek to be free. Are you free from a wife? Do not seek a wife.
1Corinthians7:28 But if you marry
1Corinthians7:29 I mean
1Corinthians7:30 and those who mourn as though they were not mourning
1Corinthians7:31 and those who deal with the world as though they had no dealings with it. For the present form of this world is passing away.
1Corinthians7:32 I want you to be free from anxieties. The unmarried man is anxious about the affairs of the Lord
1Corinthians7:33 but the married man is anxious about the affairs of the world
1Corinthians7:34 and his interests are divided. And the unmarried woman and the virgin are anxious about the affairs of the Lord
1Corinthians7:35 I say this for your own benefit
1Corinthians7:36 If anyone thinks that he is not behaving properly toward his fiancee
1Corinthians7:37 But if someone stands firm in his resolve
1Corinthians7:38 So then
1Corinthians7:39 A wife is bound as long as her husband lives. But if the husband dies
1Corinthians7:40 But in my judgment she is more blessed if she remains as she is. And I think that I too have the Spirit of God.
1Corinthians8:1 Now concerning food sacrificed to idols: we know that all of us possess knowledge. Knowledge puffs up
1Corinthians8:2 Anyone who claims to know something does not yet have the necessary knowledge;
1Corinthians8:3 but anyone who loves God is known by him.
1Corinthians8:4 Hence
1Corinthians8:5 Indeed
1Corinthians8:6 yet for us there is one God
1Corinthians8:7 It is not everyone
1Corinthians8:8 Food will not bring us close to God. We are no worse off if we do not eat
1Corinthians8:9 But take care that this liberty of yours does not somehow become a stumbling block to the weak.
1Corinthians8:10 For if others see you
1Corinthians8:11 So by your knowledge those weak believers for whom Christ died are destroyed.
1Corinthians8:12 But when you thus sin against members of your family

1Corinthians9:1 Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
1Corinthians9:2 If I am not an apostle to others
1Corinthians9:3 This is my defense to those who would examine me.
1Corinthians9:4 Do we not have the right to our food and drink?
1Corinthians9:5 Do we not have the right to be accompanied by a believing wife
1Corinthians9:6 Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to refrain from working for a living?
1Corinthians9:7 Who at any time pays the expenses for doing military service? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat any of its fruit? Or who tends a flock and does not get any of its milk?
1Corinthians9:8 Do I say this on human authority? Does not the law also say the same?
1Corinthians9:9 For it is written in the law of Moses
1Corinthians9:10 Or does he not speak entirely for our sake? It was indeed written for our sake
1Corinthians9:11 If we have sown spiritual good among you
1Corinthians9:12 If others share this rightful claim on you
1Corinthians9:13 Do you not know that those who are employed in the temple service get their food from the temple
1Corinthians9:14 In the same way
1Corinthians9:15 But I have made no use of any of these rights
1Corinthians9:16 If I proclaim the gospel
1Corinthians9:17 For if I do this of my own will
1Corinthians9:18 What then is my reward? Just this: that in my proclamation I may make the gospel free of charge
1Corinthians9:19 For though I am free with respect to all
1Corinthians9:20 To the Jews I became as a Jew
1Corinthians9:21 To those outside the law I became as one outside the law (though I am not free from God's law but am under Christ's law) so that I might win those outside the law.
1Corinthians9:22 To the weak I became weak
1Corinthians9:23 I do it all for the sake of the gospel
1Corinthians9:24 Do you not know that in a race the runners all compete
1Corinthians9:25 Athletes exercise self-control in all things; they do it to receive a perishable wreath
1Corinthians9:26 So I do not run aimlessly
1Corinthians9:27 but I punish my body and enslave it

Galatians3:1 You foolish Galatians! Who has bewitched you? It was before your eyes that Jesus Christ was publicly exhibited as crucified!

Galatians3:3 Are you so foolish? Having started with the Spirit
Galatians3:4 Did you experience so much for nothing?-- if it really was for nothing.
Galatians3:5 Well then
Galatians3:6 Just as Abraham believed God
Galatians3:7 so
Galatians3:8 And the scripture
Galatians3:9 For this reason
Galatians3:10 For all who rely on the works of the law are under a curse; for it is written
Galatians3:11 Now it is evident that no one is justified before God by the law; for The one who is righteous will live by faith.
Galatians3:12 But the law does not rest on faith; on the contrary
Galatians3:13 Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us-- for it is written

Galatians3:22 But the scripture has imprisoned all things under the power of sin

Galatians3:25 But now that faith has come
Galatians3:26 for in Christ Jesus you are all children of God through faith.

Ephesians5:8 For once you were darkness
Ephesians5:9 for the fruit of the light is found in all that is good and right and true.
Ephesians5:10 Try to find out what is pleasing to the Lord.
Ephesians5:11 Take no part in the unfruitful works of darkness
Ephesians5:12 For it is shameful even to mention what such people do secretly;
Ephesians5:13 but everything exposed by the light becomes visible
Ephesians5:14 for everything that becomes visible is light. Therefore it says
Ephesians5:15 Be careful then how you live
Ephesians5:16 making the most of the time
Ephesians5:17 So do not be foolish
Ephesians5:18 Do not get drunk with wine
Ephesians5:19 as you sing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs among yourselves
Ephesians5:20 giving thanks to God the Father at all times and for everything in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Ephesians5:21 Be subject to one another out of reverence for Christ.
Ephesians5:22 Wives
Ephesians5:23 For the husband is the head of the wife just as Christ is the head of the church
Ephesians5:24 Just as the church is subject to Christ
Ephesians5:25 Husbands
Ephesians5:26 in order to make her holy by cleansing her with the washing of water by the word
Ephesians5:27 so as to present the church to himself in splendor
Ephesians5:28 In the same way
Ephesians5:29 For no one ever hates his own body
Ephesians5:30 because we are members of his body.
Ephesians5:31 For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife
Ephesians5:32 This is a great mystery
Ephesians5:33 Each of you
Ephesians6:1 Children
Ephesians6:2 Honor your father and mother-- this is the first commandment with a promise:
Ephesians6:3 so that it may be well with you and you may live long on the earth.
Ephesians6:4 And
Ephesians6:5 Slaves
Ephesians6:6 not only while being watched
Ephesians6:7 Render service with enthusiasm
Ephesians6:8 knowing that whatever good we do
Ephesians6:9 And
Ephesians6:10 Finally
Ephesians6:11 Put on the whole armor of God
Ephesians6:12 For our struggle is not against enemies of blood and flesh
Ephesians6:13 Therefore take up the whole armor of God
Ephesians6:14 Stand therefore
Ephesians6:15 As shoes for your feet put on whatever will make you ready to proclaim the gospel of peace.
Ephesians6:16 With all of these
Ephesians6:17 Take the helmet of salvation
Ephesians6:18 Pray in the Spirit at all times in every prayer and supplication. To that end keep alert and always persevere in supplication for all the saints.
Ephesians6:19 Pray also for me
Ephesians6:20 for which I am an ambassador in chains. Pray that I may declare it boldly
Ephesians6:21 So that you also may know how I am and what I am doing
Ephesians6:22 I am sending him to you for this very purpose
Ephesians6:23 Peace be to the whole community
Ephesians6:24 Grace be with all who have an undying love for our Lord Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:1 Paul and Timothy
Philippians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:3 I thank my God every time I remember you
Philippians1:4 constantly praying with joy in every one of my prayers for all of you
Philippians1:5 because of your sharing in the gospel from the first day until now.
Philippians1:6 I am confident of this
Philippians1:7 It is right for me to think this way about all of you
Philippians1:8 For God is my witness
Philippians1:9 And this is my prayer
Philippians1:10 to help you to determine what is best
Philippians1:11 having produced the harvest of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ for the glory and praise of God.
Philippians1:12 I want you to know
Philippians1:13 so that it has become known throughout the whole imperial guard and to everyone else that my imprisonment is for Christ;
Philippians1:14 and most of the brothers and sisters
Philippians1:15 Some proclaim Christ from envy and rivalry
Philippians1:16 These proclaim Christ out of love
Philippians1:17 the others proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition
Philippians1:18 What does it matter? Just this
Philippians1:19 for I know that through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ this will turn out for my deliverance.
Philippians1:20 It is my eager expectation and hope that I will not be put to shame in any way
Philippians1:21 For to me
Philippians1:22 If I am to live in the flesh
Philippians1:23 I am hard pressed between the two: my desire is to depart and be with Christ
Philippians1:24 but to remain in the flesh is more necessary for you.
Philippians1:25 Since I am convinced of this
Philippians1:26 so that I may share abundantly in your boasting in Christ Jesus when I come to you again.
Philippians1:27 Only
Philippians1:28 and are in no way intimidated by your opponents. For them this is evidence of their destruction
Philippians1:29 For he has graciously granted you the privilege not only of believing in Christ
Philippians1:30 since you are having the same struggle that you saw I had and now hear that I still have.
Philippians2:1 If then there is any encouragement in Christ
Philippians2:2 make my joy complete: be of the same mind
Philippians2:3 Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit
Philippians2:4 Let each of you look not to your own interests
Philippians2:5 Let the same mind be in you that was in Christ Jesus
Philippians2:6 who
Philippians2:7 but emptied himself
Philippians2:8 he humbled himself and became obedient to the point of death-- even death on a cross.
Philippians2:9 Therefore God also highly exalted him and gave him the name that is above every name
Philippians2:10 so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bend
Philippians2:11 and every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord
Philippians2:12 Therefore
Philippians2:13 for it is God who is at work in you
Philippians2:14 Do all things without murmuring and arguing
Philippians2:15 so that you may be blameless and innocent
Philippians2:16 It is by your holding fast to the word of life that I can boast on the day of Christ that I did not run in vain or labor in vain.
Philippians2:17 But even if I am being poured out as a libation over the sacrifice and the offering of your faith
Philippians2:18 and in the same way you also must be glad and rejoice with me.
Philippians2:19 I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon
Philippians2:20 I have no one like him who will be genuinely concerned for your welfare.
Philippians2:21 All of them are seeking their own interests
Philippians2:22 But Timothy's worth you know
Philippians2:23 I hope therefore to send him as soon as I see how things go with me;
Philippians2:24 and I trust in the Lord that I will also come soon.
Philippians2:25 Still
Philippians2:26 for he has been longing for all of you
Philippians2:27 He was indeed so ill that he nearly died. But God had mercy on him
Philippians2:28 I am the more eager to send him
Philippians2:29 Welcome him then in the Lord with all joy
Philippians2:30 because he came close to death for the work of Christ
Philippians3:1 Finally
Philippians3:2 Beware of the dogs
Philippians3:3 For it is we who are the circumcision
Philippians3:4 even though I
Philippians3:5 circumcised on the eighth day
Philippians3:6 as to zeal
Philippians3:7 Yet whatever gains I had
Philippians3:8 More than that
Philippians3:9 and be found in him
Philippians3:10 I want to know Christ and the power of his resurrection and the sharing of his sufferings by becoming like him in his death
Philippians3:11 if somehow I may attain the resurrection from the dead.
Philippians3:12 Not that I have already obtained this or have already reached the goal; but I press on to make it my own
Philippians3:13 Beloved
Philippians3:14 I press on toward the goal for the prize of the heavenly call of God in Christ Jesus.
Philippians3:15 Let those of us then who are mature be of the same mind; and if you think differently about anything
Philippians3:16 Only let us hold fast to what we have attained.
Philippians3:17 Brothers and sisters
Philippians3:18 For many live as enemies of the cross of Christ; I have often told you of them
Philippians3:19 Their end is destruction; their god is the belly; and their glory is in their shame; their minds are set on earthly things.
Philippians3:20 But our citizenship is in heaven
Philippians3:21 He will transform the body of our humiliation that it may be conformed to the body of his glory
Philippians4:1 Therefore
Philippians4:2 I urge Euodia and I urge Syntyche to be of the same mind in the Lord.
Philippians4:3 Yes
Philippians4:4 Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say
Philippians4:5 Let your gentleness be known to everyone. The Lord is near.
Philippians4:6 Do not worry about anything
Philippians4:7 And the peace of God
Philippians4:8 Finally
Philippians4:9 Keep on doing the things that you have learned and received and heard and seen in me
Philippians4:10 I rejoice in the Lord greatly that now at last you have revived your concern for me; indeed
Philippians4:11 Not that I am referring to being in need; for I have learned to be content with whatever I have.
Philippians4:12 I know what it is to have little
Philippians4:13 I can do all things through him who strengthens me.
Philippians4:14 In any case
Philippians4:15 You Philippians indeed know that in the early days of the gospel
Philippians4:16 For even when I was in Thessalonica
Philippians4:17 Not that I seek the gift
Philippians4:18 I have been paid in full and have more than enough; I am fully satisfied
Philippians4:19 And my God will fully satisfy every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
Philippians4:20 To our God and Father be glory forever and ever. Amen.
Philippians4:21 Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The friends who are with me greet you.
Philippians4:22 All the saints greet you
Philippians4:23 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
Colossians1:1 Paul
Colossians1:2 To the saints and faithful brothers and sisters in Christ in Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.
Colossians1:3 In our prayers for you we always thank God
Colossians1:4 for we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love that you have for all the saints
Colossians1:5 because of the hope laid up for you in heaven. You have heard of this hope before in the word of the truth
Colossians1:6 that has come to you. Just as it is bearing fruit and growing in the whole world
Colossians1:7 This you learned from Epaphras
Colossians1:8 and he has made known to us your love in the Spirit.
Colossians1:9 For this reason
Colossians1:10 so that you may lead lives worthy of the Lord
Colossians1:11 May you be made strong with all the strength that comes from his glorious power
Colossians1:12 giving thanks to the Father
Colossians1:13 He has rescued us from the power of darkness and transferred us into the kingdom of his beloved Son
Colossians1:14 in whom we have redemption
Colossians1:15 He is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 for in him all things in heaven and on earth were created
Colossians1:17 He himself is before all things
Colossians1:18 He is the head of the body
Colossians1:19 For in him all the fullness of God was pleased to dwell
Colossians1:20 and through him God was pleased to reconcile to himself all things
Colossians1:21 And you who were once estranged and hostile in mind
Colossians1:22 he has now reconciled in his fleshly body through death
Colossians1:23 provided that you continue securely established and steadfast in the faith
Colossians1:24 I am now rejoicing in my sufferings for your sake
Colossians1:25 I became its servant according to God's commission that was given to me for you
Colossians1:26 the mystery that has been hidden throughout the ages and generations but has now been revealed to his saints.
Colossians1:27 To them God chose to make known how great among the Gentiles are the riches of the glory of this mystery
Colossians1:28 It is he whom we proclaim
Colossians1:29 For this I toil and struggle with all the energy that he powerfully inspires within me.
Colossians2:1 For I want you to know how much I am struggling for you
Colossians2:2 I want their hearts to be encouraged and united in love
Colossians2:3 in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
Colossians2:4 I am saying this so that no one may deceive you with plausible arguments.
Colossians2:5 For though I am absent in body
Colossians2:6 As you therefore have received Christ Jesus the Lord
Colossians2:7 rooted and built up in him and established in the faith
Colossians2:8 See to it that no one takes you captive through philosophy and empty deceit
Colossians2:9 For in him the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily
Colossians2:10 and you have come to fullness in him
Colossians2:11 In him also you were circumcised with a spiritual circumcision
Colossians2:12 when you were buried with him in baptism
Colossians2:13 And when you were dead in trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh
Colossians2:14 erasing the record that stood against us with its legal demands. He set this aside
Colossians2:15 He disarmed the rulers and authorities and made a public example of them
Colossians2:16 Therefore do not let anyone condemn you in matters of food and drink or of observing festivals
Colossians2:17 These are only a shadow of what is to come
Colossians2:18 Do not let anyone disqualify you
Colossians2:19 and not holding fast to the head
Colossians2:20 If with Christ you died to the elemental spirits of the universe
Colossians2:21 Do not handle
Colossians2:22 All these regulations refer to things that perish with use; they are simply human commands and teachings.
Colossians2:23 These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting self-imposed piety

Colossians3:6 On account of these the wrath of God is coming on those who are disobedient.

Colossians3:8 But now you must get rid of all such things-- anger
Colossians3:9 Do not lie to one another
Colossians3:10 and have clothed yourselves with the new self

Colossians3:12 As God's chosen ones
Colossians3:13 Bear with one another and

Colossians3:25 For the wrongdoer will be paid back for whatever wrong has been done

1Thessalonians1:2 We always give thanks to God for all of you and mention you in our prayers
1Thessalonians1:3 remembering before our God and Father your work of faith and labor of love and steadfastness of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Thessalonians1:4 For we know
1Thessalonians1:5 because our message of the gospel came to you not in word only
1Thessalonians1:6 And you became imitators of us and of the Lord
1Thessalonians1:7 so that you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
1Thessalonians1:8 For the word of the Lord has sounded forth from you not only in Macedonia and Achaia
1Thessalonians1:9 For the people of those regions report about us what kind of welcome we had among you
1Thessalonians1:10 and to wait for his Son from heaven
1Thessalonians2:1 You yourselves know
1Thessalonians2:2 but though we had already suffered and been shamefully mistreated at Philippi
1Thessalonians2:3 For our appeal does not spring from deceit or impure motives or trickery
1Thessalonians2:4 but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the message of the gospel
1Thessalonians2:5 As you know and as God is our witness
1Thessalonians2:6 nor did we seek praise from mortals
1Thessalonians2:7 though we might have made demands as apostles of Christ. But we were gentle among you
1Thessalonians2:8 So deeply do we care for you that we are determined to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own selves
1Thessalonians2:9 You remember our labor and toil
1Thessalonians2:10 You are witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 As you know
1Thessalonians2:12 urging and encouraging you and pleading that you lead a life worthy of God
1Thessalonians2:13 We also constantly give thanks to God for this
1Thessalonians2:14 For you
1Thessalonians2:15 who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets
1Thessalonians2:16 by hindering us from speaking to the Gentiles so that they may be saved. Thus they have constantly been filling up the measure of their sins; but God's wrath has overtaken them at last.
1Thessalonians2:17 As for us
1Thessalonians2:18 For we wanted to come to you-- certainly I
1Thessalonians2:19 For what is our hope or joy or crown of boasting before our Lord Jesus at his coming? Is it not you?
1Thessalonians2:20 Yes

1Thessalonians3:2 and we sent Timothy
1Thessalonians3:3 so that no one would be shaken by these persecutions. Indeed
1Thessalonians3:4 In fact
1Thessalonians3:5 For this reason
1Thessalonians3:6 But Timothy has just now come to us from you
1Thessalonians3:7 For this reason
1Thessalonians3:8 For we now live
1Thessalonians3:9 How can we thank God enough for you in return for all the joy that we feel before our God because of you?
1Thessalonians3:10 Night and day we pray most earnestly that we may see you face to face and restore whatever is lacking in your faith.
1Thessalonians3:11 Now may our God and Father himself and our Lord Jesus direct our way to you.
1Thessalonians3:12 And may the Lord make you increase and abound in love for one another and for all
1Thessalonians3:13 And may he so strengthen your hearts in holiness that you may be blameless before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.
1Thessalonians4:1 Finally
1Thessalonians4:2 For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 For this is the will of God
1Thessalonians4:4 that each one of you know how to control your own body in holiness and honor
1Thessalonians4:5 not with lustful passion
1Thessalonians4:6 that no one wrong or exploit a brother or sister in this matter
1Thessalonians4:7 For God did not call us to impurity but in holiness.
1Thessalonians4:8 Therefore whoever rejects this rejects not human authority but God
1Thessalonians4:9 Now concerning love of the brothers and sisters
1Thessalonians4:10 and indeed you do love all the brothers and sisters throughout Macedonia. But we urge you
1Thessalonians4:11 to aspire to live quietly
1Thessalonians4:12 so that you may behave properly toward outsiders and be dependent on no one.
1Thessalonians4:13 But we do not want you to be uninformed
1Thessalonians4:14 For since we believe that Jesus died and rose again
1Thessalonians4:15 For this we declare to you by the word of the Lord
1Thessalonians4:16 For the Lord himself
1Thessalonians4:17 Then we who are alive
1Thessalonians4:18 Therefore encourage one another with these words.

1Thessalonians5:2 For you yourselves know very well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
1Thessalonians5:3 When they say
1Thessalonians5:4 But you
1Thessalonians5:5 for you are all children of light and children of the day; we are not of the night or of darkness.
1Thessalonians5:6 So then let us not fall asleep as others do
1Thessalonians5:7 for those who sleep sleep at night
1Thessalonians5:8 But since we belong to the day
1Thessalonians5:9 For God has destined us not for wrath but for obtaining salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ
1Thessalonians5:10 who died for us

1Thessalonians5:21 but test everything; hold fast to what is good;
1Thessalonians5:22 abstain from every form of evil.

2Thessalonians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians1:3 We must always give thanks to God for you
2Thessalonians1:4 Therefore we ourselves boast of you among the churches of God for your steadfastness and faith during all your persecutions and the afflictions that you are enduring.
2Thessalonians1:5 This is evidence of the righteous judgment of God
2Thessalonians1:6 For it is indeed just of God to repay with affliction those who afflict you
2Thessalonians1:7 and to give relief to the afflicted as well as to us
2Thessalonians1:8 in flaming fire
2Thessalonians1:9 These will suffer the punishment of eternal destruction
2Thessalonians1:10 when he comes to be glorified by his saints and to be marveled at on that day among all who have believed
2Thessalonians1:11 To this end we always pray for you
2Thessalonians1:12 so that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you
2Thessalonians2:1 As to the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to him
2Thessalonians2:2 not to be quickly shaken in mind or alarmed
2Thessalonians2:3 Let no one deceive you in any way; for that day will not come unless the rebellion comes first and the lawless one is revealed
2Thessalonians2:4 He opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god or object of worship
2Thessalonians2:5 Do you not remember that I told you these things when I was still with you?
2Thessalonians2:6 And you know what is now restraining him
2Thessalonians2:7 For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work
2Thessalonians2:8 And then the lawless one will be revealed
2Thessalonians2:9 The coming of the lawless one is apparent in the working of Satan
2Thessalonians2:10 and every kind of wicked deception for those who are perishing
2Thessalonians2:11 For this reason God sends them a powerful delusion
2Thessalonians2:12 so that all who have not believed the truth but took pleasure in unrighteousness will be condemned.
2Thessalonians2:13 But we must always give thanks to God for you
2Thessalonians2:14 For this purpose he called you through our proclamation of the good news
2Thessalonians2:15 So then
2Thessalonians2:16 Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father
2Thessalonians2:17 comfort your hearts and strengthen them in every good work and word.
2Thessalonians3:1 Finally
2Thessalonians3:2 and that we may be rescued from wicked and evil people; for not all have faith.
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful; he will strengthen you and guard you from the evil one.
2Thessalonians3:4 And we have confidence in the Lord concerning you
2Thessalonians3:5 May the Lord direct your hearts to the love of God and to the steadfastness of Christ.
2Thessalonians3:6 Now we command you
2Thessalonians3:7 For you yourselves know how you ought to imitate us; we were not idle when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:8 and we did not eat anyone's bread without paying for it; but with toil and labor we worked night and day
2Thessalonians3:9 This was not because we do not have that right
2Thessalonians3:10 For even when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 For we hear that some of you are living in idleness
2Thessalonians3:12 Now such persons we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ to do their work quietly and to earn their own living.
2Thessalonians3:13 Brothers and sisters
2Thessalonians3:14 Take note of those who do not obey what we say in this letter; have nothing to do with them
2Thessalonians3:15 Do not regard them as enemies
2Thessalonians3:16 Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with all of you.
2Thessalonians3:17 I
2Thessalonians3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
1Timothy1:1 Paul
1Timothy1:2 To Timothy
1Timothy1:3 I urge you
1Timothy1:4 and not to occupy themselves with myths and endless genealogies that promote speculations rather than the divine training that is known by faith.
1Timothy1:5 But the aim of such instruction is love that comes from a pure heart
1Timothy1:6 Some people have deviated from these and turned to meaningless talk
1Timothy1:7 desiring to be teachers of the law
1Timothy1:8 Now we know that the law is good
1Timothy1:9 This means understanding that the law is laid down not for the innocent but for the lawless and disobedient
1Timothy1:10 fornicators
1Timothy1:11 that conforms to the glorious gospel of the blessed God
1Timothy1:12 I am grateful to Christ Jesus our Lord
1Timothy1:13 even though I was formerly a blasphemer
1Timothy1:14 and the grace of our Lord overflowed for me with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy1:15 The saying is sure and worthy of full acceptance
1Timothy1:16 But for that very reason I received mercy
1Timothy1:17 To the King of the ages
1Timothy1:18 I am giving you these instructions
1Timothy1:19 having faith and a good conscience. By rejecting conscience
1Timothy1:20 among them are Hymenaeus and Alexander
1Timothy2:1 First of all
1Timothy2:2 for kings and all who are in high positions
1Timothy2:3 This is right and is acceptable in the sight of God our Savior
1Timothy2:4 who desires everyone to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God; there is also one mediator between God and humankind
1Timothy2:6 who gave himself a ransom for all-- this was attested at the right time.
1Timothy2:7 For this I was appointed a herald and an apostle (I am telling the truth
1Timothy2:8 I desire
1Timothy2:9 also that the women should dress themselves modestly and decently in suitable clothing
1Timothy2:10 but with good works
1Timothy2:11 Let a woman learn in silence with full submission.
1Timothy2:12 I permit no woman to teach or to have authority over a man; she is to keep silent.
1Timothy2:13 For Adam was formed first
1Timothy2:14 and Adam was not deceived
1Timothy2:15 Yet she will be saved through childbearing
1Timothy3:1 The saying is sure: whoever aspires to the office of bishop desires a noble task.
1Timothy3:2 Now a bishop must be above reproach
1Timothy3:3 not a drunkard
1Timothy3:4 He must manage his own household well
1Timothy3:5 for if someone does not know how to manage his own household
1Timothy3:6 He must not be a recent convert
1Timothy3:7 Moreover
1Timothy3:8 Deacons likewise must be serious
1Timothy3:9 they must hold fast to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
1Timothy3:10 And let them first be tested; then
1Timothy3:11 Women likewise must be serious
1Timothy3:12 Let deacons be married only once
1Timothy3:13 for those who serve well as deacons gain a good standing for themselves and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy3:14 I hope to come to you soon
1Timothy3:15 if I am delayed
1Timothy3:16 Without any doubt
1Timothy4:1 Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will renounce the faith by paying attention to deceitful spirits and teachings of demons
1Timothy4:2 through the hypocrisy of liars whose consciences are seared with a hot iron.
1Timothy4:3 They forbid marriage and demand abstinence from foods
1Timothy4:4 For everything created by God is good
1Timothy4:5 for it is sanctified by God's word and by prayer.
1Timothy4:6 If you put these instructions before the brothers and sisters
1Timothy4:7 Have nothing to do with profane myths and old wives' tales. Train yourself in godliness
1Timothy4:8 for
1Timothy4:9 The saying is sure and worthy of full acceptance.
1Timothy4:10 For to this end we toil and struggle
1Timothy4:11 These are the things you must insist on and teach.

1Timothy4:13 Until I arrive
1Timothy4:14 Do not neglect the gift that is in you
1Timothy4:15 Put these things into practice
1Timothy4:16 Pay close attention to yourself and to your teaching; continue in these things
1Timothy5:1 Do not speak harshly to an older man
1Timothy5:2 to older women as mothers
1Timothy5:3 Honor widows who are really widows.
1Timothy5:4 If a widow has children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 The real widow
1Timothy5:6 but the widow who lives for pleasure is dead even while she lives.
1Timothy5:7 Give these commands as well
1Timothy5:8 And whoever does not provide for relatives
1Timothy5:9 Let a widow be put on the list if she is not less than sixty years old and has been married only once;
1Timothy5:10 she must be well attested for her good works
1Timothy5:11 But refuse to put younger widows on the list; for when their sensual desires alienate them from Christ
1Timothy5:12 and so they incur condemnation for having violated their first pledge.
1Timothy5:13 Besides that
1Timothy5:14 So I would have younger widows marry
1Timothy5:15 For some have already turned away to follow Satan.
1Timothy5:16 If any believing woman has relatives who are really widows
1Timothy5:17 Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor
1Timothy5:18 for the scripture says
1Timothy5:19 Never accept any accusation against an elder except on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
1Timothy5:20 As for those who persist in sin
1Timothy5:21 In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the elect angels
1Timothy5:22 Do not ordain anyone hastily
1Timothy5:23 No longer drink only water
1Timothy5:24 The sins of some people are conspicuous and precede them to judgment
1Timothy5:25 So also good works are conspicuous; and even when they are not
1Timothy6:1 Let all who are under the yoke of slavery regard their masters as worthy of all honor
1Timothy6:2 Those who have believing masters must not be disrespectful to them on the ground that they are members of the church; rather they must serve them all the more
1Timothy6:3 Whoever teaches otherwise and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching that is in accordance with godliness
1Timothy6:4 is conceited
1Timothy6:5 and wrangling among those who are depraved in mind and bereft of the truth
1Timothy6:6 Of course
1Timothy6:7 for we brought nothing into the world
1Timothy6:8 but if we have food and clothing
1Timothy6:9 But those who want to be rich fall into temptation and are trapped by many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil
1Timothy6:11 But as for you
1Timothy6:12 Fight the good fight of the faith; take hold of the eternal life
1Timothy6:13 In the presence of God
1Timothy6:14 to keep the commandment without spot or blame until the manifestation of our Lord Jesus Christ
1Timothy6:15 which he will bring about at the right time-- he who is the blessed and only Sovereign
1Timothy6:16 It is he alone who has immortality and dwells in unapproachable light
1Timothy6:17 As for those who in the present age are rich
1Timothy6:18 They are to do good
1Timothy6:19 thus storing up for themselves the treasure of a good foundation for the future
1Timothy6:20 Timothy
1Timothy6:21 by professing it some have missed the mark as regards the faith. Grace be with you.

2Timothy1:2 To Timothy

2Timothy1:4 Recalling your tears

2Timothy1:6 For this reason I remind you to rekindle the gift of God that is within you through the laying on of my hands;
2Timothy1:7 for God did not give us a spirit of cowardice
2Timothy1:8 Do not be ashamed

2Timothy1:18 -- may the Lord grant that he will find mercy from the Lord on that day! And you know very well how much service he rendered in Ephesus.
2Timothy2:1 You then

2Timothy2:19 But God's firm foundation stands
2Timothy2:20 In a large house there are utensils not only of gold and silver but also of wood and clay
2Timothy2:21 All who cleanse themselves of the things I have mentioned will become special utensils
2Timothy2:22 Shun youthful passions and pursue righteousness
2Timothy2:23 Have nothing to do with stupid and senseless controversies; you know that they breed quarrels.

2Timothy2:25 correcting opponents with gentleness. God may perhaps grant that they will repent and come to know the truth
2Timothy2:26 and that they may escape from the snare of the devil

Hebrews9:1 Now even the first covenant had regulations for worship and an earthly sanctuary.
Hebrews9:2 For a tent was constructed
Hebrews9:3 Behind the second curtain was a tent called the Holy of Holies.
Hebrews9:4 In it stood the golden altar of incense and the ark of the covenant overlaid on all sides with gold
Hebrews9:5 above it were the cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat. Of these things we cannot speak now in detail.
Hebrews9:6 Such preparations having been made
Hebrews9:7 but only the high priest goes into the second
Hebrews9:8 By this the Holy Spirit indicates that the way into the sanctuary has not yet been disclosed as long as the first tent is still standing.
Hebrews9:9 This is a symbol of the present time
Hebrews9:10 but deal only with food and drink and various baptisms
Hebrews9:11 But when Christ came as a high priest of the good things that have come
Hebrews9:12 he entered once for all into the Holy Place
Hebrews9:13 For if the blood of goats and bulls
Hebrews9:14 how much more will the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 For this reason he is the mediator of a new covenant
Hebrews9:16 Where a will is involved
Hebrews9:17 For a will takes effect only at death
Hebrews9:18 Hence not even the first covenant was inaugurated without blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when every commandment had been told to all the people by Moses in accordance with the law
Hebrews9:20 saying
Hebrews9:21 And in the same way he sprinkled with the blood both the tent and all the vessels used in worship.
Hebrews9:22 Indeed
Hebrews9:23 Thus it was necessary for the sketches of the heavenly things to be purified with these rites
Hebrews9:24 For Christ did not enter a sanctuary made by human hands
Hebrews9:25 Nor was it to offer himself again and again
Hebrews9:26 for then he would have had to suffer again and again since the foundation of the world. But as it is
Hebrews9:27 And just as it is appointed for mortals to die once
Hebrews9:28 so Christ

Hebrews11:1 Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for
Hebrews11:2 Indeed
Hebrews11:3 By faith we understand that the worlds were prepared by the word of God
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered to God a more acceptable sacrifice than Cain's. Through this he received approval as righteous
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was taken so that he did not experience death; and he was not found
Hebrews11:6 And without faith it is impossible to please God
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to set out for a place that he was to receive as an inheritance; and he set out
Hebrews11:9 By faith he stayed for a time in the land he had been promised
Hebrews11:10 For he looked forward to the city that has foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith he received power of procreation
Hebrews11:12 Therefore from one person
Hebrews11:13 All of these died in faith without having received the promises

Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph

Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 choosing rather to share ill-treatment with the people of God than to enjoy the fleeting pleasures of sin.

Hebrews11:29 By faith the people passed through the Red Sea as if it were dry land
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell after they had been encircled for seven days.
Hebrews11:31 By faith Rahab the prostitute did not perish with those who were disobedient

James3:2 For all of us make many mistakes. Anyone who makes no mistakes in speaking is perfect

1John4:8 Whoever does not love does not know God
1John4:9 God's love was revealed among us in this way: God sent his only Son into the world so that we might live through him.
1John4:10 In this is love
1John4:11 Beloved
1John4:12 No one has ever seen God; if we love one another
1John4:13 By this we know that we abide in him and he in us
1John4:14 And we have seen and do testify that the Father has sent his Son as the Savior of the world.
1John4:15 God abides in those who confess that Jesus is the Son of God
1John4:16 So we have known and believe the love that God has for us. God is love
1John4:17 Love has been perfected among us in this: that we may have boldness on the day of judgment
1John4:18 There is no fear in love
1John4:19 We love because he first loved us.
1John4:20 Those who say
1John4:21 The commandment we have from him is this: those who love God must love their brothers and sisters also.
1John5:1 Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God
1John5:2 By this we know that we love the children of God
1John5:3 For the love of God is this
1John5:4 for whatever is born of God conquers the world. And this is the victory that conquers the world
1John5:5 Who is it that conquers the world but the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
1John5:6 This is the one who came by water and blood
1John5:7 There are three that testify:
1John5:8 the Spirit and the water and the blood
1John5:9 If we receive human testimony
1John5:10 Those who believe in the Son of God have the testimony in their hearts. Those who do not believe in God have made him a liar by not believing in the testimony that God has given concerning his Son.
1John5:11 And this is the testimony: God gave us eternal life
1John5:12 Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.
1John5:13 I write these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God
1John5:14 And this is the boldness we have in him
1John5:15 And if we know that he hears us in whatever we ask
1John5:16 If you see your brother or sister committing what is not a mortal sin
1John5:17 All wrongdoing is sin
1John5:18 We know that those who are born of God do not sin
1John5:19 We know that we are God's children
1John5:20 And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding so that we may know him who is true; and we are in him who is true
1John5:21 Little children
2John1:1 The elder to the elect lady and her children
2John1:2 because of the truth that abides in us and will be with us forever:
2John1:3 Grace
2John1:4 I was overjoyed to find some of your children walking in the truth
2John1:5 But now
2John1:6 And this is love
2John1:7 Many deceivers have gone out into the world
2John1:8 Be on your guard
2John1:9 Everyone who does not abide in the teaching of Christ
2John1:10 Do not receive into the house or welcome anyone who comes to you and does not bring this teaching;
2John1:11 for to welcome is to participate in the evil deeds of such a person.
2John1:12 Although I have much to write to you
2John1:13 The children of your elect sister send you their greetings.

3John1:2 Beloved

3John1:4 I have no greater joy than this
3John1:5 Beloved
3John1:6 they have testified to your love before the church. You will do well to send them on in a manner worthy of God;
3John1:7 for they began their journey for the sake of Christ
3John1:8 Therefore we ought to support such people

3John1:10 So if I come
3John1:11 Beloved
3John1:12 Everyone has testified favorably about Demetrius
3John1:13 I have much to write to you
3John1:14 instead I hope to see you soon
3John1:15 Peace to you. The friends send you their greetings. Greet the friends there

Revelation20:2 He seized the dragon
Revelation20:3 and threw him into the pit
Revelation20:4 Then I saw thrones
Revelation20:5 (The rest of the dead did not come to life until the thousand years were ended.) This is the first resurrection.
Revelation20:6 Blessed and holy are those who share in the first resurrection. Over these the second death has no power
Revelation20:7 When the thousand years are ended
Revelation20:8 and will come out to deceive the nations at the four corners of the earth

Revelation21:21 And the twelve gates are twelve pearls
Revelation21:22 I saw no temple in the city
Revelation21:23 And the city has no need of sun or moon to shine on it
